  • 2 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1.25 litre Vegetable or Chicken Stock
  • 4 boiled eggs, sliced in half
method:
Method:

  1. Add Chang’s Sesame Oil, garlic, ginger and green onions into a deep and wide pot and over a low heat, sauté for 2 minutes. Then add hot sauce, Chang’s Soy Sauce and the diced tomatoes, stock and combine well. Simmer for a minute and then take off stove.
  2. Using a hand blender (or food processor) process until smooth and ingredients have blended well.
  3. Place back into the pot and onto the stove and bring to boil. Add Chang’s Long Life Noodles and cook for 10 minutes. Using tongs, assist to break open the noodles once they have been boiling for 3 minutes. If more liquid is needed, add ½ cup of boiling water.
  4. Evenly distribute between 4 bowls and top with boiled eggs, slices of chilli, coriander leaves, spring onion and a good drizzle of lime juice.

title: Salmon with Oyster Sauce Glaze
author: pwg
metadescription: We think baked salmon is one of the more underrated dishes a family can enjoy. Nutritious, flavourful and certainly easy to make, our Salmon with Spicy Glaze is sure to enliven any gathering!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 Side of Salmon fillet
  • 2 tbs olive oil
  • 2 garlic cloves, minced
  • 1 tsp ginger, grated
  • 2 tbs Chang's Original Oyster Sauce
  • 1 tbs hot sauce
  • Sea salt and pepper

Peas:

  • 2 cups frozen peas, thawed
  • 1 tsp sesame oil
  • 1 tbs coconut oil
  • 1 tbs toasted sesame seeds
method:
Method:

  1. Dry salmon fillet using paper towels and season with sea salt and pepper. Place onto a baking tray lined with baking paper.
  2. In a bowl combine the oil, garlic, ginger, Chang’s Oyster Sauce and hot sauce.
  3. Pour over Salmon and evenly coat. Cover with plastic wrap and allow to marinade in fridge for 20 minutes.
  4. Preheat oven 200C fan forced. Place Salmon into oven and cook for 25 minutes.
  5. In the meantime, prepare the Peas. Place in a pot the coconut oil and peas. Season lightly and cook stirring occasionally until lightly toasted. Add the sesame seeds and cook for a further few minutes. Take off heat and drizzle Chang’s Sesame Oil over the top.
  6. Remove Salmon from oven and top with prepared peas. Garnish with sprouts and a good drizzle of lime juice.

title: Noodle Omelette
author: pwg
metadescription: Ok, so omelette, and you’re thinking breakfast. However, think again! Our Noodle Omelette is so simple to make and tasty to eat that any hour is Noodle Omelette hour!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 125g Chang's Long Life Noodles (½ pkt) OR 2 cups leftover cooked noodles
  • 4 eggs, whisked
  • 6 white mushrooms, sliced
  • 1 small brown onion, chopped
  • 3 tsp olive oil
  • ¾ cup grated tasty cheese
  • 1 tsp sweet chilli sauce + extra to drizzle
  • Sea salt and pepper
method:
Method:

  1. Place Chang’s Long Life Noodles in pot of boiling water and cook for 3 minutes. Drain and set aside. Alternatively use leftover noodles that are already cooked.
  2. Place in a frying pan half the oil, half the onion and half the mushrooms. Sauté for 2 minutes.
  3. Add half the noodles, sweet chilli sauce, season and combine well, then add half the cheese and half of the whisked eggs. Place lid on pan and allow to cook for 3-4 minutes. Once surface is thickened, fold omelette in half and using the back of the spatula press down. Cook for a further minute.
  4. Slide omelette onto plate and repeat with other half of ingredients.
  5. Garnish with fresh salad of choice such as sprouts, baby tomatoes, coriander leaves, chilli slices and an added drizzle of sweet chilli sauce.

title: Noodle Fritters
author: pwg
metadescription: What could be a more appealing start to the day than our Asian Style Noodle Fritters? Crispy and flavourful these super simple fritters are quickly on the table and really can include whatever veggies you have in the fridge. Enjoy!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • ½ pkt {include file='cms_template:Changs-Vermicelli-Rice-Noodles'}, boiled and drained OR 2 cups leftover cooked noodles
  • 185g can tuna, drained and flaked
  • 4 eggs, lightly beaten
  • 1 cup grated tasty cheese
  • 1 carrot, grated
  • 1/2 cup plain flour or gluten-free plain flour
  • ½ cup corn kernels
  • ½ cup spring onions, sliced
  • ½ cup Thai mint or mint, chopped
  • 1 tbs chilli sauce
  • Olive oil as needed

Dipping Sauce:

  • ½ cup Greek Yogurt
  • 1 tbs chilli sauce
  • 1 tbs fresh lime juice
method:
Method:

  1. Using kitchen scissors, roughly cut cooked noodles into 4 cm lengths.
  2. In a bowl combine all ingredients and season with sea salt and pepper. Combine well.
  3. Coat bottom of frying pan with olive oil and add 3 heaped spoonsful of mixture into pan. Using the back of the spoon flatten the top. Cook for 2 minutes and then turn over and cook a further 2 minutes.
  4. Remove from pan and repeat process with remaining mixture. Add extra olive oil if needed.
  5. Serve warm or at room temperature with a cucumber and mint salad.

title: Potato Salad with Chang's Fried Noodles
author: pwg
metadescription: This isn’t a salad for rabbits. Full of taste, bite and sure to satisfy the hungry! Potatoes, bacon, eggs, chilli. Really, need I say more?
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1kg baby red potatoes, washed, diced 2cm pieces
  • 3 slices bacon, sliced and pan fried
  • 3 boiled eggs, cut into quarters
  • 2 green onions, sliced
  • Handful of {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Gluten-Free-Fried-Noodles'} for a gluten-free option
  • Sea salt and pepper

Dressing:

  • 2 tbs chilli sauce
  • 1/3 cup mayonnaise
  • 1/3 cup sour cream
  • 2 tbs {include file='cms_template:Crispy-Noodle-Salad-Dressing'}
method:
Method:

  1. Add potatoes in a large pot filled with water. Bring to boil and cook (approx. 15 minutes) until you can pierce it in the centre with a toothpick or knife. Drain and allow to cool. Season with sea salt and pepper.
  2. Prepare the bacon, eggs and green onions.
  3. Whisk together the Salad Dressing.
  4. Pour dressing over potatoes and combine until evenly coated. Spread over serving bowl.
  5. Top with pan fried bacon, boiled eggs, green onions and Chang’s Fried Noodles.
  6. Serve at room temperature. Refrigerate leftovers.

title: Chang's Hot Dogs
author: pwg
metadescription: Anyone can make a Hot dog, but it’s Chang's Original Hot Chilli Sauce that really puts some bark in this dog! Brioche buns, radish, sprouts. Chilli mayo and lime juice. This is a dog of beauty and surprisingly refined taste.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 6 hot dog bread buns, sliced in half
  • 6 hot dog sausages, prepared according to the packet
  • 1 carrot, grated
  • 3 radishes, thinly sliced
  • Handful of sprouts
  • Handful of {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Gluten-Free-Fried-Noodles'} for a gluten-free option

Hot Chilli Mayo:

  • 1/3 cup mayonnaise
  • 3 tbs sweet chilli sauce
  • 1 tbs lime juice

Optional to garnish:

  • Thai Mint
  • Spring Onions

Note: we call these Chang's Hot Dogs as they are dairy free and use our fried noodles instead of cheese for those with dairy sensitivities.
method:
Method:

  1. Prepare hot dogs according to packet instructions.
  2. Prepare Hot Chilli Mayo.
  3. Open up each bread bun and spread the hot chilli mayo sauce over the inside of the bun.
  4. Evenly divide the carrot, radish, sprouts and hot dogs between the bed buns.
  5. Top with an extra drizzle of hot chilli sauce and a good sprinkle of Chang’s Fried Noodles. Serve and enjoy.

title: Asian Buffalo Chicken Wings
author: pwg
metadescription: A popular classic, enjoyed all year round. Simple to make, plentiful and always appreciated. Add some Chang's Original Hot Chilli Sauce to your marinade and put some real power to the wing!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 10 chicken wings
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • 2 tsp garlic, ground
  • 1 tsp sea salt
  • 1 tsp black pepper

Buffalo Sauce:

  • 1 tbs butter, melted
  • 1 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce'}
  • 2 tbs chilli sauce
  • 2 tbs runny honey
method:
Method:

  1. Line tray with baking paper and preheat oven to 210C fan forced.
  2. Pat dry chicken with a paper towel.
  3. Add to a large bowl the baking powder, salt, pepper and garlic, and combine well.
  4. Toss the wings into the bowl and coat with the baking powder mixture.
  5. Place onto baking tray and cook for 40 minutes or until golden and cooked through. Remove from oven.
  6. Just before removing the chicken wings from the oven, melt butter and add Chang’s Sesame Oil and Chang's Tamari Soy Sauce, chilli sauce and runny honey. Bring to a simmer (not to a boil), remove from heat and then set aside until chicken is ready.
  7. Pour Buffalo Sauce over hot chicken wings and toss to evenly coat. Serve immediately.

title: Avocado Fries
author: pwg
metadescription: Go on, just say the recipe name out aloud. Avocado Fries with Hot Dipping Sauce. Isn’t that magnificent. Avocado, one of the more favoured fruits, the ever-popular fries and a chilli hot dipping sauce? Heaven in a mouthful.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 3 large avocados, pitted and peeled (Avocado should be firm, not hard or soft)
  • 1 cup breadcrumbs
  • 1 pkt {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Gluten-Free-Fried-Noodles'} for a gluten-fee option
  • 3 large eggs, whisked
  • ½ cup plain flour
  • ½ tsp sea salt
  • ½ tsp black pepper
  • Coconut oil or Olive oil Spray

Dipping Sauce:

  • 2 tbs chilli sauce
  • 2 tbs sour cream

To Garnish:

  • Coriander leaves and slices Spring onions.
method:
Method:

  1. Pre heat oven 200C fan forced and line a baking tray with baking paper.
  2. Slice each whole avocado into half and then each half into 4 slices and season with sea salt and pepper (giving you a total of 8 slices per avocado)
  3. Using a food processor, pulse Chang’s Fried Noodles and then in a bowl combine them with the bread crumbs.
  4. In a second bowl, whisk the eggs and in a third bowl add the plain flour.
  5. Lightly coat all the avocado pieces into the plain flour and set them aside on the tray. Then one by one dip each slice in the egg mixture and finally coat in Fried Noodle / Flour mixture.
  6. Place onto baking tray and lightly spray fries with extra virgin olive oil or coconut oil. Bake for 8 minutes each side.
  7. Serve with dipping sauce.

Optional:  garnish with coriander leaves and spring onions.

title: Nasi Goreng Bake
author: pwg
metadescription: Nasi Goreng is a classic Indonesian dish and our Nasi Goreng Bake adds a wonderful twist retaining the dark brown, caramelised rice but baking in the flavour.
ingredients:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups long-grain rice
  • 5 cups boiling water
  • 4 chicken thigh fillets
  • 8 green prawns, peeled, tails left intact
  • 4 eggs
  • 1 brown onion, diced
  • 4 garlic cloves, minced
  • 3 tbs cooking oil
  • 2 tbs chilli sauce
  • 2 tbs Chang's Kecap Manis Sweet Soy Sauce
  • 1 tbs Chang's Tamari Light Soy Sauce

To Garnish

  • 2 green onions, thinly sliced
  • 1 red chilli, thinly sliced
  • 1 cucumber, thinly sliced
  • 4 radishes, thinly sliced
  • Juice of 1 lime
  • Handful of Coriander or sprouts
method:
Method

  1. In a large and deep pan or wok add the cooking oil, chicken, onion and garlic. Fry until chicken is lightly golden. Pour over the chicken the chilli sauce, Chang’s Kecap Manis, sea salt, pepper and rice. Combine well. Pour the boiling water over the mixture and bring it to boil. Once it is boiling, reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
  2. Take off heat and transfer to a non-stick baking dish. Cover with foil and place in pre-heated oven at 170C fan-forced for 20 minutes. Cover well to keep moisture in.
  3. Add prawns to baking tray and spread out evenly.
  4. With a spoon create 4 holes in the rice and crack an egg in each hole. Cover once again with foil and bake a further 15 minutes (If needed pour half a cup of boiling water around the edges of the baking tray if the rice is dry).
  5. Remove from oven but do not take off the foil. Allow to sit for 10 minutes.
  6. Serve immediately and top with garnish of choice including: green onions, cucumber, radish, chilli and juice of lime. For a crunchy twist sprinkle with Chang’s fried noodles.

title: Portobello Mushroom Steaks in Hoisin Sauce
author: pwg
metadescription: We love this gourmet inspired dish made easy (and healthier!) right at home. It’s only 8 ingredients, packed with nutrients, and surprisingly filling & satisfying.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 6 Portobello Mushrooms, cleaned and cut into 1cm thick strips
  • 4 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1 green onion, finely sliced into thin strips
  • 1 tbs toasted sesame seeds
  • 2 tsp dried chilli flakes
  • Sea salt
  • Pepper
method:
Method:

  1. Begin by combining Chang’s Hoisin Sauce, Sesame Oil sea salt and pepper in a wide bowl. Add the mushrooms to the marinade and using a pastry brush coat all sides of the mushrooms. Allow to sit for 10 minutes only.
  2. In the meantime, using a sharp knife slice the green onion into thin strips and soak in cold water. This helps them curl.
  3. Place mushrooms on a pan grill or BBQ on high heat and cook each side until it begins to brown. Brush more marinade if needed.
  4. Serve over a bed of steamed rice along with some strips of green onion, a sprinkle of sesame seeds and chilli flakes and an extra drizzle of Chang’s Sesame Oil.

 

Serving Suggestions: Steamed Rice
Variation in Serving Suggestion: Serve the Mushrooms over Chinese Style Pancakes along with the green onion, sesame seeds, chilli flakes and finely sliced cucumber sticks.

title: Hoisin Pork and Pumpkin Bake
author: pwg
metadescription: In the immortal words of Homer Simpson, “Hmmm, Pork Belly”. Need we say anymore?
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 600g pork belly, diced 2cm thick
  • 800g pumpkin, peeled and diced 3cm thick
  • 10 cloves garlic, crushed
  • 1 green onion, thinly sliced
  • 400ml Chinese Shaoxing Wine OR 250ml sweet red wine
  • 2 tbs Chang's Hoisin Sauce
  • 1 tbs Chang's Soy Sauce Dark
  • 2 tsp cooking oil
  • Sea salt and Pepper
  • Fresh coriander
method:
Method:

  1. Preheat the oven 130C fan forced.
  2. Place pork in lined baking tray and pour over choice of wine, Chang's Hoisin Sauce, Chang's Soy Sauce Dark, cooking oil and crushed garlic. Combine well.
  3. Add pumpkin to tray. Season pork and pumpkin with sea salt and freshly ground pepper.
  4. Place in oven. Check after an hour and turn baking tray around to evenly brown.
  5. Sprinkle sliced green onions and coriander leaves. Serve immediately.

Optional to serve with steamed rice.

title: Crispy Noodle Salad with Quinoa in a Jar
author: pwg
metadescription: The goodness of quinoa and carrot with the convenience of a travel ready, jar sized serving and our famous Chang’s Crispy Noodle Salad. What could be better? Healthy, satisfying, easy to make and a delight to eat!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • ¼ green cabbage, finely shredded
  • ¼ purple cabbage, finely shredded
  • 6 green onions
  • 1 cup slivered almonds
  • 1 large carrot, grated
  • 1 cup quinoa
  • 1 bottle {include file='cms_template:Crispy-Noodle-Salad-Dressing'}
  • 1 packet from your choice of {include file='cms_template:Changs-Crunchy-Fried-Noodles'}, {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Gluten-Free-Fried-Noodles'}
method:
Method:

  1. Prepare quinoa according to packet instructions. (Suggested to cook 1 cup quinoa to 2.5 cups boiling water and simmer until all the water has been absorbed. Lightly season with sea salt). Allow to cool.
  2. Add to the jars the ingredients in the following order. Quinoa, purple cabbage, green cabbage, carrot, almonds, green onions and top off with Chang's Crunchy Fried Noodles (choice of Chang's Original Fried Noodles, Chang's Crunchy Fried Noodles or for a gluten free option Chang's Gluten Free Fried Noodles). Store sauce in separate jars that fit approximately 60ml.

title: Crispy Noodle Salad in a Jar
author: pwg
metadescription: Another spin on our famous Chang’s Crispy Noodle Salad, this time the travel version. With an interesting Asian flavour combined with the crunch of Fried noodles and nuts, this meal is ideal for a day at work or away!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • ¼ green cabbage, finely shredded
  • ¼ purple cabbage, finely shredded
  • 6 green onions
  • 1 cup slivered almonds
  • 1 bottle Chang's Crispy Noodle Salad Dressing
  • 1 packet Chang's Crunchy Fried Noodles (choice of Chang's Original Fried Noodles, Chang's Crunchy Fried Noodles or Chang's Gluten Free Fried Noodles)
method:
Method:

  1. Prepare jars with ingredients in the following order:
  2. Green cabbage, almonds, green onions and top off with Chang's Crunchy Fried Noodles (choice of Chang's Original Fried Noodles, Chang's Crunchy Fried Noodles or for a gluten free option Chang's Gluten Free Fried Noodles).
  3. Optional to either store sauce in a separate small jar that will fit approximately 50 ml or add in the bottom of the jar (if eating the same day only).

title: Chang’s Crispy Noodle Salad Abundance Bowl
author: pwg
metadescription: As beautiful as it looks, our Crispy Noodle Salad Abundance Bowl recipe is easy to make, satisfying and healthy! Perfect for a summer’s day, or any day really!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'}, {include file='cms_template:Gluten-Free-Fried-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Changs-Crunchy-Fried-Noodles'}
  • ½ - ¾ Chinese cabbage, thinly shredded
  • 4 green onions, thinly sliced
  • ½ - ¾ cup slivered almonds
  • ½ cup {include file='cms_template:Crispy-Noodle-Salad-Dressing'}
  • 1 large carrot, sliced into ribbons using a peeler
  • 1 red chilli, sliced thinly
  • 1 lime, sliced into 4 wedges
  • Handful of Thai mint leaves
method:
Method:

  1. Finely shred the cabbage and evenly divide between 4 bowls. Add the spring onions and almonds. Garnish with carrot ribbons, chilli and mint leaves.
  2. Divide packet of crispy noodles between the bowls and serve dressing on the side for each to add to their own salad.
  3. Serve each bowl with a wedge of lime to squeeze over salad for an extra zing!

title: Sweet and Salty Eton Mess
author: pwg
metadescription: Just look at it! Fantastic. You know you are going to love our Sweet and Salty Eton Mess dessert. Make it today to make today special.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 600ml thickened cream
  • 500g fresh strawberries, diced
  • 350g frozen blueberries
  • 200g mini meringues kisses, roughly crushed
  • ½ packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Gluten-Free-Fried-Noodles'} for a gluten-free option
  • Mint leaves to garnish (optional)
method:
Method:

  1. Prepare all your ingredients, set out your serving glasses and then whip the cream.
  2. Whip the cream until soft peaks form. Once whipped, gently fold in half of the crushed meringue kisses.
  3. Now to assemble. Firstly add the blueberries and strawberries, secondly, dollop some of the cream, thirdly add some crushed meringue kisses and finally top it off with a sprinkle of Chang’s Fried Noodles. Repeat the process and finish it with a garnish of mint leaves.

title: Marshmallows and Caramel Cups
author: pwg
metadescription: Sweet treats don’t come much simpler or sweeter than our Marshmallow and Caramel cups. Easy to make and easier to love!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 400g milk chocolate, melted
  • 3 pkt {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Gluten-Free-Fried-Noodles'} for a gluten-free option
  • ¾ cup hundreds and thousands
  • 200g Nestle Top ‘n’ Fill caramel filling
  • 16 muffin cases
  • 100g (1 pkt) Mini decoration marshmallows
method:
Method:

  1. Combine in a wide bowl the melted chocolate and noodles until well coated then pour over the hundreds and thousands and stir to evenly distribute.
  2. Place muffin cases into muffin tins and evenly divide noodle mixture. Press with a spoon the noodle mixture around the case edges, leaving an indent to pour the caramel once nest has set.
  3. Place into fridge for 30 minutes and allow nest to set.
  4. Remove from paper cases and add a heaped teaspoon of caramel into each nest and top with mini marshmallows.
  5. Serve immediately or place back into the fridge until needed.

title: Super Greens with Oyster Sauce
author: pwg
metadescription: Who doesn’t need super greens every now and then? Ok, more now, and less then! Add extra tang with a squeeze of fresh lime over each serving!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 pkt {include file='cms_template:Changs-Vermicelli-Rice-Noodles'}
  • 2 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 6 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tsp {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'} plus oil for frying
  • 2 tbs water
  • 4 garlic cloves, roughly chopped
  • 4 spring onions, sliced
  • 2 bunches broccolini florets, roughly chopped
  • 2 bunches baby bok choy, roughly chopped
  • 1 cup green cabbage, thinly sliced
  • 1 red or green chilli, thinly sliced
  • 1 lime, freshly squeezed
method:
Method:

  1. Prepare noodles by boiling in water for 3 minutes. Drain and set aside.
  2. In a bowl add the soy sauce, oyster sauce, water and teaspoon of Sesame oil. Combine well and set aside.
  3. Add to wok oil of choice, broccolini, bok choy and cabbage. Stir fry over high heat for 3-4 minutes. Now add the garlic, chilli and spring onions and further stir fry for 2-3 minutes. Remove all vegetables from wok.
  4. Add to wok the noodles and pour over the sauce. Stir fry for a minute and then add the vegetables. Toss to combine well.
  5. Serve in bowls and squeeze fresh lime juice over each serving.

title: Prawn Pad See Ew
author: pwg
metadescription: Flat noodles wind through thick sauce, crisp greens and garlic galore - it's no wonder Pad See Ew is a favourite Thai street food. Add in some prawns and you have one of the simplest, yet most tasty stir-fry’s around!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 pkt {include file='cms_template:Changs-Thai-Style-Rice-Noodles'}
  • 200g large prawns, peeled, tail intact
  • 1 bunch Chinese broccoli, stems sliced in half (chopped in large chunks)
  • 2 eggs
  • 3 garlic cloves
  • 4 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Soy-Sauce-Dark'}
  • 2 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce'}
  • 1 tbs rice vinegar
  • 2 tbs water
  • 1 tsp {include file='cms_template:Changs-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1 tbs Vegetable Oil
method:
Method:

  1. Prepare noodles according to packet instructions and set aside.
  2. Add to wok vegetable oil, garlic and prawns. Stir Fry until prawns turn from white to orange and then add broccoli and stir fry for a further minute. Remove all ingredients from wok and set aside.
  3. Add some sesame oil to wok along with the eggs and scramble until cooked. Remove from wok and set aside.
  4. Add to wok the boiled noodles and pour over them the soy sauce, oyster sauce, rice vinegar and water. Toss to evenly coat.
  5. Add all other ingredients to wok, toss to combine and serve immediately. Optional to garnish with 1 tsp sesame oil, chopped chives or slices of spring onion.

title: Orange Skewers with Noodle Salad
author: pwg
metadescription: Kids of all ages love eating off a stick, and these marinated chicken skewers are a hit. Simple to make, eat and clean up. A winner all round.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 kg chicken tenderloins
  • 1/3 cup olive oil
  • ½ cup orange marmalade
  • ¼ cup Sweet Chilli Sauce
  • 1 tablespoon Chang's Soy Sauce

Salad:

  • 1 pkt Chang's Original Fried Noodles or Chang's Gluten Free Fried Noodles
  • ½ green cabbage, shredded
  • 1/3 red cabbage, shredded
  • 1 red capsicum, cut into thin strips
  • 1 carrot, peeled, grated
  • 1 cup bean sprouts, trimmed
  • 2 green onions, thinly sliced
method:
Make Skewers:

  1. Soak bamboo skewers in water for 15 minutes. Drain.
  2. Thread chicken onto skewers. Place into a ceramic dish.
  3. Combine 1 tablespoon of oil, 1/3 cup of marmalade, 2 tablespoons of sweet chilli, and 1 tablespoon Soy Sauce. Pour over skewers. Turn to coat. Cover.
  4. Refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Make Salad:

  1. Combine all ingredients except noodles, in a bowl.
  2. Combine remaining oil, marmalade, sweet chilli sauce, and pepper and whisk to combine.

Method:

  1. Heat a frying pan or BBQ.
  2. Cook skewers for 4 minutes each side, or until golden and cooked through.
  3. Add noodles and dressing to salad. Toss.
  4. Serve salad topped with skewers.

title: Noodle Salad with Edamame Beans
author: pwg
metadescription: Making a great accompaniment to our Christmas Ham with Hoisin Sauce, Orange and Ginger Glaze this Edamame enriched noodle salad can stand in its own and levels up when entertaining!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 pkt {include file='cms_template:Changs-Long-Life-Noodles'}
  • 200g Edamame beans, frozen
  • 3 green onions, sliced
  • 1 large carrot, shredded
  • 1 red chilli, sliced
  • ¼ iceberg lettuce, shredded
  • ¼ cup {include file='cms_template:Crispy-Noodle-Salad-Dressing'}
  • 1 tbs toasted sesame seeds
  • 1 pkt {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'}
  • 2 tsp {include file='cms_template:Changs-Sesame-Oil'}
  • Coriander
method:
Method:

  1. Prepare Chang’s Long Life Noodles according to packet instructions. Set aside to cool down. Boil the edamame beans for 10 minutes, drain and set aside to cool down.
  2. In a bowl combine Chang’s Salad Dressing and Sesame Oil. Whisk until well combined and set aside.
  3. Add noodles to a large mixing bowl and pour over the dressing. Combine until evenly coated.
  4. Assemble salad. Combine noodles, beans, lettuce, carrot, onions, chilli, sesame seeds and coriander. Sprinkle Chang’s Fried Noodles and toss again until well combined.
  5. Serve.

title: Aussie Summer Pork and Pineapple Skewers
author: pwg
metadescription: Pork and pineapple are one of those pairings that screams fun. Add in some capsicum, slide into a skewer and your taste buds have a guaranteed good time!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 400-500g pork neck, diced into 3cm chunks
  • 1 large red capsicum cut into 3cm cubes
  • 1 large green capsicum cut into 3cm cubes
  • 1-2 red onions cut into 3cm cubes
  • ½ a large pineapple, cut into 3cm cubes

Sauce:

  • 100ml pineapple juice
  • 2 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Soy-Sauce-Dark'}
  • 2 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce'}
  • 1 tsp {include file='cms_template:Changs-Sesame-Oil'}
method:
Method:

  1. In a bowl combine the soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil and pineapple juice and marinate the pork for 30min in the fridge. In the meantime, prepare the vegetables and pineapple.
  2. Thread pork, vegetables and pineapple onto skewers in a repeated pattern.
  3. Barbeque high on grill for 2min each side (a total of 8-10min cooking time)

Optional:

  • 1 pkt Chang's Rice Vermicelli Noodles
  • 1 tbs Chang's Soy Sauce
  • 1 tbs Chang's Sesame Oil
  • 3 tbs pineapple juice

Method:

  1. Boil noodles according to packet instructions and drain.
  2. Add to pan along with sesame oil and toss to coat and then add soy sauce and pineapple juice. Toss until coated and liquid has been absorbed. Serve alongside pork and pineapple skewers.

title: Witches Broomsticks
author: pwg
metadescription: Halloween treats just aren’t complete without these super cute and incredibly easy to make little Witches Broomsticks.  Hop aboard!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 packet Chang's Original Fried Noodles
  • 100g pretzel sticks
  • 100g white chocolate
  • writing icing tube
method:
Method:

  1. Arrange tray with baking paper. Place fried noodles in a bowl and melt chocolate in a microwave, stirring, at 1-minute intervals.
  2. Place some fried noodles onto baking paper in a small pile, then dip 1/3 of pretzel stick into melted chocolate and place onto a pile of noodles. Sprinkle some more noodles over pretzel part that is dipped in chocolate and using your fingers mould noodles into the shape of broom brush.
  3. Repeat with remainder pretzel sticks.
  4. Using writing icing tube create a rope that holds brush together. Allow to set for 10 minutes before serving.

See all of our Scary Sweet Treats recipes here.

title: Noodle Hair Monster Pops
author: pwg
metadescription: Who doesn’t want a Noodle Hair Monster Pop? With a gluten-free option, these delectable delights, both look and taste great.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 packet Chang's Original Fried Noodles (For Gluten Free option use Chang's Gluten Free Fried Noodles.)
  • 4 chocolate blocks broken in half (we used 70% cacao)
  • 8 lolly pop sticks
  • 16 sugar candy eyeballs
  • 200g chocolate buttons
  • 2x colour of choice writing icing tubes
method:
Method:

  1. Prepare a sheet of baking paper on a tray to place monster pops. Melt half of the chocolate drops in the microwave at 45sec intervals and stirring in between.
  2. Place the chocolate onto the baking paper. On the backside of the chocolate, place the lollypop stick halfway onto the chocolate block (the other half of the stick is hanging off). Pour melted chocolate over stick and chocolate block to secure in place and put into the freezer for 10 minutes. In the meantime, melt the remaining chocolate buttons.
  3. To create the noodle hair, dip the top end of the chocolate bar into he melted chocolate and then press into the bowl of fried noodles.
  4. Dip the back of the eyes into the melted chocolate and place onto the monster face and using the frosting gel tubes create the mouth and stitches. Place into the fridge until set. Enjoy.

See all of our Scary Sweet Treats recipes here.

title: Monster Party Bark
author: pwg
metadescription: A simple, scary, spin on an old favourite. Monster Party Bark may be the simplest recipe here. Melt chocolate cover it in personality, and eat!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 220g milk chocolate buttons, melted
  • 10-12 sugar candy eyeballs
  • 2 tbs coloured sprinkles
  • 100g chocolate bar of choice, broken into pieces (we used Rolo)
  • ½ packet Chang's Original Fried Noodles
method:
Method:

  1. Place melted chocolate onto baking paper and spread until chocolate it is at an even thickness.
  2. Spread evenly the sugar candy eyeballs, chocolate bar pieces, sprinkles and fried noodles onto the melted chocolate.
  3. Place into fridge to harden for 1 hour. Once hardened break up into pieces and serve.

See all of our Scary Sweet Treats recipes here.

title: Hairy Scary Banana Ghosts
author: pwg
metadescription: Almost healthy? Well, our Hairy Scary Banana Ghosts do contain bananas and lots of fun and fright potential, so yeah, we think that’s a healthy approach to Halloween!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 4-6 medium bananas
  • ½ packet Chang's Original Fried Noodles (For Gluten Free option use Chang's Gluten Free Fried Noodles.)
  • 150-200g white chocolate
  • 8-10 paper lolly pop sticks
  • writing icing tube
method:
Method:

  1. Peel bananas and cut in half. Push lolly pop stick into the middle of each piece.
  2. Melt chocolate in a microwave at 1-minute intervals, stirring in between.
  3. Dip bananas into chocolate and coat all the way around. Place onto baking paper. Sprinkle noodles at the top end of bananas to create its hair. Repeat with remainder bananas.
  4. Using writing icing tube draw eyes and mouth. Allow setting in freezer for 3 hours before serving.

See all of our Scary Sweet Treats recipes here.

title: Noodles with Mushroom Bolognese
author: pwg
metadescription: Noodles with a mushroom Bolognese. This dish, sounds, looks and tastes great! None of the fuss of traditional Bolognese, this easy to prepare dish is a winner.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Long-Life-Noodles'}
  • 2 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1 large carrot, diced
  • 1 celery stick, diced
  • 4 cloves garlic, minced
  • 3 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Soy-Sauce-Light'}
  • 400g can diced tomatoes
  • 400g brown mushrooms, diced
  • Thai basil leaves
  • Fresh red chilli
method:
Method:

  1. In a wok place sesame oil, carrot, celery and garlic. Sauté for 3 minutes.
  2. Add mushrooms and further sauté for 2 minutes.
  3. Add tomatoes, soy sauce, pepper and optional chilli slices. Simmer for 10 minutes over low heat.
  4. In the mean time boil noodles for 3 minutes and drain, reserving half a cup of noodles water.
  5. Add noodles to mushroom bolognese along with the noodles water. Toss to combine. Divide between 4 bowls and garnish with thai basil. Enjoy!

title: Noodle Stir Fry with Chinese Sausage
author: pwg
metadescription: A great dish for a packed lunch this one, as it can be eaten hot or cold. The meaty flavours of the Chinese sausage, combined with the bok choy, carrot and Long Life noodles make for a flavour sensation.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Long-Life-Noodles'}
  • 2 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 4 Chinese sausages cut into thin slices
  • 4 baby bok choy, leaves separated
  • 1 large carrot cut into matchsticks
  • 1 large onion sliced
  • 4 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce'} (for sweeter option use hoisin)
  • 2 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Soy-Sauce-Dark'}
  • 2 green onions sliced
  • Juice of 1 lemon
method:
Method:

  1. Soak noodles in boiling water until needed. Set aside.
  2. Heat oil in wok and add onion with Chinese sausage and sauté stirring continuously for 3 minutes.
  3. Add carrot and bok choy and continue to cook for 3 minutes.
  4. Drain noodles and add to wok. Pour over noodles soy sauce and oyster sauce and toss to combine well.
  5. Take off heat, garnish with green onions and lemon juice and serve. Can be eaten hot or cold.

title: Greens Noodle Soup
author: pwg
metadescription: What a beautiful dish! A healthy warming nutritious bowl of goodness. Oyster mushrooms, bok choy, Thai basil chilli and a squeeze of lime juice. Hmmm.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 packet Chang’s Long Life Noodles
  • 6 cups vegetable stock
  • 2 tbs Chang’s Oyster Sauce
  • 2 tbs Chang’s Soy Sauce
  • 4 baby bok choy, sliced in half
  • 8 oyster mushrooms, sliced in half
  • 8 brown mushrooms, sliced thick
  • 1 tbs Chang’s Sesame Oil
  • Thai Basil
  • Green Chilli
  • Fresh Lime
method:
Method:

  1. Boil noodles and drain. Divide between 4 bowls.
  2. In a pot add the sesame oil, bok choy and mushrooms. Sauté for 2 minutes stirring continuously and then add the vegetable stock, oyster sauce and soy sauce. Bring to boil and simmer for 2 minutes.
  3. Turn off heat and divide evenly the vegetables and broth between the bowls.
  4. Garnish with thai basil, green chilli slices and a squeeze of lime juice.

title: Easy Work Place Noodles
author: pwg
metadescription: Everyone has those days. The days when you need quick, simple, and fantastic! Add chilli to taste.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 2 Individual servings of {include file='cms_template:Changs-Long-Life-Noodles'}
  • 1 tablespoon {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 3 tablespoons {include file='cms_template:Crispy-Noodle-Salad-Dressing'}
  • 2 tablespoons Chinese fried onions
  • 1 teaspoon dried chilli flakes
method:
Method:

  1. Soak noodles for 8 minutes in a bowl filled with boiling water. Place a plate over the bowl to keep steam in.
  2. Drain noodles.
  3. Pour over Crispy Salad Noodles Dressing, Sesame Oil, fried onions and chilli flakes. Toss to combine. Enjoy!

title: Easy Chicken Noodle Soup
author: pwg
metadescription: Who doesn’t love Chicken Noodle Soup? And whop doesn’t love easy? Leave it to Chang’s and our Long Life Noodles to bring this favourite into your home, simply.  Make some today!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 6 cups Chicken stock, bought or homemade
  • 400g chicken thigh fillets, cut into strips
  • 1 packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Long-Life-Noodles'} or (2 Packets {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Noodles'}, as a gluten-free option)
  • 2 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Sesame-Oil'} plus extra
  • 1 tbs fresh ginger, grated
  • 1 large carrot, cut into matchsticks
  • 2 cups Chinese cabbage, shredded
  • 1/4 cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce'}
  • 2 tablespoons {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce'}
  • 2 tbs rice vinegar
  • 1 green onion, sliced
  • 1 red chilli, sliced
method:
Method:

  1. Cook noodles for 3 minutes in a pot of boiling water. Drain and divide between 4 soup bowls.
  2. In a wide base pot add the sesame oil and chicken. Sauté for 3 minutes. Add grated ginger and sauté for a further minute.
  3. Add chicken stock, soy sauce, oyster sauce and rice vinegar. Bring to boil and then simmer for 3 minutes.
  4. In the meantime divide the cabbage and carrot amongst the soup bowls.
  5. Turn off heat and using a ladle serve the soup into the bowls. Garnish with green onion and chilli and drizzle extra Sesame Oil over the top.
  6. Enjoy!

title: The Best 2 minute Noodle Meal Ever
author: pwg
metadescription: Big claim. The BEST 2-minute noodles, ever. Put this recipe to the test. An absolute favourite. Quick, easy and fantastic.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 pkt {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Wide-Noodles'}
  • 1 small clove garlic, minced
  • 1cm x 1cm fresh ginger finely chopped
  • 1 tsp {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}

Sauce:

  • 2 tsp {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tsp {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tsp maple syrup
  • ½ tsp rice vinegar

Garnish:

  • fresh green onion, finely sliced
  • fresh coriander leaves
  • chilli flakes
method:
Method:

  1. Rinse and drain noodles according to packet instructions. Combine the sauce ingredients in a bowl and whisk.
  2. Add to a pan the sesame oil, ginger and garlic and sauté for 1 minute.
  3. Add to the pan the noodles and sauce. Over high heat combine for 1 minute.
  4. Turn off heat. Pour into bowl and garnish with green onions, coriander and chilli flakes.

title: Rainbow Vegetarian Pad Thai with Crispy Noodles
author: pwg
metadescription: A Pad Thai dish that is nut free and school box friendly with Chang’s Original Fried Noodles used instead of peanuts for that special Pad Thai crunch!)
ingredients:
A Pad Thai dish that is nut free and school box friendly as Chang’s Original Fried Noodles are used instead of peanuts for that special Pad Thai crunch!

Ingredients:

Noodles:

  • 250g Chang’s Thai Style Rice Noodles
  • 1 green zucchini, spiralised or sliced into ribbons
  • 1 red capsicum, thinly sliced
  • 1 yellow capsicum, thinly sliced
  • 1 brown onion, thinly sliced
  • 1 carrot, cut into matchsticks
  • 1 tbs Chang’s Sesame oil

Sauce:

  • 3 tbs Chang's Soy Sauce
  • 1 tbs rice vinegar
  • 1 tbs maple syrup
  • 1 tsp fish sauce
  • 1 tsp sriracha chilli sauce

Garnish:

  • 3 green onions, sliced on the diagonal
  • 1 pkt Chang's Original Fried Noodles
  • Juice of 1 lime
method:
Method:

  1. Prepare noodles according to packet instructions. Do not over boil.
  2. Prepare sauce by adding all ingredients to a bowl and whisk together. Set aside.
  3. Add to wok sesame oil and onion and stir fry for 2 minutes then add the carrots and both capsicum to the wok and further stir fry for 2 minutes. Finally, add the zucchini and combine for only one minute. Remove from wok onto a plate and set aside.
  4. Add the prepared noodles to wok and pour the prepared sauce over the noodles. Stir fry for 1-2 minutes or until the noodles have absorbed the sauce.
  5. Once absorbed add the vegetables back into the wok. Combine with the noodles. Turn off heat and add the green onions, lime juice and fried noodles to garnish. Combine and serve immediately.

title: Salmon with Asian Guacamole Noodles
author: pwg
metadescription: A simple and sensational way to add more fish into your diet, and our Salmon with Asian Guacamole Noodles can be even served cold and is possibly better the next day. How good is that?
ingredients:
Ingredients:

Salmon:

  • 4 x 100 g Salmon Fillets
  • 3 tsp Chang's Soy Sauce
  • 1 tsp Chang's Sesame Oil
  • 1 tbs fresh lime juice
  • 2 cm piece of ginger, peeled & finely grated
  • 1 garlic clover, grated
  • 1 tbs toasted sesame seeds to garnish 

Noodles:

  • 250g pkt Chang's Rice Vermicelli Noodles
  • 1 tbs fresh lime juice
  • 2 tsp chilli flakes
  • 1 tsp nut oil
  • ½ avocado peeled, mashed well
  • ½ tsp Chang's Soy Sauce
  • ½ tsp sriracha sauce
  • ¼  tsp sea salt
  • ½ tsp pepper
method:
Method:

  1. Cook the noodles following the packet instructions. Drain and set aside to completely cool down.
  2. Prepare noodles dressing by combining avocado, oil, lime juice, soy sauce, sea salt, pepper and sriracha. Whisk or blend in food processor. Combine well with noodles, sprinkle chilli flake and place in the fridge to allow to go cold.
  3. Combine and whisk in a shallow bowl the soy sauce, sesame oil, lime juice, ginger and garlic to make the salmon marinade. Coat salmon fillets with marinade and place in the fridge for at least 15 minutes.
  4. Heat some sesame oil in a skillet or pan and add Salmon fillets, skin side down. Cook each side over high heat for 3-4 minutes. Take off heat and set aside. Sprinkle with Sesame seeds.
  5. Place noodles onto plates with a piece of fish per person and serve.
  6. Recommended to combine noodle and salmon with each bite for ultimate flavour combination. 

Optional: fresh coriander leaves

Note: This dish can also be served cold and is even better the next day.

title: Fried Noodle Salad with Mint, Chilli and Lime
author: pwg
metadescription: The combination of mint, chilli, lime and our famous Original Fried Noodles Salad make this salad special. Light, tangy, and sensational.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 packet Chang's Original Fried Noodles, or Chang's Gluten Free Fried Noodles
  • ½ Chinese cabbage
  • 6 green onions, sliced on the diagonal
  • ½ cup slivered almonds
  • 1 large carrot, cut into matchsticks
  • 1 red onion, thinly sliced
  • 1 long red chilli, thinly sliced
  • juice of 1 lime
  • handful on Thai mint
  • 2/3 cup Chang's Crispy Noodle Salad Dressing
method:
Method:

  1. Finely shred cabbage and place in large bowl and drizzle with Chang’s Fried Noodle Salad Dressing.
  2. Add green onion, almonds, carrot, red onion, mint, and chilli. Combined well.
  3. Squeeze lime juice over salad, then add Chang’s fried noodles and toss.
  4. Garnish with extra mint and chilli. Serve immediately.

title: Fried Noodle Salad with Tofu Skewers
author: pwg
metadescription: Our Original Fried Noodle salad, with tofu and a kick! Healthy, fantastic and fast.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

Fried Noodle Salad:

  • 1 packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'}
  • ½ Chinese cabbage
  • 6 green onions, sliced on the diagonal
  • ½ cup slivered almonds
  • ¼ cup {include file='cms_template:Crispy-Noodle-Salad'}

Tofu Skewers:

  • 350g extra firm tofu
  • ¼ cup {include file='cms_template:Crispy-Noodle-Salad'}
  • 1 tsp {include file='cms_template:Changs-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1 tbs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce'}
  • 1 garlic clove minced

To Garnish:

  • a handful of coriander leaves
  • juice of 1 lime
  • 1 red chilli, sliced
method:
Method:

  1. Cut the tofu block into 3cm cubes. Add to a wide bowl the soy sauce, fried noodle salad dressing, sesame oil and garlic and whisk. Add the tofu to the sauce and coat well. Marinate in the fridge while preparing the salad.
  2. Prepare Chang’s Original Fried Noodle salad by shredding cabbage and combining with green onion and slithered almonds. Set aside. Do not add sauce or noodles yet.
  3. Thread tofu onto skewers. Heat skillet/pan and brush with Chang’s Sesame oil before placing skewers. Cook each side for 2-3 minutes, brushing all sides with leftover marinade as they cook.
  4. Once tofu skewers are cooked pour Chang’s Fried Noodle salad dressing over salad, toss and then sprinkle Chang’s Original Fried Noodles over. Combined well.
  5. Serve salad and then place 1-2 skewers per person over each salad plate. To garnish, add coriander, slices of chilli and a good squeeze of lime juice. Serve immediately

title: Rainbow Fried Noodle Salad
author: pwg
metadescription: Another spin on our Original Fried Noodle salad, with an added tang of squeezed lime to top this colourful salad.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

Salad:

  • 1 packet Chang's Fried Noodles 
  • ½ Chinese cabbage, shredded
  • 6 green onions, sliced
  • ½ cup slivered almonds
  • 1 carrot cut into matchsticks
  • 1 red capsicum thinly sliced
  • 1 yellow capsicum thinly sliced 
  • ½ bunch coriander leaves

Dressing:

  • ¼ cup Chang's Fried Noodle salad dressing 
  • 1 tbs Chang's Soy-sauce
  •  Juice of 1 lime
method:
Method:

  1. Combined cabbage, almonds, onions, carrot, red and yellow capsicum in a large bowl.
  2. In a separate bowl combine and whisk well Chang’s Salad dressing and soy sauce.
  3. Drizzle salad with dressing. Toss and combine well, then add the packet of fried noodles.
  4. Squeeze lime juice over entire salad and serve immediately.

title: Fried Noodle Nest with Soy Beef Noodles
author: pwg
metadescription: An easy to prepare, soon to be family favourite! Stir-fry Bok Choy, Beef and noodles. Sensational.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 pkt Chang's Long Life Noodles
  • 400g Minute steak, cut into strips
  • 3 cloves garlic, minced
  • 3 tbs Chang's soy sauce
  • 3 tbs Chang's oyster sauce
  • 4 bok choy, sliced in half lengthways
  • 1 large carrot cut into julienne or shredded
  • 2 tsp Chang's sesame oil
  • 1 pkt Chang's Original Fried Noodles
  • 1 cup bean sprouts
  • Freshly ground pepper
method:
Method:

  1. Prepare Chang’s Long Life Noodles according to packet instructions by adding to boiling water to soften, then remove and drain. Set aside.
  2. Add Chang’s Oyster sauce and soy sauce to bowl and whisk together.
  3. Add 1 tsp sesame oil to wok and heat. Add garlic and minute steak and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes. Season with freshly ground pepper then add long life noodles to wok and pour over the prepared sauce. Combine well and stir-fry until sauce has coated all noodles evenly. Remove from wok and set aside.
  4. Add 1 tsp Chang’s Sesame oil to wok again and heat. Add Bok Choy and carrots and over high heat stir-fry for 1-2 minutes. Remove from heat.
  5. Assemble Fried Noodle Nest by evenly distributing Chang’s Original Fried Noodles into 4 bowls or plates. Then add the beef and noodles and top with Bok Choy, carrots and a few bean sprouts.

Optional: squeeze lime juice over noodles for zest.

title: Banana Split with Maple Syrup and Fried Noodles
author: pwg
metadescription: Whip up this super easy dessert by frying bananas till they are caramelised and top with ice-cream and caramel or maple sauce. An absolute winner.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 2 tbs Chang’s Original Fried Noodles
  • 2 small bananas, cut in half down the centre
  • 1 tbs maple syrup
  • 1 tsp coconut oil
  • ½ tsp ground cinnamon
  • Vanilla ice cream of choice
  • Drizzle of caramel sauce OR maple syrup
method:
Method:

  1. Add to a frying pan the maple syrup, coconut oil and cinnamon. Once melted add the bananas open side facing down first and over medium heat fry each side for 1-2 minutes or until golden.
  2. Prepare to scoop ice cream into bowls and drizzle some caramel sauce or maple syrup over ice cream.
  3. Place cinnamon bananas over ice cream and then sprinkle with 1 tbs of Chang’s Original Fried Noodles. Enjoy

title: Asian Peanut Chicken Noodle Bowl
author: pwg
metadescription: Abundance bowls are the perfect meal to use up whatever you have in your fridge or pantry. Don’t let the ingredient list fool you, this is super quick and easy recipe to prepare!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

Noodles

  • 2 packets {include file='cms_template:Wok-Ready-Noodles'} OR 1 packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Shelf-Fresh-Noodles-Hokkien-Style'}
  • ¼ cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce'}
  • 1 tablespoon cooking oil

Chicken

  • 400g chicken breast fillets, sliced into strips
  • 1 tablespoon cooking oil
  • 1 tablespoon {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1 tablespoon{include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce'}
  • 1 tablespoon Sriracha Sauce
  • 2 tablespoon water
  • 4 tablespoons Crunchy Peanut Butter

Salad

  • 100g rocket, washed
  • 1 large red capsicum, thinly sliced
  • ¼ purple cabbage, thinly sliced
  • A handful of fresh coriander
method:
Method:

  1. Prepare the Sauce: In a bowl or food processor, whisk together Chang’s Sesame Oil, Chang's Tamari Light Soy Sauce, Sriracha Sauce, water, and Crunchy Peanut Butter until smooth and combined.
  2. Marinate the Chicken: Coat chicken strips with half of the prepared sauce. Cover and refrigerate for 10 minutes to marinate. Reserve the remaining sauce for later.
  3. Cook the Noodles: Soak the Chang’s Gluten Free Wok Ready Noodles or Chang's Hokkien Style Shelf-Fresh Noodles in warm water until softened, then drain. Heat 1 tablespoon cooking oil in a wok or large pan over high heat. Stir-fry the noodles for 2 minutes until heated through. Add Chang’s Hoisin Sauce and cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring constantly. Remove from heat and set aside.
  4. Cook the Chicken: In the same wok or pan, add 1 tablespoon cooking oil and heat over medium-high heat. Add marinated chicken strips and cook for about 2 minutes on each side or until browned and cooked through. Remove from heat and sprinkle with sesame seeds for extra flavour and crunch.
  5. Assemble the Bowl: Place rocket in serving bowls. Top with a portion of hoisin-coated noodles, cooked chicken, sliced red capsicum, sliced purple cabbage, and fresh coriander. Serve with the reserved sauce on the side and offer extra soy sauce for individual preference if desired.

title: Spicy Fried Noodle Salad
author: pwg
metadescription: With a gluten-free option, this easy to prepare, super tasty, spicy, and crunchy recipe is also good for you! What’s not to love about our Spicy Fried Noodle Salad?
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 packet Chang's Original Fried Noodles, or Chang's Gluten Free Fried Noodles
  • ¼ Chinese cabbage, shredded
  • ¼ purple cabbage, shredded
  • 1 large carrot, sliced thinly
  • 4 green onions, sliced on the diagonal
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • 2 red chillies, finely sliced
  • 125g can of corn
  • Juice of 1 lime
  • ½ cup Chang's Crispy Noodle Salad Dressing
method:
Method:

  1. In a dish combine Chang’s Fried Noodle Salad Dressing and juice of 1 lime and whisk.
  2. Combine all salad ingredients (excluding Fried Noodles) in a deep bowl. Pour over the sauce and toss until well coated.
  3. Add Chang’s Fried Noodles to salad.  Serve immediately.

title: Easter Nest Surprise
author: pwg
metadescription: Make an Easter nest surprise. Step 1. Make an Easter treat with a chocolaty surprise and share with friends and family. Don’t forget to take a picture and tag Chang’s #cookingwithchangs
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 400g milk chocolate, melted
  • 3 pkt Chang's Original Fried Noodles
  • ¾ cup hundreds and thousands
  • 200g Nestle Top ‘n’ Fill caramel filling
  • 16 muffin cases
  • 48 mini candy-coated Easter eggs
method:
Method:

  1. Combine in a wide bowl the melted chocolate and noodles until well coated then pour over the hundreds and thousands and stir to evenly distribute.
  2. Place muffin cases into muffin tins and evenly divide noodle mixture. Press with a spoon the noodle mixture around the case edges, leaving an indent to pour the caramel once nest has set.
  3. Place into fridge for 30 minutes and allow nest to set.
  4. Remove from paper cases and add a heaped teaspoon of caramel into each nest and top with 3 easter eggs.
  5. Serve immediately or place back into the fridge until needed.

title: Changs Abundance Bowl
author: pwg
metadescription: Abundance bowls are the perfect meal to use up whatever you have in your fridge or pantry. Don’t let the ingredient list fool you, this is super quick and easy recipe to prepare!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

Salad:

  • 1 Lebanese cucumber, cut into matchsticks
  • 1 carrot, cut into matchsticks
  • ¼ red cabbage, sliced thinly
  • a handful of bean sprouts and coriander leaves

Note: It is easier and faster to use a mandoline slicer to slice the cabbage, carrot and cucumber if you have one available.

Chang's Fried Noodle Salad:

  • ¼ Chinese cabbage, sliced thinly
  • 2 green onions, sliced on the diagonal
  • 2 tbs slithered almonds
  • ½ pkt Chang's Crunchy Fried Noodles (choice of Chang's Original Fried Noodles, Chang's Crunchy Fried Noodles or Chang's Gluten Free Fried Noodles)
  • ¼ cup Chang's Crispy Noodle Salad Dressing

Combine all ingredients. Evenly coat salad with dressing.

Chang's Konjac 2 minute Noodles

  • 1 pkt Chang's Traditional Low Cal Noodles, drained and rinsed
    1 tbs Chang's Soy Sauce
    1 tbs toasted sesame seeds

Combine all ingredients in a pot and cook over high heat for 2 minutes.

Garnish:

  • 3-4 tbs Chang's Fried Noodle Salad Dressing served on the side for extra dressing options.

Optional to add a handful of baby rocket leaves at the base of abundance bowl for added greens option.
method:
Method:

  1. Assemble Abundance Bowl by adding all ingredients to the bowl.
  2. Begin with Chang’s Fried Noodle Salad, then red cabbage, cucumber, carrot, noodles, bean sprouts and coriander and top off with adding the extra dressing on the side and some extra fried noodles for added crunch!
  3. Enjoy.

title: Hoisin beef with shitake, noodles and spring onion dressing
author: pwg
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 400g beef rump, thinly sliced
  • 1/3 cup Chang’s Hoisin sauce
  • 2 tbsps peanut oil
  • 1 large brown onion, sliced
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed
  • 250g shitake mushrooms, sliced
  • 1 packet Changs Hokkien noodles
  • 1/4 cup gluten-free beef stock
  • 1/2 cup thinly sliced spring onions
  • 1/4 cup firmly packed coriander leaves, finely chopped
  • 1 red chilli, finely chopped
  • 1/4 cup Chang’s Light Soy Sauce
  • 2tbsps Chang’s Sesame oil
method:
Method:

  1. Place beef and Hoisin sauce in a large bowl, stir until well combined.
  2. Heat half the peanut oil in a large frying pan or wok over high heat; add beef, in batches, and stir-fry 3-4 minutes or until golden and cooked through.
  3. Transfer to a plate.
  4. Add remaining oil to the pan and stir-fry brown onion, garlic and mushrooms for 2-3 minutes or until onions are browned and softened.
  5. Return beef to pan with stock and noodles and stir-fry a further 2-3 minutes or until combined.
  6. Serve garnished with the combined spring onion, coriander, chilli, Soy and sesame oil.

Gluten Free Option: use Wok-Ready Noodles in place of Hokkien Noodles

title: Crunchy noodle ice cream sandwiches
author: pwg
metadescription: Our famous Original Fried Noodles and ice cream? What's not to love?
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1lt vanilla ice cream, slightly softened
  • 300g white marshmallows
  • 120g salted butter, chopped
  • 1/3 cup smooth peanut butter
  • 1 x 100g packet Changs Crunchy Fried Noodles
  • 1 packet Changs Fried Noodles
  • 1 cup Maltesers, roughly chopped
method:
Method:

  1. Line the base and sides of a 20 x 30cm slab pan with non-stick baking paper.
  2. Spread ice-cream evenly into tin. Smooth surface. Refreeze for 3-4 hours or until firm.
  3. Melt the marshmallows, butter and peanut butter in a bowl over gently simmering water, stirring occasionally until smooth.
  4. Remove pan from heat, add noodles and stir to coat evenly.
  5. Spoon into a lined 35 x 25cm baking tray with a slight lip.
  6. Press Maltesers into the top of the mixture then refrigerate for 20 minutes or until firm, cut into 8-12 bars.
  7. Sandwich ice cream between two bars and serve immediately.

For Gluten Free version use 3 packets Gluten Free Fried Noodles. Stir two packets into marshmallow mixture and spread into the tin. Sprinkle the remaining noodles over the top and press to stick. Follow as per recipe.

Note: use ice-cream squares if preferred.

title: Sesame noodles with Salmon and Asian herbs
author: pwg
metadescription: Made with Changs Eggs Noodles, which are versatile in a stir-fry, soups and even salads. Chang’s noodles carry the Heart Foundation Tick of approval for contributing to a balanced diet.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 200g packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Egg-Noodles'}
  • 3 tbsps {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 3tbsps rice wine vinegar
  • 4tbsps {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1tbsp finely grated ginger
  • 1 clove garlic, crushed
  • 2tbsps hulled tahini paste
  • 1 tbsp smooth peanut butter
  • 3 Lebanese cucumbers, seeds removed, finely sliced
  • 4 Atlantic skinless salmon fillets
  • 1 cup combined mint and coriander leaves
method:
Method:

  1. Cook noodles according to directions on the packet; drain.
  2. Combine Soy, vinegar, oil, ginger, garlic, tahini and peanut butter together in a large bowl until smooth.
  3. Add noodles and cucumber and stir gently to coat noodles evenly.
  4. Heat an extra tablespoon of sesame oil in large frying pan over high heat, add salmon, skin side down and cook for 2 minutes or until crispy.
  5. Flip salmon over a cook a further 2 minutes or until cooked to your liking.
  6. Remove from pan, flake flesh and serve with the sesame noodles and herbs.
  7. Garnish with fried shallots and a squeeze of lime if desired.

title: Skirt steak with kale slaw and crispy noodles
author: pwg
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 4x 200g skirt steaks, trimmed or 2 x 350g packets pillow steaks
  • 1/2 cup Chang's Crispy Noodle Salad Dressing
  • 1 bunch curly kale, stalks removed, leaves shredded
  • 2 green onions or shallots, sliced into thin strips
  • 1/2 Chinese cabbage, shredded
  • 2 large carrots, finely julienned
  • 2 packets Chang's Original Fried Noodles
method:
Method:

  1. Brush steaks with 2tbsps of the dressing and cook in an oiled or non-stick frying pan over high heat; turning once until well browned and cooked to your liking ( 2 minutes each side for medium rare).
  2. Remove from pan to a plate and set aside to rest.
  3. Combine kale, onion, cabbage, carrot and remaining dressing in a large bowl, slice beef thinly across the grain and serve with the kale slaw.
  4. Serve topped with mint and coriander leaves and toasted sesame seeds if desired.

title: Grilled King Prawns with Pad Thai noodles lime and mint
author: pwg
metadescription: Ah, spring, what would we do without you and your delicious aromas, seasonal vegetables and extra hours of daylight?
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1.2 kg green king prawns, about 12 large, split lengthways deveined, heads discarded
  • 2/3 cup Chang’s Asian salad dressing
  • 2 tbsps Chang’s Sesame oil
  • 2 tbsps garlic chives
  • 1/2 daikon, julienned, placed in a bowl of cold water
  • 1/2 bunch radishes, thinly sliced
  • 1/2 iceberg lettuce, thinly sliced
  • 2 green onions or shallots, sliced thinly
  • 1 x 250g packet Changs Pad Thai noodles, prepared according to directions on packet
method:
Method:

  1. Combine 2tbsps of the dressing with the sesame oil and brush over prawns.
  2. Heat a char grill pan or bbq over high heat and cook prawns, flesh side-down.
  3. Cook in batches for 2-3 minutes or until browned and just cooked through, turning halfway.
  4. Remove from pan, scatter with chives and serve with combined daikon, radish, lettuce, onion, noodles and remaining dressing.
  5. Serve topped with mint leaves and lime wedges if desired.

You can swap out the pork mince for lamb or chicken mince if desired.

title: Vegan Rice Paper Rolls with Spring Florals
author: pwg
metadescription: A simple, tasty, healthy recipe using our Rice Vermicelli Noodles and Hoisin, Soy, and Sesame oil sauces. Beautiful and really speaks to the season!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 250g {include file='cms_template:Changs-Vermicelli-Rice-Noodles'}
  • 20x edible rice paper rounds
  • 40 edible flowers and nasturtium leaves
  • 1 telegraph cucumber cut into matchsticks
  • 200g firm tofu or tempeh, sliced into matchsticks
  • 3 green onions, sliced thinly
  • 1 cup combined mint and coriander leaves
  • 10 green oak lettuce leaves, halved
  • 1/2 cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1/4 cup roasted peanuts, crushed
  • 2tbsps {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • Juice of 1 lime
method:
Method:

  1. Place vermicelli in a large heatproof bowl, pour over enough boiling water to cover noodles and stand 4 minutes or until noodles are softened: drain and cut into rough lengths.
  2. Combine vermicelli in a large bowl with cucumber, tofu, onion, herbs and lettuce: set aside.
  3. Fill a large bowl with warm water.
  4. Using one piece of rice paper round at a time, submerge in water for 10-15 seconds to soften then transfer to clean working surface.
  5. Place two edible flowers or nasturtium leaves along the centre of the softened rice paper round and top with 1/4 cup of the noodle mixture.
  6. Fold in ends then roll rice paper to seal.
  7. Place on baking paper lined tray and cover with a clean damp tea towel and repeat with remaking rice paper rounds, flowers and noodle mixture.
  8. Combine Hoisin, Soy, sesame oil and lime juice in a small bowl and serve with rice paper rolls

title: Bang Bang chicken with Asian noodles
author: pwg
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 750ml ( 3 cups) chicken stock
  • 125 ml ( 1/2 cup) Shaoxing wine
  • 4 cm piece ginger, thinly sliced
  • 3 large chicken breast fillets
  • 250g Chang’s Longlife noodles
  • 1/4 Chinese cabbage, thinly sliced
  • 1/4 red cabbage, thinly sliced
  • 2 large carrots, cut into julienne or shredded
  • 125ml (1/2 cup) Chang’s Oriental Asian Salad Dressing
method:
Method:

  1. Combine stock, wine and ginger in a medium saucepan and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
  2. Add chicken, reduce heat to low and simmer 8-10 minutes until chicken is just cooked through.
  3. Remove from stock and set aside to cool slightly. Meanwhile, add noodles to stock and simmer for 2-3 minutes; drain and reserve noodles.
  4. Shred chicken and toss in a large bowl with cabbage, carrot, dressing and noodles.
  5. Serve garnished with toasted nuts, chilli, mint and extra dressing if desired.

title: Chinese-style stir fry pork and noodles with snake beans
author: pwg
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 2tbsps peanut oil
  • 3 shallots, finely chopped
  • 1 long red chilli, finely chopped
  • 2 garlic cloves, crushed
  • 4 cm piece ginger, peeled, finely grated
  • 600g minced pork
  • 1/3 cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1/4 cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsps {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 200g snake beans, quartered
  • 2x 200g packets {include file='cms_template:Wok-Ready-Noodles'}
method:
Method:

  1. Heat peanut oil in a large frying pan or wok over medium heat, add shallots, chilli, ginger and garlic and stir fry for 2 minutes or until softened.
  2. Add pork and increase heat to high.
  3. Stir fry fir 4-5 minutes or until pork is browned.
  4. Add hoisin, Soy, sesame oil, snake beans and noodles and stir fry a further 2 minutes or until beans are just tender.
  5. Serve with chopped green onions and extra chilli if desired.

You can swap out the pork mince for lamb or chicken mince if desired.

title: Garlic Lamb with Shitake and Sesame
author: pwg
metadescription: Garlic Lamb with Shitake and Sesame: swap out the lamb for beef or chicken if desired
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 8 cloves garlic, crushed
  • 2tbsps {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2tbsps {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2tbsps {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1/2 tsp castor sugar
  • 500g lamb leg steaks, thinly sliced
  • 1 large red onion, cut into small cubes
  • 500g shitake mushrooms, halved
  • 2 bunches broccolini, cut into 5cm lengths
  • 2x 390g packets {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Noodles'}, drained, rinsed under cold water
  • 1/3 cup vegetable stock
method:
Method:

  1. Mix together the garlic, half the sesame oil, sauces and sugar in a large bowl, add lamb, stir to combine and set aside, covered in the fridge for 10 minutes.
  2. Meanwhile heat remaining sesame oil in a large frying pan or wok over high heat and stir fry the lamb in batches, until well browned, remove from pan and add onion, mushrooms and broccolini and stir fry until onion is browned and softened,
  3. Return beef to pan with noodles and stock and stir fry around 2 minutes or until ingredients are mixed together and sauce is slightly thickened,
  4. Serve garnished with toasted sesame seeds and garlic chives if desired.

You can swap out the lamb for beef or chicken if desired

title: Rocky Road Style Slice With Fried Noodle
author: pwg
metadescription: Line the base and sides of an 20cm square cake pan with baking paper. Prepare all the ingredients, cutting glacé cherries and large marshmallows with scissors. Then place all the ingredients in a bowl.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 150g packet large marshmallows
  • 40g dry roasted peanuts
  • 50g Changs Gluten Free Fried noodles
  • 350g good quality milk chocolate, chopped
  • 20g Glacé Cherries
method:
Method:

  1. Line the base and sides of a 20cm square cake pan with baking paper. Prepare all the ingredients, cutting glacé cherries and large marshmallows with scissors. Then place all the ingredients in a bowl.
  2. Place chocolate into a microwave-safe bowl and cook on high power for 30 sec. Stir and repeat until chocolate is melted. Cool for a few mins, pour over the marshmallow mix and stir until combined.
  3. Spoon mixture into the pan and set aside for 2 hr or until chocolate is set – if it is extremely hot, chill in the refrigerator until set. Cut into pieces to serve.

title: Thai Prawn & Ginger noodles
author: pwg
metadescription: Thai prawns tossed into Chang’s Super Low Cal Noodles, mixed with bean sprout and ginger, with Changs fish sauce to serve.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 100g Changs Traditional Super Low Cal noodles
  • 1½-2 tbsp red curry paste
  • 1-2 tsp Changs fish sauce
  • 2 tsp light brown soft sugar
  • 1 tbsp sunflower oil
  • 25g ginger
  • 2 large garlic cloves, sliced
  • 1 red pepper, deseeded and sliced
  • 85g sugar snap peas
  • 140g beansprouts
  • 175g pack raw king prawns
method:
Method:

  1. Put the noodles in a bowl and pour over boiling water to cover them. Set aside to soak for 10 mins. Stir together the curry paste, fish sauce, sugar and 3 tbsp water to make a sauce.
  2. Heat the oil in a large wok and add half the ginger and the garlic. Cook, stirring, for 1 min. Add the pepper and stir-fry for 3 mins more. Toss in the sugar snaps, cook briefly, then pour in the curry sauce. Add the bean sprouts and prawns, and continue cooking until the prawns just turn pink.
  3. Drain the noodles, then toss these into the pan with the herbs and remaining ginger. Mix until the noodles are well coated in the sauce, then serve.

title: Sticky Salmon Super Lo-Cal Ribbon Noodle Salad
author: pwg
metadescription: Marinate Salmon with Changs Soy Sauce and Changs Hoisin Sauce and fry with honey. Mix with cucumber, capsicum, green beans and ribbon noodles
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 60ml Changs soy sauce
  • 60ml Chinese rice wine
  • 2 tablespoons Changs hoisin sauce
  • 1 tablespoons brown sugar
  • 375g salmon
  • Vegetable spray oil
  • 2 teaspoons honey
  • Crunchy noodle salad
  • ¼ Chinese cabbage
  • 2 green onions, sliced thinly
  • 1 red capsicum
  • 1 cucumber
  • 200g snow peas
  • 100g Changs Super Lo Cal Ribbon Noodles
method:
Method:

  1. In a shallow bowl, combine Changs soy sauce, rice wine, Chang's hoisin sauce and brown sugar. Place salmon in the marinade. Cover and marinate for 30 minutes.
  2. Combine salad ingredients; set aside.
  3. Remove salmon from marinade and transfer marinade to a small saucepan.
  4. Heat a non-stick pan with oil and cook salmon to taste
  5. In a small saucepan, heat reserved marinade with honey until sauce reduces and thickens slightly.
  6. Divide salad among serving plates and top with salmon. Drizzle with reduced marinade and sprinkle with sesame seeds.

title: Spicy curry noodle soup
author: pwg
metadescription: Spicy curry noodle soup with chicken and sweet potato - Boil sliced chicken, chilli, coconut milk and Changs fish sauce together, slowly adding sweet potato, peas and onion and edamame beans
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 450g Chang's Ribbon Super Low Cal noodles
  • 2 1/2 tablespoons Changs fish sauce
  • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil
  • 3 tablespoons chopped shallots
  • 3 garlic cloves, chopped
  • 2 tablespoons minced peeled fresh ginger
  • 2 tablespoons Thai yellow curry paste
  • 400ml unsweetened coconut milk
  • 300g snow peas
  • 700g sweet potato
  • 350g skinless boneless chicken thighs, thinly sliced
  • 75g cup thinly sliced red onion
  • 2 red jalapeño chiles
  • 1 lime
method:
Method:

  1. Heat oil in a heavy large saucepan over medium heat. Add shallots, garlic and ginger. Reduce heat to medium-low. Stir in curry paste, Add coconut milk and fish sauce; bring broth to boil.
  2. Cook snow peas in boiling water. Add sweet potato and cook until tender. Using the strainer, remove sweet potato from pot and rinse under cold water to cool. Bring water in same pot back to boil and cook noodles until just tender but still firm to bite. Drain; rinse under cold water to cool.
  3. Bring broth to simmer. Add chicken; simmer until chicken is cooked through. Stir in sweet potato.
  4. Heat noodles in a microwave in 30-second intervals to rewarm. Divide noodles among bowls. Divide snow peas and hot soup among bowls. Scatter red onion and chiles over soup. Garnish with lime wedges and serve.

title: Chicken & Rice Noodle Salad + Changs Oriental Fried Noodle Salad Dressing
author: pwg
metadescription: Add the noodles, cabbage, carrot, green shallot, mint and coriander to the chicken and gently toss to combine. Drizzle with dressing and gently toss to combine. Add the sesame seeds to taste
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 3 small (about 500g) single chicken breast fillets
  • 150g Changs Super Low Cal noodles
  • 1 tablespoon rice vinegar
  • 2 tablespoons fresh lime juice
  • 1 tablespoon sweet chilli sauce
  • 2 teaspoons Changs Oriental Fried Noodle Salad Dressing
  • 100g Chinese cabbage
  • 2 carrots, peeled, coarsely grated
  • 8 green shallots
  • 1/4 cup loosely packed fresh mint leaves
  • 1/4 cup firmly packed fresh coriander leaves
  • Sesame seeds to taste
method:
Method:

  1. Place chicken in a medium saucepan and cover with cold water. Place over high heat and bring to the boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer, uncovered, for 10 minutes or until chicken is cooked through. Set aside for 10 minutes to cool slightly. Shred chicken and place in a large bowl.
  2. Meanwhile, place noodles in a large heatproof bowl and cover with boiling water. Set aside for 5 minutes to soften. Drain.
  3. Combine vinegar, lime juice, sweet chilli sauce, fish sauce and oil for dressing
  4. Add the noodles, cabbage, carrot, green shallot, mint and coriander to the chicken and gently toss to combine. Drizzle with dressing and gently toss to combine. Add the sesame seeds to taste

title: White chocolate noodle crunch
author: pwg
metadescription: Melt white chocolate, milk and butter over a low heat and pour over a baking tray covered in Changs Gluten Free Fried Noodles. Leave to chill until set. Enjoy!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 100g Changs Gluten Free Fried noodles
  • Cooking spray
  • 1 cup white chocolate chips
  • 1 1/2 tablespoons milk
  • 1 tablespoon salted butter
  • 40g cranberries
method:
Method:

  1. Coat a baking sheet with the noodles, spreading out evenly to the edges.
  2. Meanwhile, add the white chocolate chips, milk, and butter to a small saucepan over low heat. Stir constantly until completely melted.
  3. Pour the entire mixture over the tray of noodles, adding the cranberries.
  4. Let sit for about an hour at room temperature.
  5. Enjoy!

title: Chicken and bok choy noodle soup
author: pwg
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 pack Changs Long Life Noodles
  • 8 cups Changs Chinese Masterstock
  • 2 chicken breasts, trimmed
  • 2 bunch baby bok choy trimmed
method:
Method:

  1. Soften the Changs Long Life Noodles in boiling water then drain and set aside.
  2. Heat the Changs Chinese Masterstock in a large saucepan with two cups water. Add the chicken breasts and simmer gently until just cooked through. Turn heat off and leave chicken in the stock for 15 minutes. Remove the chicken from stock. Keep warm.
  3. Stir the noodles and bok choy through the stock and simmer until the bok choy is tender.
  4. Thinly slice the chicken breasts. Serve with soup, noodles and bok choy. Serve the soup with Changs Sesame Oil and green onions if desired.

Note: If Changs Chinese Masterstock is unavailable, make a simple Asian stock using water, soy sauce, a little Chinese cooking wine and spices such as ginger, garlic and star anise.

title: Barbecued pork noodle soup
author: pwg
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 pack Changs Rice Vermicelli Noodles
  • 8 cups Changs Chinese Masterstock
  • 1 bunch gai larn or choy sum, trimmed and cut into 10cm lengths
  • 300g Chinese barbecued pork, thinly sliced
method:
Method:

  1. Soften the Changs Rice Vermicelli Noodles in boiling water then drain and set aside.
  2. Heat the Changs Chinese Masterstock in a large saucepan with two cups water. Add the gai larn or choy sum and simmer until tender. Stir through the noodles and barbecued pork and simmer gently until pork is heated through.
  3. Serve the soup with Changs Hoisin Sauce for dipping.

Note: If Changs Chinese Masterstock is unavailable, make a simple Asian stock using water, soy sauce, a little Chinese cooking wine and spices such as ginger, garlic and star anise.

title: Red cabbage slaw with crunchy noodles
author: pwg
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 pack Changs Crunchy Noodles
  • ½ small red cabbage
  • 1 carrot, cut into thin strips
  • ½ red capsicum, cut into thin strips
  • ½ cup coriander leaves
  • ½ cup mint leaves
  • ¾ cup Changs Oriental Fried Noodle Salad Dressing
method:
Method:

  1. Remove the core from the cabbage, discard. Roughly shred the leaves and place in a large bowl.
  2. Add the carrot, capsicum and herbs and mix.
  3. Just before serving add the Changs Crunchy Noodles and Changs Oriental Fried Noodle Salad Dressing. Toss until fully combined. Great served with chicken, meat or seafood.

title: Chilled prawn, tofu and ginger noodle salad
author: pwg
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 2 packs Changs Super Lo-Cal Noodles
  • 50g snow peas, trimmed
  • 1 teaspoon finely grated ginger
  • 3 teaspoons rice wine vinegar
  • 1 teaspoon caster sugar
  • 20 cooked king prawns, peeled and de-veined
  • 250g silken firm tofu, drained and cut into 1cm thick slices
  • 1 lebanese cucumber, shaved into ribbons
  • 1 cup snow pea sprouts, to serve
  • 2 teaspoons toasted sesame seeds, to serve
method:
Method:

  1. Rinse the Changs Super Lo-Cal Noodles under cold water then drain. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
  2. Blanch the snow peas, then finely shred. Set aside.
  3. To make the dressing, mix together the ginger, rice wine vinegar, sugar and two tablespoons water.
  4. To serve, arrange the noodles in bowls, top with prawns, tofu, snow peas and cucumber ribbons. Drizzle over dressing and garnish with sesame seeds and snow pea sprouts. Serve with Changs Soy Sauce if desired.

title: Fish, broccolini and cashew stir fry
author: pwg
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 pack Changs Long Life Noodles
  • 500g white fish fillets, cut into 2cm cubes
  • 1 onion, sliced
  • ½ red capsicum, cut into thin strips
  • 2 bunches broccolini, cut into 4cm lengths
  • 1/3 cup cashews
  • 1/3 cup Changs Oyster Sauce
method:
Method:

  1. Heat a little oil in a large wok or non stick frying pan over high heat. Add fish and stir fry in batches until golden and nearly cooked through. Remove from the heat.
  2. Whilst the fish is cooking soften the Changs Long Life Noodles in boiling water. Drain and set aside.
  3. Add onion, red capsicum and broccolini to the wok along with a little extra oil and stir fry for 2-3 minutes.
  4. Return the fish to the wok with the noodles and cashews. Toss through the Changs Oyster Sauce until fully coated and serve.

title: Pork and noodle san choy bau cups
author: pwg
metadescription: Nothing starts a meal better than San Choy Bau and this year it will bring you luck as well. Use our recipe to serve 4 (a lucky number during the Year of the Dog), while the green of the lettuce cups is also very lucky this year.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 pack {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'}
  • 1kg pork mince
  • 100g green beans, trimmed and shredded diagonally
  • 2 green onions, sliced diagonally
  • ¾ cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tablespoon toasted sesame seeds, to serve
  • 8-10 iceberg lettuce leaves, trimmed, to serve
method:
Method:

  1. Heat a little oil in a large wok or non-stick frying pan over high heat. Add pork and stir fry until browned and nearly cooked through.
  2. Add the beans and green onions and stir fry for 2-3 minutes then stir through the Changs Hoisin Sauce. Toss until the pork mixture is fully coated and cooked through then remove from the heat.
  3. Serve the pork mix in iceberg lettuce cup garnished with sesame seeds. Drizzle over a little extra Changs Hoisin Sauce if desired.

title: Salmon avocado and fried noodle salad
author: pwg
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 pack Changs Original Fried Noodles
  • 2 salmon fillets, skin and bones removed
  • 50g baby spinach leaves
  • 75g cherry tomatoes, halved
  • 1 avocado, thinly sliced
  • 1/3 cup Changs Oriental Fried Noodle Salad Dressing
  • 1 cup snow pea sprouts, to serve
method:
Method:

  1. Heat a little oil in a non-stick frying pan and cook salmon fillets until golden and still a little pink in the centre. Cool slightly then flake into large pieces.
  2. Toss the Changs Original Fried Noodles with the baby spinach and cherry tomatoes.
  3. To serve, arrange salad on serving plates along with the avocado and flaked salmon. Drizzle with Changs Oriental Fried Noodle Salad Dressing and garnish with snow pea sprout. Serve with wedges of lemon.

title: Five spice crunchy noodles
author: pwg
metadescription: Great party snack served with Changs Hoisin Sauce
ingredients:
Ingredients:
