  • 1 pack Changs Crunchy Noodles
  • 1-2 teaspoons Chinese five spice powder
  • 1/3 cup Changs Hoisin Sauce, to serve
method:
Method:

  1. Place the Changs Crunchy Noodles in a bowl and toss with the five spice powder.
  2. Season with salt and cracked black pepper

Great party snack served with Changs Hoisin Sauce

Tip
As an alternative to five spice powder, try tossing Changs Crunchy Noodles with a little chilli powder or smoky paprika.

title: Beef, Broccolini and Cashew Stir Fry
author: pwg
metadescription: For a quick and easy meal to munch on while waiting for the main course you can’t go past a Chang’s stir fry. The Broccolini brings the luck with the green and the cashews bring a satisfying crunch into the mix.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 pack {include file='cms_template:Changs-Long-Life-Noodles'}
  • 400g beef strips
  • 1 onion, sliced
  • 2 bunches broccolini, cut into 4cm lengths
  • 1/3 cup cashews
  • 1/3 cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce'}
method:
Method:

  1. Heat a little oil in a large wok or non-stick frying pan over high heat. Add beef strips and stir fry in batches until browned and nearly cooked through. Remove from the heat.
  2. Whilst the beef is cooking soften the Changs Long Life Noodles in boiling water. Drain and set aside.
  3. Add onion and broccolini to the wok along with a little extra oil and stir fry for 2-3 minutes.
  4. Return the beef strips to the wok with the noodles and cashews. Toss through the Changs Oyster Sauce until fully coated and serve.

title: Chicken oriental crispy noodle salad
author: pwg
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 pack Changs Original Fried Noodles
  • ½ Chinese cabbage (wombok)
  • 5 green onions, sliced
  • ½ cup slivered almonds, toasted
  • 1 long red chilli, sliced
  • ¼ cup coriander leaves
  • 2 cooked chicken breasts, shredded
  • 2/3 cup Changs Oriental Fried Noodle Salad Dressing
method:
Method:

  1. Finely shred the cabbage and place in a large bowl along with the Changs Original Fried Noodles, green onions and almonds. Toss until combined.
  2. Just before serving add the chicken and pour Changs Oriental Fried Noodle Salad Dressing over salad and toss until salad is fully coated.

title: Prawn oriental crispy noodle salad
author: pwg
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 pack Chang's Original Fried Noodles
  • ½ Chinese cabbage (wombok)
  • 6 green onions, sliced
  • ½ cup slivered almonds, toasted
  • 18 cooked king prawns, peeled and de-veined
  • ¾ cup Chang's Crispy Noodle Salad Dressing
method:
Method:

  1. Finely shred the cabbage and place in a large bowl along with the Changs Original Fried Noodles, green onions and almonds. Toss until combined.
  2. Just before serving add the prawns and pour Chang's Crispy Noodle Salad Dressing over salad and toss until salad is fully coated.

title: Pork Meatballs with Oyster Sauce
author: pwg
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 600g pork mince
  • 4 green onions, finely chopped
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed
  • 2 tbsps {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 3 tbsps {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1tbsp {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 egg, lightly whisked
  • 1/4 cup rice bran or vegetable oil
  • 100g mini mixed capsicums, sliced
  • 200g Swiss brown mushrooms, sliced
  • 150g oyster mushrooms, sliced
  • 150g cherry tomatoes
  • 1/2 small bunch watercress, washed, picked
  • 2x 100g packets {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'}
method:
Method:

  1. Combine mince, onion, 1/2 the garlic, Oyster sauce, 1/2 the Sesame oil, Soy sauce and egg in a medium bowl until well mixed.
  2. Shape 1/4 cup portions into balls, flatten slightly.
  3. Heat the rice bran oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
  4. Cook meatballs, in batches, turning, for 4-5 minutes or until golden and cooked through.
  5. Transfer to a plate and add remaining garlic and Sesame oil to the frying pan with the capsicum, mushrooms and tomatoes.
  6. Stir fry 2-3 minutes or until mushrooms are softened.
  7. Return meatballs to pan with watercress and noodles and toss to combine.

title: Peppered Beef and Crispy Noodles stir fry
author: pwg
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 4 tbsps rice bran oil
  • 500g beef stir fry strips
  • 1 large red onion, thinly sliced
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed
  • 1tsp cracked black pepper
  • 1 bunch snake beans, sliced
  • 1 bunch baby bok choy, sliced
  • 3tbsps {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp raw sugar
  • 1 tbsp {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2x100g packets {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'} li>
method:
Method:

  1. Heat a wok over high heat, add 1/2 the oil and beef strips, in batches, for 2 minutes.
  2. Transfer to a plate. Heat remaining oil in the wok and add onion, garlic and pepper, stir fry for 2 minutes or until, onion is softened. Add beans and bok choy to the wok and stir fry a further 3 minutes.
  3. Combine soy sauce, sugar and hoisin sauce in the wok with 2tbsps cold water and toss to combine well.
  4. Return beef to the wok with the noodles, tossing again to combine and serve garnished with lime wedges, sliced red chilli and fried shallots if desired.

title: Thai Fish Cakes with Crunchy Asian Slaw
author: pwg
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 600g boneless firm white fish fillets
  • 1/4 cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed
  • 2tbsps lime juice
  • 3 green onions, finely chopped
  • 1/2 cup panko breadcrumbs
  • 1x 100g packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Crunchy-Fried-Noodles'}
  • 1/3 cup vegetable oil
  • 1x100g packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'}
  • 2 carrots, coarsely grated/li>
  • 1/2 small wombok cabbage, finely sliced
method:
Method:

  1. Process the fish, soy sauce, garlic and lime juice in a food processor until well combined.
  2. Transfer to a large bowl, add onions, breadcrumbs and the Crunchy noodles and combine well with a large spoon.
  3. Shape the mixture into 1/3 cup sized patties.
  4. Heat oil in a large frying pan over medium heat. Cook patties, in batches, turning, for 4-5 minutes or until golden and cooked through.
  5. Serve with combined fried noodles, carrots and cabbage.
  6. Garnish with lime wedges, sweet chilli sauce and drizzle with extra soy sauce if desired.

title: Crispy Fried Chicken Noodle Salad
author: pwg
metadescription: Crispy Fried Chicken Noodle Salad - what's not to love?
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 500g chicken stir fry strips
  • 2 egg whites, lightly beaten
  • 4 tsps cornflour
  • 1 cup ( 250ml) vegetable oil
  • 2 bunches broccolini, sliced
  • 1 large brown onion, sliced
  • 2/3 cup fresh bean sprouts
  • 3 tbsps Chinese rice wine
  • 2tbsps Changs Oyster sauce
  • 1/2 cup chicken stock
  • 2 x 100g packets Changs Oriental Fried noodles
method:
Method:

  1. Toss chicken strips in a bowl with cornflour, salt and pepper until chicken is evenly coated.
  2. Cover bowl and refrigerate 10 minutes.
  3. Heat oil in a wok and fry chicken strips for 3 minutes or until golden and cooked through, remove from oil with a slotted spoon and place on a plate.
  4. Carefully remove all but 2tbsps of the oil from the wok and stir fry broccolini and onion 2 minutes or until broccolini is bright green but still crisp.
  5. Add bean sprouts, wine, oyster sauce and stock and stir fry a further 1 minute.
  6. Return chicken to the wok with the crispy noodles and toss to coat in the sauce.
  7. Divide between 4 plates and garnish with roasted peanuts and coriander if desired.

title: Thai Chicken Drumsticks
author: pwg
metadescription: Who doesn't love drumsticks?
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1/4 cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1/3 cup sweet chilli sauce
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed
  • 12 chicken drumsticks, skin on
  • 2x 100g packets {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'}
  • 3 cups finely shredded red cabbage
  • 4 green onions, sliced
  • 2 carrots, coarsely grated
method:
Method:

  1. Combine soy sauce, sweet chilli sauce and garlic in a small bowl.
  2. Place chicken in a large glass bowl and pour over the sauce mixture, tossing well to combine.
  3. Place chicken on a baking rack inside a baking tray and bake in a 200 degree Celsius oven, basting and turning the chicken for 20-25 minutes or until chicken is golden and cooked through.
  4. Serve chicken with combined fried noodles, cabbage,  onions and carrot.
  5. Garnish with toasted slivered almonds, coriander and sesame seeds if desired.

title: Lime and Lemongrass Beef Noodle Salad
author: pwg
metadescription: Asian infused beef with lime and lemongrass
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 800g beef rump steak, thinly sliced
  • 1/4 cup Chang’s Oriental Salad Dressing
  • 1 lemongrass stalk, white part only, finely chopped
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed
  • 2 tsps finely grated lime rind
  • 2 tsps Chang’s Fish Sauce
  • 2 tbsps peanut oil
  • 1 bunch chop sum, (150g), washed, roughly chopped
  • 1/4 cup vegetable stock
  • 1 packet Chang’s Crunchy Noodles
method:
Method:

  1. Combine beef, dressing, lemongrass, garlic, rind and fish sauce together in a large bowl. Cover and refrigerate for 20mins.
  2. Heat peanut oil in large wok over high heat until smoking. Add the beef mixture, in batches, and cook, stirring for two minutes or until browned. Remove to small bowl.
  3. Add choy sum and stock to the wok and cook, stirring, a further two minutes or until choy sum is wilted. Return beef to wok and cook for a further minute to combine.
  4. Stir through noodles and serve with lime wedges, fresh chopped chilli and coriander if desired.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Stir Fried Hoisin Chicken and Fresh Herbs
author: Joy
metadescription: Stir Fried Hoisin marinated chicken, cooked with lots of fresh herbs and vermicelli noodles
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 pack of {include file='cms_template:Changs-Vermicelli-Rice-Noodles'}
  • 500g chicken (or pork) mince
  • 1 tbsp. vegetable, peanut or canola oil
  • 3 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce'}
  • 1 tbsp. rice vinegar
  • 1 tsp. sugar
  • ½ tsp. salt
  • 1 bunch asparagus, tender parts only, cut into 1 cm pieces
  • ½ medium red chilli, finely sliced - optional
  • 1 cup basil leaves, finely sliced
  • 1 cup coriander leaves, roughly chopped
  • 1 cup flat leaf parsley leaves, roughly chopped
  • 1 cup finely chopped spring onions-white part only
method:
Method:

  1. Place noodles in a bowl and pour over boiling water. When just soft drain under cold water and reserve.
  2. Heat a wok and add the oil then the chicken mince and stir until it begins to colour. Add all other ingredients and stir until the meat is cooked.
  3. Transfer the noodles to a serving platter or shallow bowl and spoon over the chicken and herb stir fry.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Chinese Master Stock Wonton Soup
author: pwg
metadescription: Chang’s Chinese Master Stock Prawn and Chicken Wonton Soup
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 300g green prawns, peeled, deveined, finely chopped
  • 200g chicken mince
  • 2 tbsp. finely chopped coriander
  • 2 tsp. finely grated fresh ginger
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce'}
  • 1 tsp. brown sugar
  • 20 egg wonton wrappers
  • 1 x 500ml {include file='cms_template:Changs-Master-Stock'}
  • 1 x 250g packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Long-Life-Noodles'}
  • 115g baby corn
  • 1 tbsp. sesame seeds, toasted
  • 2 small red chillies, finely chopped
  • 1/2 cup loosely packed coriander leaves
method:
Method:

  1. To make the wontons, combine prawn meat, chicken mince, coriander, ginger, oyster sauce and sugar in a medium bowl.
  2. Place a wonton wrapper on a clean surface. Spoon a heaped teaspoon of mixture onto the centre of the wrapper.
  3. Use a pastry brush dipped in water to brush the edges.
  4. Fold the wrapper diagonally to enclose the filling.
  5. Brush one corner with water and bring down under the filling to meet the second corner and press to join together. Repeat with remaining wrappers and mixture.
  6. Combine Chang’s Chinese Master Stock and water in a large saucepan over high heat and bring to the boil. Reduce heat to low and add the corn and wontons.
  7. Cook for five minutes or until the stock returns to a simmer and wontons are cooked through.
  8. Meanwhile, cook the noodles according to directions on the packet and divide between four deep serving bowls.
  9. Divide wontons and corn between the bowls, ladle over some of the hot soup, sprinkle with sesame seeds, chilli and coriander.

title: Chinese Master Stock Tofu and Asian Greens
author: pwg
metadescription: Braised Asian Greens and Tofu in Chinese Master Stock with Red Peanuts
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 x 500ml {include file='cms_template:Changs-Master-Stock'}
  • 500ml cold water
  • 1 bunch baby bok choy, thickly sliced
  • 1 bunch choy sum, thickly sliced
  • 140g snow peas, trimmed
  • 600g silken tofu, cut into 12 cubes
  • 1 small red chilli, finely sliced
  • 1 small green chilli, finely sliced
  • 1/2 cup red peanuts, roughly chopped
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
method:
Method:

  1. Combine Chang’s Chinese Master Stock and water in large saucepan.
  2. Bring to the boil over high heat and add bok choy, choy sum, peas and tofu.
  3. Reduce heat, simmer for four-to-five minutes or until vegetables are just cooked through.
  4. Divide greens and tofu between four deep serving bowls and ladle over some of the hot stock.
  5. Serve topped with chilli, peanuts and drizzled with the sesame oil.

title: Chinese Master Stock Crispy Chicken
author: pwg
metadescription: Red Roasted Crispy Skinned Chicken
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 x 500ml {include file='cms_template:Changs-Master-Stock'}
  • 500ml cold water
  • 1.6kg free range chicken, cut into 8 pieces
  • 500ml sunflower oil
  • 1 x 250g packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Long-Life-Noodles'}, cooked according to directions on the packet
  • 1 cup loosely packed mint leaves, torn
  • 1 cup loosely packed coriander leaves
  • 1 spring onions, thinly sliced
  • 1 large carrot, sliced into matchsticks
  • 1/3 cup toasted cashews, roughly chopped
  • 1/4 cup {include file='cms_template:Crispy-Noodle-Salad-Dressing'}
method:
Method:

  1. Pour Chang’s Chinese Master Stock and water into a large saucepan or stockpot and bring to the boil over high heat.
  2. Add chicken and cover. Simmer over medium heat for 20 minutes.
  3. Turn heat off and let chicken sit in the stock for one hour, covered.
  4. Remove chicken from stock and place on a paper towel lined tray for 30 minutes to dry the skin.
  5. Pour oil into large wok or saucepan and heat to 180 degrees.
  6. Place chicken pieces, skin side up carefully into the hot oil.
  7. Fry, ladling the hot oil over the top of the chicken for four-to-five minutes or until chicken is crisp and golden.
  8. Combine remaining ingredients in a large bowl and serve with the crispy skinned chicken.

title: Chinese Master Stock Braised Turkey and Asian Slaw
author: pwg
metadescription: Red Braised Turkey Breast with Crunchy Asian Slaw and Mint
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 x 500ml {include file='cms_template:Changs-Master-Stock'}
  • 500ml cold water
  • 800g turkey breast
  • 1 large carrot, thinly sliced
  • 1 small red onion, thinly sliced
  • 1/4 small red cabbage, finely shredded
  • 1/4 small Chinese cabbage, finely shredded
  • 1 cup loosely packed mint leaves
  • 1/3 cup {include file='cms_template:Crispy-Noodle-Salad-Dressing'}
  • 1 x 100g packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'}
method:
Method:

  1. Combine Chang’s Chinese Master Stock and water in a large saucepan.
  2. Bring to the boil over medium heat, add the turkey breast, cover and simmer for 15 minutes.
  3. Turn off the heat and leave the turkey in the stock a further 15 minutes or until cooked through.
  4. Meanwhile combine carrot, onion, cabbages, mint and noodles in a large bowl, drizzle over dressing and mix gently to combine.
  5. Remove turkey from stock and slice thickly.
  6. Divide turkey between four serving plates or shallow bowls, ladle over a little of the hot stock and serve with the coleslaw.

title: Chinese Master Stock Poached Salmon
author: pwg
metadescription: Atlantic Salmon poached in Chinese Master Stock with Shiitake Mushrooms and Coriander Salad
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 x 500ml {include file='cms_template:Changs-Master-Stock'}
  • 500ml cold water
  • 4 x 180g Atlantic salmon fillets, skin on
  • 8 spring onions, sliced into 3cm lengths
  • 100g shitake mushrooms, sliced thinly
  • 1 cup bean sprouts
  • 1 small red chilli, finely sliced
  • 1 cup loosely packed coriander leaves
  • 1/4 cup {include file='cms_template:Crispy-Noodle-Salad-Dressing'}
method:
Method:

  1. Combine Chang’s Chinese Master Stock and water in a medium saucepan, bring to the boil over high heat.
  2. Add salmon fillets, spring onions and mushrooms, cover and bring back to the boil.
  3. Turn off the heat and let the salmon and vegetables sit in the stock for six minutes or until salmon fillets are cooked through.
  4. Meanwhile, combine bean sprouts, chilli, coriander and dressing in a small bowl.
  5. Divide salmon, spring onions and mushrooms between four shallow serving bowls or plates, ladle over a little of the hot stock and serve with the coriander salad.

title: Chinese Master Stock Drunken Chicken Noodles
author: pwg
metadescription: Chinese Master Stock Drunken Chicken with Egg Noodles
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 500ml {include file='cms_template:Changs-Master-Stock'}
  • 500ml cold water
  • 2 spring onions, sliced into 3cm lengths
  • 1.6kg free range chicken, cut into 8 pieces
  • 800g packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Shelf-Fresh-Noodles-Hokkien-Style'} or {include file='cms_template:Changs-Egg-Noodles'}
  • 200g sugar snap peas, lightly steamed
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 2 small red chillies, finely chopped
  • 2 tbsp. crispy fried shallots
method:
Method:

  1. Combine Chang’s Chinese Master Stock, water and spring onions together in a large stock pot or saucepan, add chicken pieces and bring to the boil over high heat.
  2. Reduce heat to medium and simmer, covered for 20 minutes or until chicken is cooked through.
  3. Cook noodles according to directions on packet, drain and divide between four deep serving bowls.
  4. Divide chicken and spring onions between bowls, ladle over some of the stock and serve drizzled with the sesame oil, chill and crispy fried shallots.

title: Chinese Master Stock Caramelised Pork Belly
author: pwg
metadescription: Caramelised Pork Belly in Chinese Master Stock with Chilli and Orange
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 x 500ml {include file='cms_template:Changs-Master-Stock'}
  • 500ml cold water
  • 1kg piece pork belly
  • 150g brown sugar
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fish-Sauce'}
  • 1 lime, juiced
  • 1 large orange, thickly sliced
  • 1 bunch baby bok choy, thickly sliced, steamed
  • 1 bunch choy sum, thickly sliced, steamed
  • 2 tbsp. sesame seeds, toasted
  • 2 small red chillies, finely sliced
method:
Method:

Preheat oven to 160 degrees.

  1. Combine Chang’s Chinese Master Stock and water in a large saucepan and bring to the boil over high heat.
  2. Add pork belly, cover and bring back to the boil.
  3. Place saucepan in the preheated oven for two hours. Remove pork belly from pan and set aside on a tray while you prepare the caramel.
  4. In a large frying pan heat brown sugar over medium heat and stir until dissolved and caramelised – around five minutes.
  5. Add half a cup of the hot stock, fish sauce, lime juice and orange slices.
  6. Simmer gently for five minutes or until caramel is reduced and thickened.
  7. Cut pork belly into thick pieces and add to the caramel, tossing gently to coat the pieces.
  8. Serve with the orange slices, steamed greens, sesame seeds and chilli, and drizzle with remaining caramel.

title: Chinese Master Stock Poached Barramundi and Crunchy Noodles
author: pwg
metadescription: Barramundi poached in Chinese Master Stock with Greens and Crunchy Noodles
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 x 500ml {include file='cms_template:Changs-Master-Stock'}
  • 500ml cold water
  • 4 x 200g barramundi fillets
  • 150g yellow and green beans, trimmed
  • 1 bunch Chinese broccoli, roughly chopped
  • 1/2 cup loosely packed coriander leaves
  • 1 x 100g packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Crunchy-Fried-Noodles'}
method:
Method:

  1. Combine Chang’s Chinese Master Stock and water in a large saucepan and bring to the boil over medium heat.
  2. Add fish fillets and simmer gently for three minutes.
  3. Add beans and broccoli and simmer a further two minutes or until fish is cooked through.
  4. Divide fish fillets, beans and broccoli between four deep serving bowls.
  5. Ladle over some of the hot stock and garnish with the coriander and crunchy noodles.

title: Chang's Marinated Steak
author: pwg
metadescription: The perfect BBQ steak seasoned with a zesty warm marinade
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1kg of your favourite cut of steak, cut to 3-4 cm thick
  • 1 bunch asparagus
  • 1 ¼ cups extra virgin olive oil
  • 4 cloves garlic, crushed or grated
  • 1 thumb ginger, grated
  • Finely grated zest of half a lemon
  • ½ - 1 medium chilli, finely chopped – optional
  • ½ cup finely chopped coriander
  • ½ cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • ¼ cup lemon juice
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
method:
Method:

  1. Brush the steak with a little of the olive oil and cook over medium heat on a BBQ, char grill or frying pan, turning once or just a couple of times. When the steak is half cooked, add the asparagus to the hot plate.
  2. For the warm marinade, place a heavy metal baking dish just big enough to hold the steak on a flame and when hot, add a tablespoon of the oil, the garlic, ginger and chilli if using. Cook for 30 seconds then add the lemon zest and juice, oyster sauce, coriander and the remaining olive oil. Remove from heat and add the sesame oil.
  3. Place the steak and asparagus in the warm marinade and cover with a clean tea towel or very loosely with foil. Rest for five minutes then turn steak over and rest for another five minutes.
  4. Slice steak against the grain and serve with warm marinade spooned over and asparagus
  5. For an added extra serve with Chang’s Long Life Noodles, or for those looking for a gluten free option, steamed rice.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free,

title: Beef with Broccoli on Crispy Noodles
author: pwg
metadescription: A rich and tasty Chinese inspired stirfry
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 100g {include file='cms_template:Changs-Vermicelli-Rice-Noodles'} , cut into 4-6 cm lengths (or cheat and use {include file='cms_template:Changs-Crunchy-Fried-Noodles'})
  • 1 ½ cups neutral oil
  • 400g sirloin, rump or fillet, trimmed of all fat and cut into thin strips across the grain
  • 300g broccoli florets
  • 1 clove garlic, finely chopped or crushed
  • 2cm piece of ginger, peeled and grated
  • 1 small can bamboo shoots
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Soy-Sauce-Dark'}
  • ½ tsp. salt
  • 2/3 cup chicken stock or water
  • 1 tbsp. cornflour dissolved in 1 tablespoon of water

Marinade:

  • 1 tbsp.Shaosing rice wine
  • 1 tsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • ½ tsp. sugar
  • 1 tsp. cornflour, heaped
method:
Method:

  1. Combine marinade ingredients, add the beef and set aside. Boil water in readiness to blanch the broccoli.
  2. Heat the oil in a wok to very hot and drop in the noodles in three or four batches. Be careful as they'll puff right up in seconds. Drain noodles on kitchen paper, reserving the oil in the wok, straining if needed. Reduce the heat of the oil and cook the marinated beef until it colours, drain and put aside.
  3. Heat the oil to very hot and return the beef, cooking until golden brown. Drain again on kitchen paper and discard all but 2 tablespoons of the oil.Blanch the broccoli in boiling water.
  4. Add the garlic and ginger to the wok and stir for a second before returning the beef to the wok along with the drained, still firm broccoli and bamboo shoots.
  5. Add the oyster, light soy and dark soy sauces and salt, stirring to coat the meat and vegetables. Add the stock or water and finally the dissolved cornflour and stir until the sauce thickens.
  6. To serve, place the noodles on a plate and the stir fry in the centre on top.
  7. If you'd like this dish to be gluten free, be sure select the Chang's Vermicelli Rice Noodles and a gluten free chicken stock.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Giant Freckles with Marshmallows
author: pwg
metadescription: Classic Giant Freckles with Marshmallow and Crunchy Noodles
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 2 x 200g  blocks white chocolate, chopped
  • 1 x 200g block milk chocolate, chopped
  • 1 packet Chang's Crunchy Fried Noodles
  • 2/3 cup hundreds and thousands
  • 1 cup marshmallows , sliced
method:
Method:

  1. Line 3 baking trays with non- stick baking paper. Place white and milk chocolates in two heatproof microwave safe bowls. Microwave on medium (50%) for 2-3 minutes or until melted and smooth, stirring with a metal spoon every 30 seconds.
  2. Spoon 1/4 cup measures of chocolate onto a prepared tray. Repeat three more times to form four rounds.
  3. Sprinkle generously with noodles, hundreds and thousands and marshmallows. Repeat with remaining chocolate , noodles, hundreds and thousands and marshmallows.
  4. Set aside 1 hour or until firm.
allergen: Contains Gluten Contains Dairy Nut Free

title: Crunchy Salted Caramel Coated Popcorn Bars
author: pwg
metadescription: Tasty salted caramel popcorn bars with added Chang’s noodle crunch
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • ¼ cup vegetable oil
  • 1/3 cup popping corn
  • 2tsp sea salt flakes
  • 2 packets Chang’s Crunchy Noodles
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 200g butter, chopped
  • ½ cup golden syrup
method:
Method:

  1. Grease and line a 20 by 30cm slice pan with baking paper.
  2. Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. When oil is hot, add corn. Cover with a tight fitting lit, and shake pan gently until popping stops. Transfer popcorn to large bowl with the salt and noodles.
  3. Place sugar, butter and golden syrup in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Cook, stirring, 5 minutes or until butter has melted and sugar is dissolved. Bring to the boil for 8-10 minutes, or until mixture is golden.
  4. Spoon quickly into prepared pan. Place a piece of baking paper over popcorn to cover, and press firmly into pan.
  5. Stand at room temperature for 1 hour, or until cool.
allergen: Contains Dairy Contains Gluten Contains Nuts

title: Double Decker Chocolate Crackles
author: pwg
metadescription: A fun double-decker take on the traditional Chocolate Crackle favourite
ingredients:
Ingredients:

Milk Chocolate Crackles

  • 200g milk chocolate, finely chopped
  • 60g butter, chopped
  • 200g white marshmallows
  • 3 cups Chang’s Crunchy Noodles
  • 1 cup Rice Bubbles
  • ¼ cup Dollar Sweets Magic Jurassic Bitz

White Chocolate Crackles

  • 200g white chocolate, finely chopped
  • 60g butter, chopped
  • 200g white marshmallows
  • 3 cups Chang’s Crunchy Noodles
  • 1 cup Rice Bubbles
method:
Method:

  1. Heat milk chocolate, butter and marshmallow in a small saucepan over low heat until melted and smooth.
  2. Combine melted milk chocolate in a large bowl with noodles, rice bubbles and Dollar Sweets.
  3. Spoon mixture into a greased and lined 16 by 26cm slice pan, pressing down firmly with the back of a spoon. Set this mixture aside.
  4. Repeat the above steps with white chocolate.
  5. Spoon white chocolate mixture over the milk chocolate layer, pressing down firmly with the back of a spoon.
  6. Refrigerate for 1 hour, or until firm.
  7. Remove crackle from pan, and cut into 12 pieces.
allergen: Contains Dairy Contains Gluten May Contain Nuts

title: Funtastic Marshmallow Easter Egg Bars
author: pwg
metadescription: Quick marshmallow bars that are quick to make and great for the kids!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 120g butter, chopped
  • 200g white marshmallows
  • 2 packets Chang’s Fried Noodles
  • 2 cups Froot Loops cereal
  • 32 small chocolate Easter eggs to decorate
method:
Method:

  1. Place butter and marshmallows into a medium saucepan. Cook, stirring over medium heat, until mixture is melted and smooth.
  2. Transfer to a large bowl. Add noodles and cereal, stir well to combine.
  3. Spoon into a 25 by 30cm slab pan, lined with baking paper. Allow to cool for 20 minutes, then while still warm press Easter Eggs into the top of the mixture.
  4. Refrigerate for 30 minutes, or until firm.
  5. Cut into 16 bars.

title: Crunchy Chocolate Monsters
author: pwg
metadescription: Adorable Milk Chocolate monsters with Chang’s Fried Noodles
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 400g milk chocolate, melted
  • 3 packets Chang's Original Fried Noodles
  • 1 packet M&M’s
  • Writing Icing Pens in red and orange to decorate
  • Icing sugar to dust
method:
Method:

  1. Line a large baking tray with baking paper and set aside.
  2. Combine melted chocolate and noodles; mix until well combined. Form 2 heaped tablespoons of mixture into a mound onto prepared tray, repeat with remaining mixture.
  3. Refrigerate, uncovered, for 30 minutes or until firm.
  4. Use the writing icing pens to stick 3 M&M’s in a row on each mound, forming a row of eyes.
  5. Draw a squiggly line underneath the eyes as a mouth.
  6. Dust lightly with icing sugar if desired.
allergen: Contains Dairy Contains Gluten Contains Nuts

title: Bunny Bait
author: pwg
metadescription: Colourful and fun Bunny Bait textured bark
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 3 cups popped plain popcorn
  • 2 x 200g white chocolate, melted
  • 1 packet Chang's Crunchy Fried Noodles
  • 1 packet Chang's Original Fried Noodles
  • 1 cup mini salted pretzels
  • 2 cups mini M&M’s
  • 1 cup Hundreds and Thousands
method:
Method:

  1. Combine all ingredients except hundreds and thousands into a large bowl.
  2. Spoon onto a large tray lined with baking paper. Sprinkle over hundreds and thousands and set aside for 1 hour, or until firm.
  3. Break into pieces to serve
allergen: Contains Dairy, Contains Gluten, May Contain Nuts

title: Taiwanese Beef and Noodle Soup
author: pwg
metadescription: A fresh Taiwanese beef soup served with Chang’s Egg Noodles
ingredients:
Ingredients:

Beef Broth:

  • 1 kg beef brisket or beef cheeks
  • 1 carrot, peeled and roughly chopped
  • 1 tomato, cut into chunks
  • 3 cloves garlic, crushed or finely chopped
  • ½ thumb ginger, sliced
  • 2 spring onions, cut into 6 cm lengths
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. chilli sauce
  • 100ml {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • ¼ tsp ground black pepper
  • 1 medium red chilli, sliced
  • 2 tsp. honey
  • 60ml (¼ cup) red wine
  • 1 tsp. five spice powder

To serve:

  • 1 packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Egg-Noodles'}
  • 1 bunch baby bok choy, quartered lengthways
  • Chilli, sliced (optional)
method:
Method:

  1. Combine the broth ingredients in a large pot and cover with cold water. Bring to boil, skimming any grey scum that forms on top and discarding.
  2. Simmer gently for three hours until beef is tender. Slice cooked beef and reserve.
  3. Fill a separate saucepan with water and bring to boil. Simmer noodles until just cooked, adding the bok choy for the final 30 seconds, and drain.
  4. Place noodles, bok choy and beef in bowls, pour or ladle over the hot broth.
  5. For a gluten free alternative, use Chang’s Gluten Free Wok-Ready Pad Thai Noodles.

title: Steamed Whole Fish with Ginger and Soy
author: pwg
metadescription: A tasty classic Chinese special occasion banquet dish.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 x 800g whole Snapper, scaled
  • 1 large thumb ginger, very finely julienned
  • 4 spring onions, 2 very finely sliced diagonally, 2 julienned lengthways for garnish
  • ½ cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • ½ cup peanut or other neutral oil
  • 1 tsp. Chang’s Sesame Oil
  • 1 bunch coriander to garnish
  • 3 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'} to serve

Rub:

  • 2 tbsp. Shaosing rice wine
  • 1 tsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1 tsp. sugar
  • 1 tsp. salt
method:
Method:

  1. Pat the fish dry and make two deep incisions on each side. Mix the rub ingredients and rub all over the fish and into the incisions.
  2. Place the fish into a steamer (best on a sheet of baking paper or a cabbage leaf) and steam for about 10-15 minutes, checking the fish is just ‘set’ near the bone in the incisions.
  3. While the fish is steaming, place the ginger and sliced spring onions in a small bowl with half the soy sauce. Heat half the oil to smoking hot and pour over the spring onions, soy and ginger.
  4. Transfer the fish to a platter, and pour over the remaining soy and the spring onion and ginger mixture. Heat the remaining oil to smoking and pour over the fish.
  5. Sprinkle over sesame oil and garnish with coriander and julienned spring onion.
  6. Serve with a small bowl of oyster sauce.
allergen: Dairy Free Gluten Free

title: Steamed Scallops with Ginger and Soy
author: pwg
metadescription: Asian style steamed scallops in a flavourful dinner party entrée
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 12 scallops in the shell, removed from the shell and hard, shiny filament removed, replaced in the shell
  • 3 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 4 tbsp. Shaoxing rice wine
  • 1 tsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1 clove garlic, crushed or grated
  • ½ thumb ginger, grated
  • 1 medium chilli, finely sliced – optional
  • 2 spring onions, white and pale green parts only, cut into 5cm pieced then shredded lengthways
  • 24 coriander leaves
method:
Method:

  1. Place scallops on a plate in a large bamboo steamer. Set aside.
  2. Combine soy sauce, Shaosing, sesame oil, garlic, grated ginger and chilli in a bowl, and spoon evenly over each scallop in the shell.
  3. Place steamer over a wok of boiling water, and cook scallops for a 2-3 minutes or until just opaque.
  4. Scatter spring onions and coriander over scallops and serve from steamer or on individual plates.
allergen: Dairy Free Gluten Free

title: Malaysian Fried Chicken Noodle Salad
author: pwg
metadescription: Malaysian inspired Fried Chicken Salad with Noodles.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 6 boned chicken thighs, whole or cut into 3-4cm pieces
  • 2-3 packets Chang’s Original Fried Noodles
  • 1 lemon, quartered

Marinade:

  • 1 tbsp. Malay curry powder
  • 1 tsp. Turmeric powder
  • 1 tsp. five spice powder
  • 1 egg, beaten
  • 1 tsp. sugar
  • ½ tsp. salt
  • 1 tbsp. Chang’s Oyster Sauce
  • 1 tbsp. Chang’s Soy Sauce
  • 1 tbsp. cornflour
  • 1 tbsp. Shaosing rice wine or dry sherry
  • 1 tbsp. Chang’s Sesame Oil
method:
Method:

  1. Combine marinade ingredients in a bowl, adding the chicken. Stir the mixture to coat chicken until covered, then place in refrigerator for 12-24 hours.
  2. Shallow fry the chicken in vegetable or peanut oil in a frying pan until golden brown and cooked through, turning once. Once cooked, drain excess oil on paper towel.
  3. Place the Chang’s Original Fried Noodles on a serving platter, and top with fried chicken.
  4. Serve with lemon wedges.
allergen: Dairy Free

title: Ginger and Hoisin Glazed Pork
author: pwg
metadescription: Delicious ginger and soy glazed pork neck
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 piece pork neck, approximately 1.2-1.4 kilos

Glaze:

  • 1/3 cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 180g brown sugar
  • 1/3 cup rice wine vinegar
  • 4 cloves garlic, grated or crushed
  • 1 thumb ginger, peeled and finely grated or chopped and pounded to a paste in a mortar and pestle
  • 1 packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Long-Life-Noodles'} - to serve
method:
Method:

  1. Combine glaze ingredients in a small saucepan and heat gently to dissolve sugar, stirring occasionally.
  2. Place pork on a rack in a baking dish, and brush all over with 1/3 of the glaze.
  3. Cook pork for one hour at 180 ̊ celsius, removing after 20-40 minutes to brush again with the glaze. Allow pork to rest, loosely covered with foil for ten minutes.
  4. Pour boiling water over the noodles, then drain and place on an oval shaped platter.
  5. Cut pork into thin slices, and place on top of the noodles. Drizzle any pan or resting juices over the pork to serve.
allergen: Nut Free Dairy Free Gluten Free

title: Gai Lan with Oyster Sauce
author: pwg
metadescription: Yum Cha favourite Gai Lan with Oyster Sauce
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 bunch fresh Gai Lan (Chinese Broccoli), washed thoroughly
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • ½ tsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
method:
Method:

  1. Bring a large saucepan of water to the boil, set aside to simmer. Cut the Gai Lan into 4-6 pieces, and either boil or steam until cooked al dente.
  2. Drain the Gai Lan and set aside.
  3. Combine the Chang’s Oyster Sauce, Sesame Oil and Light Soy Sauce in a medium bowl.
  4. Spoon oyster sauce over the Gai Lan before serving.
    5. TIP: For an authentic Yum Cha experience, cut the Gai Lan after boiling or steaming.
allergen: Dairy Free

title: Easy Vietnamese Chicken Pho Noodle Soup
author: pwg
metadescription: Simple and easy do-it-yourself Chicken Pho Noodle Soup.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 small organic or free-range chicken, trimmed of excess fat
  • 25g rock sugar
  • 2 tbsp. fish sauce
  • ½ thumb ginger
  • 2 spring onions, white and pale green parts only
  • 1 packet Chang’s Thai Rice Noodles
  • 1 medium white onion, sliced thinly on a mandolin
  • 1 bunch Vietnamese mint (or regular mint)
  • 1 bunch Thai basil
  • 2 cups fresh bean sprouts
  • 2 medium red chillies, sliced
  • 1 lime, quartered
  • 6 tbsp. Chang’s Hoisin sauce
method:
Method:

  1. Place chicken, sugar, fish sauce, ginger and spring onions in a large pot and cover with plenty of cold water.
  2. Bring chicken to the boil, and reduce to a simmer, cooking for one hour. Skim off any scum that forms on the surface of the broth during the process. Remove the chicken and cook the stock for another 20 minutes.
  3. Drain the stock through a fine sieve and discard the ginger and spring onions.
  4. Remove the meat from the chicken and shred or cut into thin, bite sized pieces. You can refrigerate the chicken and the stock at this stage, and come back to it in a day or two if you like. If you do refrigerate the stock, peel off any fat that may settle on the top.
  5. Arrange the basil, mint, sliced chilli, lime quarters and bean sprouts on a plate to share. Place Hoisin sauce in one large, or four smaller dishes.
  6. Place the rice noodles and the chicken in a bowl and pour over boiling water to heat through. Drain off water and divide chicken meat, noodles and onion between four large soup bowls. Ladle over the stock and serve with the plate of condiments and Hoisin sauce.
  7. Serve the bowls of soup and encourage guests to add the bean sprouts, mint, basil, chilli, lime juice and hoisin sauce to taste.
allergen: Nut Free Dairy Free Gluten Free

title: Dumplings
author: pwg
metadescription: Delicious, traditional Asian dumplings.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 400g finely ground minced lean pork
  • 1 spring onion, finely chopped
  • 1/3 cup Chinese cabbage, boiled, drained and finely chopped
  • 1 tsp. sugar
  • 1 tsp. freshly grated ginger
  • 1 egg, beaten
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. neutral oil
  • 20 gyoza wrappers, or wheat-based wonton wrappers
method:
Method:

  1. Combine all ingredients with the oil, besides the wrappers, and mix thoroughly with your hands.
  2. Place one teaspoon of mixture inside each wrapper, and fold in half. Secure the pastry by slightly wetting the edge with a little water, then pinch down gently to seal.
  3. Place finished gyoza on a board or plate, pushing down gently so the bottom of each flattens. Repeat with remainder of wrappers.
  4. Heat a heavy based frying pan with a lid, and add the neutral oil. Place the gyoza into the pan, frying until the base begins to brown. Once golden, add ¼ cup of water to the pan and replace the lid. Reduce the heat to low, and cook for a further five minutes.
  5. Remove gyoza from the pan, and allow to cool.
  6. Serve with Chang's Gluten Free Tamari Light Soy Sauce (280ml) to dip.
allergen: Nut Free Dairy Free

title: Chinese Marinated Whole Roasted Chicken
author: pwg
metadescription: For the main course we suggest our Chinese marinated Chicken. With a mix of sweet and spicy, this is a meal that will titillate the senses but most of all, will keep the whole family full and happy this Chinese New Year.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 whole chicken, excess fat removed

Marinade:

  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsp. C{include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 2 tbsp. honey
  • 1 tsp. Chinese five spice
  • 2 cloves garlic, finely grated or crushed
  • 1 tbsp. ginger, grated
method:
Method:

  1. Combine all marinade ingredients into a saucepan and bring to the boil. Simmer contents for a minute to dissolve the honey, then allow to cool.
  2. Place the chicken in a bowl and cover both inside and out with the marinade. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for 2-12 hours, ideally turning the chicken in the marinade occasionally.
  3. Bring the chicken back to room temperature and bake on a rack in a 200 degree oven for 60-70 minutes, brushing with the remaining marinade after 30 minutes.
  4. Check the progress of the chicken by inserting a skewer or point of a sharp knife into thigh; if the juices run clear, it is cooked, if they are still rosy, cook for another 10 minutes.
  5. Serve marinated chicken with steamed Chinese broccoli or other Asian green vegetables.
allergen: Nut Free Dairy Free Gluten Free

title: Buddah’s Delight
author: pwg
metadescription: Colourful vegetarian stir fry with a Chang’s kick.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 2 tbsp. vegetable oil
  • 2 cloves garlic, finely chopped or crushed
  • 1 tsp. ginger, grated
  • 1 cup Wombok or Chinese cabbage, cut into 2cm pieces
  • ½ cup water chestnuts, halved lengthways
  • 8 ears baby corn
  • ½ carrot, cut into thin diagonal slices
  • 8 small (or 4 medium, halved) shitake mushrooms, stalks removed
  • ½ cup sliced bamboo shoots or whole tips
  • 2 baby bok choy, leaves separated
  • 200g firm tofu, cut into 2cm pieces
  • 1 cup bean sprouts
  • 1 small head broccoli, florets removed, stem discarded
  • 100g enoki mushrooms
  • 1 tbsp. Shaosing rice wine
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 2 tbsp. water
method:
Method:

  1. Heat the oil in a large wok, adding the cabbage, carrot, corn and shitakes, cooking over high heat. Add the garlic and ginger, and cook for 30 seconds, then add water chestnuts, bamboo shoots, bok choy, and broccoli, and stir fry for one minute. Add the bean sprouts and enoki and cook for a further minute.
  2. Add the Shaosing, soy and oyster sauce, and two tablespoons of water. Cook for one minute before adding the sesame oil and tofu, gently folding through.
  3. Serve immediately.
allergen: Dairy Free Gluten Free

title: Roast Asian Pork Belly
author: pwg
metadescription: Simple and impressive Asian Pork Belly
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1kg (or larger) piece of skin on pork belly, all bones removed
  • 1 tbsp. pouring salt

Marinade:

  • 1 thumb ginger, finely grated
  • 4 cloves garlic, crushed or finely grated
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 2 tbsp. Shaosing rice wine
  • 2 tbsp. brown sugar
method:
Method:

  1. Score the skin of the pork with a very sharp knife, cutting through the skin to the white fat to portion the pork (this is a lot easier than trying to cut through the crackling).
  2. Place the pork on a rack and pour a cup of boiling water over the skin, patting it dry immediately with paper towel – this helps dry the skin out and makes for better crackling.
  3. Mix the marinade ingredients well to dissolve the sugar and place the marinade in a baking dish large enough to hold the pork. Place the pork in the marinade skin-side up, being careful not to get the marinade on the skin. Place in the refrigerator uncovered for 2-12 hours.
  4. Pour enough water around the sides of the pork for the liquid to come up to just below the skin. Rub the skin with the salt, forcing the salt into the scores of the cuts.
  5. Bake at 220 degrees for 15 minutes then reduce to 160 degrees for another 90 minutes. If the skin begins to burn, cover carefully with foil. If the skin has not yet crisped up, turn the grill on your oven and grill the skin for a few minutes, until cooked golden brown.
  6. Allow pork to rest for 10 minutes before portioning along the cut marks made previously.

Serve with Chang's famous Chang’s Crispy Noodle Salad.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Poached Whole Salmon Fillet with Asian Flavours
author: pwg
metadescription: Impress your guests with this poached whole salmon
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 cup Shaosing rice wine
  • 1 cup chicken stock
  • ½ cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • ½ cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsp. ginger, finely grated
  • 2 tbsp. palm sugar, grated or chopped
  • 4 whole star anise
  • 2 strips (2cm x 4cm) orange peel, white pith removed from back or one orange piece, dried orange or tangerine peel
  • 1 stick cinnamon
  • ½ medium red chilli, sliced or finely diced
  • 1 whole salmon fillet, skin on, pin bones removed
  • Shredded spring onions, to serve
method:
Method:

  1. Combine all ingredients except the salmon and spring onions and bring to the boil, then gently simmer for 10 minutes to dissolve the palm sugar and integrate the flavours.
  2. Place the salmon skin side down in a baking dish just big enough to hold it. Pour the liquid over the salmon, and cover the dish tightly with foil. Bake in a 200 degree oven for 15 minutes then remove from the oven, and allow to rest for another 10 minutes without removing the foil.
  3. Remove the foil and serve straight from the baking dish, or transfer to a serving platter, spooning cooking juices over the fish and topping with the shredded spring onion.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Oysters with three dressings
author: pwg
metadescription: A Thai, Chinese and Japanese dressing that will make your fresh oysters pop!
ingredients:
Thai:

Ingredients:

  • 2 dozen oysters, preferably freshly shucked
  • 24 coriander leaves

Dressing:

  • ½ cup lime juice
  • 1 tsp. palm sugar, grated
  • ¼ - ½ medium red chilli, very finely diced
  • 1 red shallot, very finely diced
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fish-Sauce-280ml'}

Japanese:

Ingredients:

  • 2 dozen oysters, preferably freshly shucked

Dressing:

  • 1/3 cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1/3 cup mirin
  • 1 tbsp. rice vinegar
  • ½ tsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1 tbsp. very finely sliced chives

Method: Japanese

  1. Combine all dressing ingredients in a bowl, and spoon over oysters.
  2. Serve immediately.

Chinese:

Ingredients:

  • 2 dozen oysters, preferably freshly shucked

Dressing:

  • 2 tbsp. neutral oil
  • 1 clove garlic, very finely diced
  • 1 thumb ginger, cut in fine julienne
  • ¼ cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. Shaosing rice wine
  • ½ tsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1 spring onion, shredded
method:
Method: Thai

  1. Combine all dressing ingredients in a medium bowl, and mix to dissolve the sugar.
  2. Spoon a little over each oyster, and top with a coriander leaf.
  3. Serve immediately.

Method: Japanese

  1. Combine all dressing ingredients in a bowl, and spoon over oysters.
  2. Serve immediately.

Method: Chinese

  1. Heat one tablespoon of oil in a small saucepan, and soften the garlic and ginger. Add the soy and Shaosing, and remove from heat and allow to cool.
  2. Stir through the sesame oil and the remaining tablespoon of neutral oil.
  3. Spoon dressing over oysters and top with shredded spring onion.
  4. Serve immediately.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Leftover Turkey and Noodle Stir Fry
author: pwg
metadescription: Quick and easy fix for that leftover Christmas turkey!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Shelf-Fresh-Noodles-Hokkien-Style'}
  • 200g leftover turkey meat, thinly sliced
  • 2 tbsp. vegetable oil
  • 1 clove garlic, crushed or sliced
  • ½ thumb ginger, peeled and grated or cut into matchsticks
  • 2 spring onions, white and pale green parts only, sliced
  • ½ medium red chilli, sliced – optional
  • 2 shitake mushrooms, sliced
  • 4 stalks asparagus, cut into 3-4cm lengths
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
method:
Method:

  1. Heat the oil in a wok, and add the garlic, ginger, spring onion, mushrooms and asparagus. Stir fry vegetable for 30 seconds, or until soft.
  2. Add the turkey meat, and stir fry for a further minute.
  3. Add the noodles, oyster and soy sauces, and cook for another minute.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Hoisin Glazed Christmas Ham
author: pwg
metadescription: Jazz up your traditional Christmas Ham with an Asian-style glaze!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 whole ham, bone in leg
  • 20 cloves
  • 2 oranges, sliced

Glaze:

  • 1 cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsp. rice vinegar
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsp. honey
  • 1 tsp. five spice powder
method:
Method:

  1. Combine all the glaze ingredients in a medium bowl, and stir or whisk to combine.
  2. Carefully peel the skin from the ham and discard. Make 1cm deep cuts in a diamond pattern approximately 2-3cm apart all over the fat on the surface of the ham.
  3. Place a clove at the intersection of each cut. Place the ham into a large baking dish alongside the orange slices, adding one cup of water.
  4. Brush half the glaze all over the ham and bake in a hot (220 degree) oven for 15 minutes. Remove the ham, and brush with the remainder of the glaze. Bake for another 15 minutes.
  5. Serve ham warm from the oven, or cold from the refrigerator.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Hoisin and Honey Baked Chicken Drumsticks
author: pwg
metadescription: Child friendly, snack-sized chicken drumsticks with hoisin and honey.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • ½ cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1 tsp. rice vinegar
  • 1 tbsp. honey, dissolved in 2 tbsp boiling water
  • 2 cloves garlic, finely grated or crushed
  • 8 meaty chicken legs, skin on
method:
Method:

  1. Combine all ingredients except the chicken in a large bowl, then add the chicken and stir to coat evenly. Cover chicken and refrigerate for 2-12 hours.
  2. Place chicken in a foil lined baking dish, and bake in a pre-heated oven at 200 degrees for 30-35 minutes, or until the juices run clear when pierced with the point of a knife.
  3. Serve hot or cold, garnish with sesame seeds for flavour.
allergen: Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Five Spice Marinated Duck Breast
author: pwg
metadescription: A hearty, slow roasted, Five Spice and Hoisin marinated duck breast with cherries on top.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 4 duck marylands
  • 2 tbsp. Chang's Hoisin Sauce
  • 2 tbsp. Shaosing rice wine
  • 2 tbsp. brown sugar
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed or finely grated
  • 1 tsp. five-spice powder
  • 2 cinnamon sticks
  • 24 large cherries
method:
Method:

  1. Combine all ingredients except duck and cherries in a large, ceramic bowl. Add the duck marylands, stirring to coat well. Cover marinade, and refrigerate for 8-12 hours.
  2. Place marylands skin side up in a baking dish with the marinade, including cinnamon. Add one cup of water, and cover dish with foil.
  3. Cook for 45 minutes at 170 degrees. Remove foil, and add cherries to the baking dish, making sure they are covered with liquid. Cook for a further 20 minutes to crisp the duck skin.
  4. Transfer a Maryland portion and six cherries to four, shallow bowls and serve with steamed Asian greens and rice or noodles.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Bacon-wrapped Yakitori
author: pwg
metadescription: BBQ ready bacon-wrapped yakitori skewers.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 2 chicken thighs, excess fat and skin removed, cut into 2cm pieces
  • 20 very thin slices streaky bacon, halved
  • 6 thick spring onions, white and pale green parts only
  • 4 thick spears asparagus
  • 8 small button mushrooms or 6 medium, halved
  • 5 tbsp. Chang’s Hoisin sauce
  • 1 tbsp. Chang’s Light Soy Sauce
  • 1 tsp. Chang’s Sesame Oil
method:
Method:

  1. Marinate the chicken in two tablespoons of Hoisin sauce for 2-12 hours, covered in the fridge.
  2. Once ready, wrap the bacon around the chicken, and alternate with spring onions on soaked wooden skewers.
  3. Heat the remaining Hoisin sauce with the Soy sauce, Sesame Oil and two tablespoons of water in a small saucepan and reserve.
  4. Cook the skewers over medium heat on a BBQ or grill until just done. Transfer to platters and spoon over the remaining sauce to serve.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Asian Marinated Roast Beef Fillet
author: pwg
metadescription: Asian inspired marinated roast beef fillet with oyster sauce
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 2 garlic cloves, finely grated or crushed
  • 1 kg beef fillet
  • 2 tbsp. vegetable oil
  • 250g Chang’s Rice Vermicelli Noodles
  • ¼ cup (60ml) Chang’s Fish Sauce
  • 2 long red chillies, seeds removed, chopped
  • ¼ cup (55g) caster sugar
  • ¼ cup (60ml) rice vinegar
  • ¼ cup (60ml) lime juice
  • 75g roasted peanuts, chopped
  • 1 golden shallot or spring onion, thinly sliced
  • 1/3 cup chopped coriander

Marinade:

  • 2 tbsp. Chang’s Light Soy Sauce
  • 2 tbsp. Chang’s Oyster Sauce
  • 1 tbsp. honey
  • 1 tbsp. Chang’s Sesame Oil
  • 1 tsp. ginger, grated
  • 2 garlic cloves, finely grated or crushed
method:
Method:

  1. Whisk soy, honey, sesame, ginger and half the garlic in a small bowl. Coat the beef in marinade, then refrigerate for two hours. Remove the meat from the refrigerator, and allow to warm to room temperature before cooking.
  2. Preheat oven to 190°C. Heat the oil in a flameproof roasting pan in the stove over medium-high heat. Sear the beef for 1-2 minutes on each side.
  3. Transfer beef to the oven, and roast for 20-25 mins for medium-rare (or done until your liking).
  4. Rest beef, loosely covered, for 10 minutes and prepare salad.
  5. Soak noodles in boiling water according to packet instructions, then drain and place in a large bowl.
  6. Shake fish sauce, chilli, sugar, vinegar and lime juice in a screw top jar to combine. Toss half the dressing with noodles, shallot or spring onion, nuts and coriander.
  7. Slice the beef and serve with the noodles and sprouts, drizzled with the remaining dressing.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Magical Wands
author: pwg
metadescription: Enchanted liquorish wands to keep your kids spellbound!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 150g white chocolate, chopped
  • ¾ cup hundreds and thousands
  • 15 sticks black liquorice
  • 1 packet Chang's Crunchy Fried Noodles
method:
Method:

  1. Line a baking tray with non-stick baking paper.
  2. Place chocolate in a heatproof microwave safe bowl. Heat, uncovered, for 1-2 minutes on high (100% power), stirring every 30 seconds with a metal spoon until smooth.
  3. Combine hundreds and thousands and Chang’s Crunchy Noodles in a small, deep bowl.
  4. Dip ¼ of each liquorice stick into the chocolate mixture, then dip straight into the noodle mixture to coat.
  5. Place liquorice onto tray, repeat with remaining liquorice.
  6. Allow to set on trays.

title: White Chocolate Birds Nests
author: pwg
metadescription: A tasty tangle of chocolate, colourful Hundreds & Thousands and Chang’s Original Fried Noodles.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 2 x 200g blocks of white chocolate, melted
  • 3 packets Chang’s Original Fried Noodles
  • 2/3 cup Hundreds and Thousands
  • 10 paper texas muffin cases
  • 30 assorted mini Easter eggs
  • Icing sugar to dust
method:
Method:

  1. Combine chocolate, noodles and Hundreds and Thousands in a medium bowl. Divide mixture amongst paper cases.
  2. Using a teaspoon, press into the centre of the mixture to make a deep indent.
  3. Place cases in the fridge for 30 minutes, or until firm. Remove the nests from the paper cases.
  4. Place three Easter eggs into each nest, and dust lightly with icing sugar.
allergen: Contains Gluten Contains Dairy May Contain Nuts

title: Homemade Fright Night Lollypops
author: pwg
metadescription: Easy to make spooky toffee lollypops with added Chang’s crunch
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 12 lollypop sticks
  • 1 ½ cups white sugar
  • 3 Tsps glucose syrup
  • ¼ cup + 2 tsps cater
  • 1-2 drops red food colouring if desired
  • 1 packet Chang's Original Fried Noodles
method:
Method:

  1. Line 2 flat baking trays with baking paper. Refrigerate until ready for use.
  2. Combine sugar, glucose syrup and water in a medium saucepan. Cook over low heat, stirring with a wooden spoon until sugar is completely dissolved.
  3. Once dissolved, increase the heat to medium-high, and bring to the boil (do not stir) then educe the heat to low. Boil gently for 15 minutes, or until mixture has reached hard crack stage (149C to 154C). To test for hard crack, drop a spoonful of toffee into a bowl of cold water. Allow to cool then remove from water. The mixture is ready if the toffee is brittle.
  4. When the toffee is ready, remove saucepan from heat. After removing the trays from the fridge, drop tablespoons of mixture onto the baking trays.
  5. Using a toothpick, swirl the food colouring gently through the centre of each lollypop.
  6. While mixture is still warm, press a lollypop stick into the centre of each circle and scatter with noodles, pressing in gently.

title: Spooky Toffee Bark
author: pwg
metadescription: Quick and tasty Halloween Toffee bark to keep kids and adults enchanted
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 2 Packets Chang's Crunchy Fried Noodles
  • 1 cup mini salted pretzels
  • 2 Mars bars, roughly chopped
  • 2 Crunchie bars, roughly chopped
  • 250g butter, chopped
  • 2 cups white sugar
  • 100g milk chocolate, finely chopped
method:
Method:

  1. Combine noodles, pretzels, mars and crunchie bars together in a large bowl. Scatter mixture over two large baking trays lined with baking paper.
  2. Combine butter and sugar in a medium saucepan, cooking and stirring over low heat until sugar is dissolved. Increase heat to medium, and boil gently for 10 minutes or until toffee reaches hard crack stage. To test for this stage, drop a spoon full of mixture into a bowl of cold water. When cooled, remove toffee from the water. Mixture is ready when the toffee is brittle.
  3. When the toffee is ready, pour over noodle mixture on trays. Sprinkle over chocolate.
  4. Refrigerate, uncovered, for 30 minutes or until hard. Break into pieces before serving.

title: Grown Up’s Rocky Road
author: pwg
metadescription: The classic Rocky Road with an adult twist.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 500g high quality dark chocolate
  • 2/3 cup raisins
  • 2 tbsp. brandy
  • 1 packet Chang’s Original Crispy Noodles
  • 100g Turkish Delight
method:
Method:

  1. Combine raisins and brandy in a medium bowl, and soak for 30 minutes.
  2. Break the chocolate into small pieces and place in two rectangular plastic takeaway containers. Place the containers in the microwave, and heat the chocolate in 30 second increments, stirring regularly until smooth.
  3. Stir in the soaked raisins, noodles and Turkish delight through the chocolate, and refrigerate for two hours, or until set.
  4. Remove the Rocky Road from the container, and slice into pieces with a heavy kitchen knife.
allergen: Nut Free

title: Sesame and Miso Glazed Chicken with Fresh and Crunchy Noodle Salad
author: pwg
metadescription: Asian infused sesame and miso glazed chicken with a fresh, crunchy noodle salad
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1/2 cup (110g) yellow miso paste
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed
  • 1 tbsp {include file='cms_template:Changs-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 800g skinless chicken thigh fillets, cut into 4cm wide strips
  • 1/4 cup vegetable oil
  • 3/4 cup self- raising flour
  • 3/4 cup cornflour
  • 2 tbsp sesame seeds

Fresh and Crunchy Noodle Salad:

  • 1 bunch mint, leaves picked
  • 1 bunch coriander, leaves picked
  • 2 green onions, thinly sliced
  • 2 packets {include file='cms_template:Changs-Crunchy-Fried-Noodles'}
  • 1/2 cup {include file='cms_template:Crispy-Noodle-Salad'}
method:
Method:

  1. Place miso paste, garlic and sesame oil in a large bowl and mix well to combine. Add chicken and toss to combine. Cover and set aside for 20 minutes.
  2. Add vegetable oil to large frying pan and heat over medium-high heat until hot. Place flours in a large bowl and whisk to combine. Place the marinated chicken into the flour mixture and toss to coat.
  3. Cook the chicken, in batches, for 2-3 minutes or until golden and cooked through. Drain on paper towel.
  4. Sprinkle with sesame seeds and serve immediately with the fresh and crunchy noodle salad.

title: Chicken and Watermelon Noodle Salad
author: pwg
metadescription: Asian inspired chicken and watermelon salad with a hint of freshness
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 500g chicken mince
  • 2 tbsps Chang’s Sesame Oil
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed
  • 2 tbsps Chang’s Light Soy Sauce
  • 1 tbsp Chang’s Fish sauce
  • 1/4 cup Chang’s oriental salad dressing
  • 1 red onion, thinly sliced
  • 1 cup bean sprouts, trimmed
  • 1/2 cup Vietnamese mint leaves
  • 2 packets Chang’s Crunchy Noodles
  • 250g seedless red watermelon, chopped
method:
Method

  1. Heat oil in a wok over high heat until smoking. Add chicken mince and cook, stirring to break up any lumps, for 4 minutes or until chicken starts to brown.
  2. Add garlic and cook, stirring, a further 2 minutes or until aromatic. Remove from heat.
  3. Add soy sauce, fish sauce and dressing and toss to combine. Add onions, bean sprouts, mint leaves, noodles and watermelon and toss gently to combine.
  4. To prepare the salad, combine mint, coriander, onion and noodles together in a large bowl. Stir in dressing and serve immediately. Serve with lime wedges if desired.
  5. Serves 4 as a main meal.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Spiced Chicken with Couscous and Noodle Salad
author: pwg
metadescription: Asian inspired chicken with couscous and noodle salad
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 tsp ground Chinese five spice
  • 2 tsps ground cumin
  • 2 tbsps Chang’s Hoisin Sauce
  • 2 tbsps Chang’s Sesame Oil
  • 4 x 200g skinless chicken breast fillets
  • 11/2 cups couscous
  • 250g mixed medley tomatoes or cherry tomatoes, chopped
  • 2/3 cup mint leaves, torn
  • 2/3 cup coriander leaves
  • 1 packet Chang’s Original Fried Noodles
  • 1/2 cup Chang’s Oriental Salad Dressing
method:
Method:

  1. Combine five spice, cumin, sauce, oil and chicken in a large bowl and mix well to combine. Refrigerate, covered, for 20 minutes.
  2. Heat a chargrill pan over medium-high heat and cook the chicken for 5 minutes each side until golden and cooked through. Set aside and keep warm.
  3. Cook couscous according to directions on packet. Combine couscous, tomatoes, mint, coriander and noodles together in a large bowl. Serve with the chicken and drizzle with dressing just before serving.

title: Roast Pumpkin and Quinoa Noodle Salad
author: pwg
metadescription: Asian inspired salad with roast pumpkin, quinoa, feta and walnuts
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 small Japanese pumpkin (2.8 kg), cut into 4cm slices
  • 2 tbsps {include file='cms_template:Changs-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 2 tbsps olive oil
  • 2 tbsps thyme leaves
  • 300g tri- coloured quinoa, rinsed
  • 1 cup walnuts, roughly chopped
  • 150g feta, crumbled
  • 1 packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'}
  • 1/2 cup {include file='cms_template:Crispy-Noodle-Salad-Dressing'}
method:
Method:

  1. Preheat oven to 180 degrees centigrade. Spread pumpkin over a large baking tray lined with baking paper.
  2. Drizzle with combined oils and sprinkle with thyme and bake for 25-30 minutes or until golden and tender. Transfer to large bowl.
  3. Cook quinoa according to directions on packet and set aside to cool.
  4. Gently toss pumpkin, quinoa, walnuts, noodles and dressing together and serve topped with crumbled feta.
allergen: Dairy free

title: Pork, Spinach and Coconut Noodle Salad
author: pwg
metadescription: Asian inspired pork with a spinach and coconut salad
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 2 tbsps Chang’s Sesame Oil
  • 500g minced pork
  • 1 large brown onion, finely chopped
  • 2 garlic cloves, crushed long fresh red chilli, finely chopped
  • 2 bunches English spinach, washed, roughly chopped
  • 2 tbsps Chang’s Fish sauce
  • 2/3 cup coconut water
  • 1/2 cup mint leaves
  • 1/4 cup Thai basil leaves
  • 1 packet Chang’s Original Fried Noodles
  • 1/3 cup Chang’s Oriental Salad Dressing
method:
Method:

  1. Heat oil in a wok over high heat until smoking. Add pork mince and cook, stirring to break up any lumps, until pork starts to brown.
  2. Add onion and garlic and cook whilst stirring for a further two minutes or until aromatic. Add spinach to the wok and stir until wilted.
  3. Add fish sauce, and coconut water and cook for a further two minutes.
  4. Remove wok from heat and stir in mint, basil leaves and Chang’s fried noodles. Serve immediately with lime wedges and shaved coconut if desired.

title: Lime and Ginger Chicken with Cucumber and Coconut Noodle Salad
author: pwg
metadescription: Asian infused chicken with sticky lime and ginger along with a fresh cucumber and coconut salad
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 tbsp finely grated ginger
  • 2 tbsps honey
  • 2 tbsps {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsps finely grated lime rind
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed
  • 8 chicken thigh fillets, skin on

Cucumber and coconut salad:

  • 1 tbsp {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1 tbsp coconut or brown sugar
  • 2 Lebanese cucumbers, roughly chopped
  • 1 cup fresh shaved coconut
  • 2 packets {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Gluten-Free-Fried-Noodles'} for a gluten-free option.
method:
Method:

  1. Preheat oven to 200 degrees centigrade. Combine ginger, honey, soy sauce, lime zest, garlic and chicken in a large bowl and refrigerate, covered, for 20 minutes.
  2. Spread chicken over the base of a greased baking tray and cook for 20 minutes, turning halfway, or until cooked through. Remove from oven and keep warm.
  3. To prepare the salad, place oil and sugar in a large bowl, stirring well until sugar is dissolved. Add cucumber and toss to combine. Gently toss through coconut and noodles and serve immediately with the chicken.
allergen: Dairy free

title: Lamb, Beetroot, Apple & Watercress Noodle Salad
author: pwg
metadescription: Asian inspired lamb with a red apple and beetroot watercress salad
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 12 lamb cutlets, drenched
  • 3/4 cup Chang’s Oriental Salad Dressing
  • 1/4 cup mint leaves, chopped
  • 2 tbsps Chang’s Sesame Oil
  • 2 yellow beetroots, washed, thinly sliced
  • 2 red beetroots, washed, thinly sliced
  • 2 large red apples, cored, julienned
  • 1 bunch watercress, washed, picked
  • 1 bunch rocket, washed, roughly chopped
  • 1 packet Chang’s Original Fried Noodles
  • 1/4 cup Chang’s Oriental Salad Dressing
method:
Method:

  1. Combine lamb cutlets, dressing and chopped mint in a large bowl. Cover and refrigerate for 20 minutes.
  2. Brush oil over a heated chargrill pan and cook the lamb, in batches, 2 minutes each side or until cooked as desired. Remove lamb to large plate, cover loosely with foil and leave to rest while you prepare the salad.
  3. Combine beetroots, apple, watercress, rocket and noodles in a large bowl and serve lamb cutlets with the salad and a drizzle of Chang’s salad dressing
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Coconut Chicken with Apple and Fennel Slaw
author: pwg
metadescription: Coconut flavoured chicken with apple and fennel slaw - a perfect summer meal
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 375ml (1/2 cups) coconut yoghurt
  • 2 tbsps brown sugar
  • 1 tbsp Chang’s Fish sauce
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed
  • 1 lime, zest and juice
  • 800g chicken tenderloins
  • 2 tbsps Chang’s Sesame Oil

Apple and fennel slaw:

  • 3 tbsps extra virgin olive oil
  • 21/2 tbsps Apple cider vinegar
  • 1/2 tsp brown sugar
  • 2 fennel bulbs, thinly sliced plus 2 tbsps chopped fennel fronds
  • 2 large pink lady apples, cored, thinly sliced
  • 1 packet Chang’s Original Fried Noodles
method:
Method:

  1. Whisk yoghurt, sugar, fish sauce, garlic, zest and juice together in a large bowl. Add chicken and stir well to coat. Refrigerate, covered, for 20 minutes.
  2. Brush oil over heated chargrill pan and cook chicken, in batches, 2 minutes each side or until golden and cooked through. Remove from chargrill and keep warm.
  3. To prepare the slaw, whisk oil, vinegar and sugar together in a large bowl. Add the fennel, fennel fronds, apple, chicken and noodles and toss quickly to combine.
  4. Serve immediately.
allergen: Nut Free

title: Sesame Lime Beef and Noodle Salad
author: pwg
metadescription: Asian infused beef and noodle salad with sesame lime
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 3 tsps {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 3 tsps {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 2 tbsps sesame seeds
  • 1 tbsp finely grated lime rind
  • 750g beef rump steak, thinly sliced
  • 2 tbsps peanut oil
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed
  • 1 bunch (150g) Choy sum, trimmed, coarsely chopped
  • 200g broccolini, trimmed, roughly chopped
  • 1/2 cup vegetable stock
  • 1 packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Crunchy-Fried-Noodles'}
method:
Method:

  1. Combine soy sauce, sesame oil, seeds, rind and beef in a large bowl. Refrigerate, covered, for 20 minutes.
  2. Heat oil in a large wok until smoking. Cook beef, in batches, for 2 minutes or until browned. Remove from wok and set aside.
  3. Add garlic, and cook for 20 seconds. Add Choy sum, broccolini and stock and cook for 2-3 minutes until broccolini is tender.
  4. Return beef to wok and cook a further 2 minutes.
  5. Remove wok from heat and quickly toss through the noodles. Serve with chopped fresh chilli if desired.
allergen: Dairy free

title: Zucchini Fritters with Roast Duck and Hoisin Sauce
author: Joy
metadescription: Zucchini Fritters served with Roast Duck and Hoisin Sauce. An interesting Asian Dinner Party entrée.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 3 tbsp. vegetable or other neutral oil
  • 3 medium zucchini, coarsely grated
  • 1 clove garlic, crushed
  • 1 small red onion, very finely sliced then roughly chopped
  • 1 tbsp. finely chopped parsley
  • 1 tbsp. finely chopped coriander
  • ½ tsp. Chinese five spice powder
  • ½ cup self-raising flour
  • ½ Chinese roast duck – available from Chinese BBQ restaurants
  • 1 Lebanese cucumber, cut into 4 cm long batons
  • 4 spring onions, white and pale green parts only, halved lengthways and cut into 4 cm lengths
  • 2/3 cup Chang's Hoisin Sauce
method:
Method:

  1. Soften the onion and garlic in one tablespoon of oil then allow to cool and mix with the zucchini, egg yolks, flour, five spice powder, coriander and parsley. Season well with sea salt and black pepper.
  2. Whisk the egg whites until firm and fold through the mixture.
  3. Heat the duck in a hot oven to re-crisp the skin and then remove meat from bones and cut into ½ cm slices.
  4. Spread remaining two tablespoons of oil over the flat grill and spoon or ladle out enough mixture to make 10-12cm fritters (2-3 tablespoons). Turn the fritters when the bottom is golden brown and bubbles appear on the surface.
  5. Transfer the fritters to serving plates and top with the duck slices, cucumber and spring onion, spooning a little Chang’s Hoisin sauce over the top and serving more in a small bowl separately.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Vegetable and Tofu Stir Fry in Oyster Sauce
author: Joy
metadescription: A quick and easy meat - free stir fry!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 pack {include file='cms_template:Changs-Long-Life-Noodles'}
  • 2 tbsp. neutral oil
  • 1 clove garlic, crushed
  • 4 spring onions, white and pale green parts only, cut into 3-4 cm lengths
  • 1 small head broccoli, florets removed and stem discarded
  • 2 medium bok choy, quartered
  • ½ small carrot, thinly sliced
  • 1 bunch asparagus, cut into 3-4 cm lengths
  • 3-4 shitake mushrooms, halved or quartered
  • ½ cup vegetable stock or water
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce'}
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce'}
  • 1 bunch flowering garlic chives or Chinese chives - optional
  • 1 tsp. sugar
  • 100g firm tofu, cut into 1-2 cm cubes
method:
Method:

  1. Cook the noodles as per instructions then drain and reserve.
  2. Heat the oil in a wok and add the garlic, spring onion and mushrooms, cooking for just 30 seconds so the garlic doesn’t burn.
  3. Add the broccoli, bok choy carrot and asparagus and stir fry for another 30 seconds. Add the stock or water and place a lid on the wok for one minute. Remove the lid and add the soy and oyster sauces and the sugar plus the chives if using, stirring to combine and to dissolve the sugar. Add the tofu and gently fold through to heat it without breaking the pieces up.
  4. Place the noodles in a shallow serving dish and spoon the vegetable mixture and sauce over the top.
  5. For a gluten free alternative, swap the Chang’s Life Long Noodles for the Rice Vermicelli, and select an unflavoured tofu brand.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Variation - Gluten Free

title: Twice Cooked King Prawns
author: Joy
metadescription: These twice cooked prawns make a great entrée or light midweek meal.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 12 large green king prawns, shells left on but digestive tract removed
  • 1 ½ cups vegetable or other neutral oil
  • 2 tsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 50ml Shaoxing rice wine
  • 2 tsp. caster sugar
  • ¼ cup finely chopped spring onion
  • ½ cup finely chopped coriander
  • ½ medium red chilli, finely sliced – optional
  • 1 pack of {include file='cms_template:Changs-Vermicelli-Rice-Noodles'}
method:
Method:

  1. Combine the sauces, Shaoxing wine and sugar in a bowl and stir to combine. Boil water in a saucepan to cook the noodles.
  2. Heat the oil in a wok and when hot, (carefully) add the prawns and cook until the shells turn red- approx one-to-two minutes. Remove with tongs or a slotted spoon and drain on paper towel.
  3. Add noodles to boiling water. When cooled, drain and reserve.
  4. Reduce heat to medium and pour off all but one tablespoon of the oil. Add the sauce mixture and prawns and stir fry for 30 – 60 seconds. Serve with any sauce poured over vermicelli noodles and sprinkled with spring onion, coriander and chilli (if using).
  5. You can simplify this dish and maintaining the flavours by simply stir frying peeled green king prawns in one tablespoon of oil, then adding the sauce mixture and finishing with spring onions, coriander and chilli.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Summer Tomato and Crispy Noodle Salad
author: Joy
metadescription: The perfect side salad at any BBQ
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 500g mixed, different types and coloured tomatoes, cut in different sizes and shapes
  • 2 spring onions, white and pale green parts only, sliced
  • ½ cup coriander leaves, roughly chopped
  • 1 pack Chang’s Original Crispy Noodles
  • ½ cup Chang’s Asian Salad Dressing
  • ¼ cup olive oil
method:
Method:

  1. Mix the Chang’s Asian Salad Dressing and oil in a salad bowl then add the tomatoes, spring onions, and coriander, stirring well to coat. Just before serving stir through the Chang’s Original Fried Noodles.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Stir Fried Prawns and Vegetables with Hoisin
author: Joy
metadescription: Stir Fried Prawns and Vegetables with Hoisin
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 12 medium green king prawns, peeled with tails left on, digestive tract removed and prawn butterflied
  • 150ml Chang’s Hoisin Sauce
  • 3 tbsp. neutral oil
  • 1 clove garlic, crushed
  • 1 tsp. grated ginger
  • 2 spring onions, white and pale green parts only cut into 1 cm slices
  • ½ stalk/rib celery, cut into 1 cm slices
  • ½ small red capsicum, cut into 2cm pieces
  • ½ small green capsicum, cut into 2 cm pieces
  • 8 ears baby corn
method:
Method:

  1. Place the prawns in a bowl with 100ml of the Chang’s Hoisin Sauce and marinate, covered in the fridge for 30 minutes.
  2. Remove the prawns from the fridge and drain off marinade.
  3. Heat two tablespoons of oil in a wok until very hot and quickly stir fry the prawns until three quarters cooked through. Remove to a bowl.
  4. Add the remaining oil to the wok and add the garlic and ginger, cooking for just a few seconds before adding the other vegetables. Stir fry for only a minute or two so the vegetables are hot but still firm. Return the prawns to the wok with the remaining 50ml of Chang’s Hoisin Sauce, tossing to coat the prawns and vegetables.
  5. Serve with steamed rice. Serves four as part of an Asian banquet.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Stir Fried Noodles with Chinese Sausage and Pippies
author: Joy
