metadescription: Stir Fried Noodles with Chinese Sausage and Pippies. An unusual but easy noodle recipe.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 4 tbsp. vegetable or other neutral oil
  • ½ bunch Garlic chives, cut into 4 cm lengths - optional
  • 2 Lup cheong sausages, thinly sliced
  • 2 Spring onions, sliced
  • 2 cloves garlic, chopped
  • ½ thumb of ginger, peeled and grated, or finely sliced
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 500g fresh pippies or clams
  • 1 packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Long-Life-Noodles'}
method:
Method:

  1. Soften noodles in warm water and drain well. Heat half the oil in a small frying pan or wok and fry the noodles until golden brown, turning in a single piece one. Alternatively, reserve the noodles and stir fry through the dish.
  2. Place a half cup of water in a large saucepan with a lid and bring to the boil. Add the clams and place lid on, cooking for just a few minutes until the clams begin to open.
  3. At the same time, heat a wok and add the remaining oil. Add the garlic, ginger, lup cheong sausage and spring onions and cook until sausage begins to colour.
  4. Drain the clams, reserving to liquid they have produced in the saucepan.
  5. Add the clams, garlic and chives if using, Light soy and Oyster sauces and ½ cup of the clam liquid and stir gently to combine. Either spoon the mixture over the fried noodle cake or stir through the reserved noodles if preparing that way.
  6. For a gluten free alternative, replace the Long Life Noodles with rice or Chang’s Thai-Style Rice Noodles.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free variation

title: Sticky Pork Ribs
author: Joy
metadescription: Sticky Pork Ribs
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 kg meaty pork spare ribs
  • 2 cups water
  • 1 thumb of ginger, sliced
  • 1 150 ml bottle of Chang’s Japanese Teriyaki Sauce or Chang’s Hoisin Sauce
  • 3 gloves garlic, finely chopped
  • 2 tbsp. chopped ginger
  • 2 tbsp. brown sugar
  • A few very thin slices or matchsticks of ginger, as garnish
  • 2 spring onions, sliced diagonally, as garnish
method:
Method:

  1. Cut ribs into 6cm pieces and place in a large saucepan, cover with 2 cups of water and sliced ginger.
  2. Bring water to simmering point and simmer for 15 mins. Drain and rinse under cold water, discarding the ginger.
  3. Put ribs in a large bowl, combine Chang’s Japanese Teriyaki Sauce, garlic, ginger and sugar and pour mixture over ribs to cover. Place the ribs in a baking dish, add ½ cup of water and bake at 180 degrees for 40 mins.
  4. Serve hot sprinkled with ginger and spring onions, and freshly steamed rice.

Tip: Ribs can be marinated in the sauce overnight (covered, in the fridge) before simmering for a more intense flavour.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Spaghetti Bolognese, Chang’s Style
author: Joy
metadescription: Spaghetti Bolognese, Chang’s Style
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 2 tbsp. neutral oil
  • 1 onion, finely chopped
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 tsp. grated ginger
  • 1 small carrot, peeled and finely chopped
  • 500g pork mince
  • ½ cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 cans diced tomatoes
  • 1 tbsp. tomato paste
  • ½ cup chopped coriander
  • 2 packets {include file='cms_template:Changs-Long-Life-Noodles'} (or your choice of {include file='cms_template:Changs-Egg-Noodles'}, {include file='cms_template:Wok-Ready-Noodles'}, {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Wide-Noodles'}).
method:
Method:

  1. Pour the oil into a saucepan or deep sided frying pan and soften the onion, garlic, ginger and carrot over medium heat without browning. Add the pork and cook until the meat changes colour, stirring frequently.
  2. Add the hoisin, light soy sauce, diced tomatoes, tomato paste and coriander and stir well to combine. Bring to the boil then gently simmer for at least 45 minutes and up to 2 hours.
  3. Cook noodles as per instructions on pack and drain before placing in a large bowl. Pour sauce over noodles and serve immediately.
  4. For a gluten free alternative, use Chang’s Gluten Free Wok Ready Noodle or Chang’s Super Lo Cal Noodle.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Steak with Mushroom Sauce
author: Joy
metadescription: This easy Asian twist on steak with mushroom sauce is always a weeknight pleaser
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 4x 200-250g steaks, sirloin, fillet or scotch fillet
  • 2 tbsp. butter
  • 2 tbsp. olive oil
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed or finely chopped
  • 2 spring onions, finely sliced
  • 1 punnet fresh shitake mushrooms
  • 3 or 4 Portobello or field mushrooms
  • 2 tbsp. Chang’s Oyster Sauce
  • 2 tbsp. Chang’s Hoisin Sauce
method:
Method:

  1. Bring the steaks back to room temperature and season well with salt and pepper.
  2. Heat a large frying pan and add one tablespoon of the oil. Cook the steaks over high heat to your desired level of doneness, turning once.
  3. Place in a shallow dish and loosely cover with foil or a clean tea towel during the preparation of the sauce.
  4. Add the remaining oil and butter to the pan and when the butter melts add the garlic and the mushrooms.
  5. Cook over a high heat until the mushrooms begin to brown and add the spring onions, cooking for a few seconds before adding the oyster and hoisin sauces and two tablespoons of water.
  6. Place steaks on four plates and add any juices from the resting process to the sauce. Spoon sauce over the steak and serve with Asian greens.
allergen: Nut Free, Gluten Free

title: Spicy Asian Osso Buco
author: Joy
metadescription: A warming winter Osso Buco, with an Asian flair
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 4 thick (3-4 cm) slices meaty veal shin, more if they are on the small side
  • 2 tbsp. neutral oil
  • 1 thumb ginger, peeled and finely sliced
  • ½ cup Shaosing rice wine
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsp. brown sugar
  • 2 tbsp. rice wine vinegar
  • 1 tsp (more to taste) chilli bean paste or sauce/ chilli sauce
  • 6 star anise
  • 1 tsp Sichuan peppercorns, finely ground in mortar and pestle
  • 2 pieces dried tangerine or orange peel
method:
Method:

  1. Brown the meat well on both sides in the oil in a hot pan and transfer to a deep baking dish just big enough to fit the meat.
  2. Add all other ingredients and top with enough water to just cover the meat. Cover with foil or the lid of the dish. Place in a pre-heated 150 degree oven for two hours, until falling off the bone tender.
  3. Serve with steamed Asian greens like bok choy and steamed rice.
  4. Serves 4 as a small main course or generously as part of an Asian banquet.
allergen: Gluten Free, Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Smoked Trout and Snow Pea Noodle Salad
author: Joy
metadescription: A quick an easy smoked trout salad with snow peas and noodles
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 400g smoked trout, all bones removed and flesh flaked into large pieces
  • 16 snow peas, trimmed and blanched in boiling water then refreshed in iced water and drained
  • 2 spring onions, sliced
  • 8 cherry or grape tomatoes, halved
  • 2 packets of {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Noodles'}, rinsed under cold water and drained
  • ½ bunch coriander, leaves only

Dressing:

  • 1 clove garlic, grated
  • 2 tbsp. neutral oil
  • 1 tbsp. mirin
  • 1 tbsp. rice vinegar
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • *Alternatively use 2/3 cup {include file='cms_template:Crispy-Noodle-Salad-Dressing'}
method:
Method:

  1. Gently toss all salad ingredients in a large bowl with enough of the dressing to thoroughly moisten all the ingredients.
  2. Transfer to one large or four small serving plates.
  3. If not using Chang’s Oriental Salad Dressing, combine all dressing ingredients well and reserve.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: San Choy Bow
author: Joy
metadescription: This quick and easy San Choy Bow makes a delicious entrée or light midweek meal.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 tbsp. neutral oil
  • 200g beef mince
  • 1 clove garlic, crushed
  • ½ onion finely chopped
  • 2 tbsp. finely chopped spring onions
  • 2 tbsp. finely chopped bamboo shoots
  • 6 green beans cut in 5mm pieces
  • 2 tbsp. Chang’s Oyster Sauce
  • 4 iceberg lettuce leaves, trimmed into a circular bowl shape-refrigerated
method:
Method:

  1. Prepare and heat oil in a wok. Add the beef, garlic and onion, cooking until the beef turns grey and the onion softens, stirring frequently.
  2. Add the beans and bamboo shoots and stir for 30 seconds before adding the Chang’s Oyster Sauce and simmering for one minute.
  3. Allow to cool slightly and spoon into chilled lettuce cups
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Salmon and Ginger Fishcakes With Cucumber Relish
author: Joy
metadescription: These fresh fishcakes served with a cucumber relish is the perfect start to a family meal.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 500g boneless, skinless salmon or ocean trout
  • 80g Panko crumbs or dried fresh breadcrumbs
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 egg, beaten
  • 4 spring onions, white and pale green parts only, finely sliced (reserve dark green tops for garnish)
  • 1 tbsp. finely grated ginger
  • Finely grated zest of 1 lime or 2 kaffir lime leaves, very thinly sliced
  • Neutral oil for frying

Relish:

  • 4 tbsp. rice vinegar + 4 tbsp. water
  • 30g or 2 tbsp. sugar
  • ½ thumb ginger, peeled and cut into very fine matchsticks
  • 1 small cucumber, seeds removed and cut into matchsticks
  • 2 red shallots, cut into matchsticks
  • ½ cup coriander leaves
method:
Method:

  1. To make relish, heat the vinegar, water and sugar in a small saucepan or in microwave until sugar dissolves. Allow to cool then combine with ginger, cucumber, shallots and coriander and refrigerate, covered, until ready to use.
  2. Combine all fishcake ingredients in a bowl and shape into rissoles or meatballs. Ideally refrigerate for 20-30 mins for them to bind, then shallow fry in the oil over medium heat, turning occasionally.
  3. Drain on paper towel and serve with relish.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Roast Chicken Pancakes
author: Joy
metadescription: Inspired by the Peking Duck Pancake, this Roast Chicken Pancake is the perfect entrée or start to a banquet.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 2 skin-on chicken breasts, ideally still on the bone
  • 2 tbsp. honey
  • 1 tsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 12-16 Chinese pancakes (sold frozen in Asian food stores), warmed in a steamer or microwave
  • 4 spring onions, white and pale green parts only halved, then halved lengthways
  • 1 small cucumber, peeled, seeded and cut into 4cm lengths
  • 1/2 cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
method:
Method:

  1. Melt honey with sesame oil and light soy sauce in a small saucepan or microwave and brush three quarters of the marinade on chicken skin and over chicken meat. Place skin side up on a rack in a 210 degree oven for ten minutes then brush remaining marinade on skin and bake for another ten minutes.
  2. Remove chicken from oven and allow to rest for five minutes before carving thick slices on the diagonal.
  3. Arrange two or three pancakes on each plate and spread about a teaspoon of hoisin down one side of each.
  4. Place a piece of cucumber and spring onion on top and slice a piece of chicken skin and meat about 8-10 cm x 3-4cm on top. Fold the pancake in half and serve.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Quick Ramen Style Noodle Soup
author: Joy
metadescription: Delicious pork and tofu Chinese noodle soup
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1litre best quality chicken stock
  • ½ cup sake
  • ¼ cup Chang's Soy Sauce Light
  • 200g cold roast pork- rolled shoulder, loin, leg or fillet- thinly sliced
  • 2 boiled eggs, peeled and halved
  • 250g Chang's Long Life Noodles or Chang's Organic Ramen Noodles
  • 100g bamboo shoots, thinly sliced
  • 4 fresh shitake mushrooms, sliced or quartered
  • ½ sheet nori, thinly sliced
  • 2 spring onions, sliced diagonally
method:
Method:

  1. Place chicken stock, sake, Chang’s Light Soy Sauce, bamboo shoots and mushrooms in a saucepan and bring to the boil, then simmer for a few minutes to incorporate flavours and gently cook the mushrooms.
  2. While the soup is simmering, cook the Chang’s Long Life Noodles as per instructions on the pack and divide between four soup bowls.
  3. Ladle soup over the noodles and arrange sliced pork, eggs, nori strips and spring onions on top of each bowl.
  4. Enjoy!
  5. You can also use Chang’s Egg Noodle, Rice Vermicelli or Super Lo Cal Noodle.

Note: you can substitute the fresh shitake mushrooms with dried, but soak them in very hot or boiling water for 20 minutes then discard stems
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Pork Belly Hotpot With Okra
author: Jackie
metadescription: This warming pork belly hotpot is a must-try this winter!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 600g lean pork belly, cut into 1cm x 4 cm approx. pieces
  • 200 ml chicken stock
  • 1 cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • Juice and finely grated zest of an orange
  • ½ thumb ginger, thinly sliced
  • 2 cloves garlic, thinly sliced
  • 1 fresh or 3dried chillies, thinly sliced
  • 4 Star anise
  • 1 stick Cinnamon
  • 4 Cloves
  • 2tbsp. honey
  • 12 Okra
method:
Method:

  1. Place pork belly in a pot of cold water and bring to the boil then simmer for five minutes. Discard water and any grey scum that formed on top of the water and place all ingredients except the okra in a hot pot with a lid and bake in a 180 degree oven for one hour then add the okra and bake for another 30 minutes.
  2. Note, that you can substitute the star anise, cinnamon and cloves for one heaped teaspoon of five-spice powder. You can also substitute the pork belly for chicken if preferred.
  3. Serve with rice and steamed green vegetables.
allergen: Nut Free, Diary Free, Gluten Free

title: Miso Marinated Tuna
author: Jackie
metadescription: Tuna steaks marinated with miso and served with Chang’s Super Lo Cal Noodles
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 4 x Tuna steaks (or make kebabs with chunks on soaked wooden skewers)
  • 1 tsp. white sesame seeds
  • 1 tsp. black sesame seeds
  • 2 bunches spring onions, white and pale green parts only
  • ½ cup neutral oil
  • 1 packet {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Changs-Wide-Potato-Glass-Noodle'}

Marinade:

  • ½ cup white miso paste
  • 2 tbsp.mirin
  • 2tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • ½ thumb ginger, grated
  • 1 tsp.{include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1 tbsp. brown sugar
  • ¼ cup water
method:
Method:

  1. Combine all marinade ingredients in a shallow dish and place tuna in a single layer turning to coat completely.
  2. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for four hours.
  3. Bring back to room temperature before cooking.Prepare noodles as per instructions on pack. Reserve.
  4. Brush the char grill with oil and grill the tuna and the spring onions, cooking the tuna no more than medium rare.
  5. Arrange the noodles, spring onions and tuna on four plates and sprinkle the sesame seeds over the fish.
allergen: Gluten Free, Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Minute Steak Mixed Mushrooms
author: Jackie
metadescription: Quick and tasty minute steak mixed mushrooms with Hoisin
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 4x 100-150 gram sirloin steaks, excess fat trimmed off
  • 150ml Chang’s Hoisin Sauce
  • 4 tbsp. neutral oil
  • 1 clove garlic, crushed
  • 1 spring onion, finely sliced
  • 200g mixed Asian mushrooms, all cut into bite sized pieces
method:
Method:

  1. Begin by marinating the steaks in 100ml of the Chang’s Hoisin for 30 minutes.Remove the meat from the marinade.
  2. Heat a grill plate, BBQ or large frying pan, and brush with half the oil. Quickly grill the steak on both sides to your preferred level of doneness, turning only once.
  3. Rest meat for a few minutes on a plate and loosely cover with foil or a clean tea towel during the preparation of the mushrooms.
  4. Heat a wok, or large frying pan, and add the oil. When hot, add the mushrooms, garlic and spring onion, tossing only occasionally so the mushrooms brown. When nearly cooked, add the remaining Chang’s Hoisin Sauce and toss to coat.
  5. Serve the steaks with a generous spoonful of the mushrooms and some Chang’s Original Fried Noodles, Wok Ready Gluten Free or Super Lo Cal Noodles twirled around a fork.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free - variation

title: Quick Pan-Fried Fish with a Fresh Herb Salad
author: Joy
metadescription: A quick and easy marinated, pan fried fish served with a fresh herb salad
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 4 x 160-200 gram while fish fillets, bones removed
  • ½ cup Chang’s Oyster Sauce
  • 2 tbsp. plain flour
  • 2 spring onions, white and pale green parts only, thinly sliced
  • 1 medium red chilli, seeds removed, thinly sliced lengthways
  • 1 cup coriander leaves
  • 1 cup basil or Thai basil leaves
  • ¼ cup sprigs of dill
  • ½ medium red chilli, cut into long fine strips - optional
  • 2 tbsp. fried shallots - optional
  • 2 tsp. extra virgin olive oil
  • 1 tsp. Chang’s Sesame oil
  • 1 tsp. Chang’s Light Soy Sauce
  • 1 tsp. rice vinegar
  • Lime, quartered, to serve
method:
Method:

  1. Marinate the fish for 20 minutes up to 12 hours in the Chang’s Oyster Sauce.
  2. Mix the olive oil, sesame oil, light soy sauce and rice vinegar in a bowl and add the coriander, basil and dill, tossing to coat.
  3. Wipe off excess oyster sauce from the fish and dust lightly in flour. Then fry the fish in the neutral oil in a frying pan over medium heat until just cooked.
  4. Place a piece of fish on each of four plates and top with the herb salad and the chilli and/or fried shallots if using. Place lime quarter beside the fish.
  5. For the gluten free, swap the plain flour for a gluten free variety.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free Alternative

title: Marinated Ling and Salmon Skewers
author: Jackie
metadescription: These ling and salmon skewers are a quick and easy weeknight meal.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 400g thick ling fillet (or a thick, firm white fish), skin and bones removed, cut into 3-4cm pieces
  • 400g salmon fillet, skin and bones removed, cut into 3-4cm pieces
  • 150 ml Chang’s Oriental Salad Dressing
  • 150g bag salad leaves
  • 2 tbl olive oil
  • Pre-soaked wooden skewers
method:
Method:

  1. Alternate the pieces of fish on the pre-soaked wooden or small metal skewers. Season with just a little sea salt and black pepper.
  2. Cook over medium/high heat on a lightly oiled flat grill, brushing 100ml of the Chang’s Oriental Salad Dressing.
  3. Toss the salad leaves with the remaining 50ml Oriental Salad Dressing and and the oil. Serve the salad with the grilled fish skewers.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Ma Po Bean Curd
author: Jackie
metadescription: Delicious pork and tofu Chinese stew
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 250g lean pork mince
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed
  • ½ thumb ginger, peeled and grated
  • 1 tbsp.Shaosing rice wine
  • 1 tbsp.neutral oil
  • 3 spring onions, finely sliced, dark green tops reserved
  • 1 tbsp.chilli bean sauce
  • 1 cup Asian (plain) chicken stock
  • 1 tbsp.Chang’s Oyster Sauce
  • 1 tbsp.Chang’s Hoisin sauce
  • 1 tbsp.cornflour, dissolved in half a cup of cold water
  • 3-400g firm bean curd (tofu), cut into1cm cubes
  • ½ - 1 medium red chilli, finely sliced
method:
Method:

  1. Marinate the pork with the garlic, ginger and rice wine for about an hour.
  2. Heat the oil in a wok and add the pork, stirring occasionally until it has nearly all changed colour. Add the spring onions and sliced chilli and cook for another thirty seconds.
  3. Add the chilli bean sauce, Chang’s Oyster and Hoisin sauces and stir well to incorporate. Add the chicken stock and cook for a minute, stirring occasionally. Add the cornflour and stir until the sauce boils and thickens. Gently fold through the tofu until hot and transfer to a serving plate. Top with the sliced dark green spring onion and serve with boiled rice.
  4. Serves four as part of a Chinese meal/banquet.
  5. Gluten Free dependant upon the brand of chicken stock used.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Korean Beef Chap Chae
author: Jackie
metadescription: Korean Beef Chap Chae served with bean thread noodles.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 500g beef-rump, sirloin or topside, fat removed, thinly sliced, or best quality beef mince
  • 100g or half a packet of {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Wide-Noodles'}
  • 2 tbsp. neutral oil
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 bird’s eye chilli, seeds removed, finely sliced
  • 6 button mushrooms, halved or quartered
  • ½ carrot cut into thin diagonal slices
  • 1 small onion, sliced
  • 2 bok choy, quartered
  • 3 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. rice vinegar
  • 1 tbsp. sugar
  • 1 tsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}

Marinade:

  • 1 tsp. cornflour
  • 1 tsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1 tbsp. chilli sauce
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 tbsp. grated ginger
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
method:
Method:

  1. Marinate the beef in the cornflour, sesame oil, chilli sauce, garlic, ginger and soy for 2-4 hours, covered in the fridge.
  2. Rinse the noodles under cold water, and drain.
  3. Heat one tablespoon of oil in a wok and sear the meat quickly. Remove from the wok and reserve. Add the remaining oil and the vegetables, cooking for a minute. Add the noodles and cook for one minute more before adding the beef, soy, sugar, vinegar and sesame oil.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Japanese-style Pork Potato Salad
author: Jackie
metadescription: Japanese style pork schnitzel served with a creamy potato salad.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 4 Asian cut pork cutlets, bone removed or 1 thick pork fillet, cut into 1 cm slices and
  • slightly flattened
  • 1 cup plain flour
  • 1-2 eggs, beaten with a little cold water
  • 2 cups panko crumbs
  • 200ml Chang’s Hoisin sauce
  • 2 tbsp. white sesame seeds

Potato Salad:

  • 1 large Desiree potato, peeled and cut in 1cm dice
  • 1 small cucumber, peeled (optional), seeds removed and cut into 1cm dice
  • ½ small red onion, very finely sliced
  • ½ cup mayonnaise
  • Pinch salt
  • 1 tbsp.mirin
method:
Method:

  1. Bring a pot of salted water to the boil.Add potatoes and carrots, continuing to cook until they are al dente.
  2. Add the cucumber and onion to the boiling water, and continue to cook for 30 seconds more.
  3. Drain and rinse vegetables under cold water, then set aside to cool.Mix through mayonnaise, mirin and salt and reserve.
  4. Next, crumb the pork by placing each slice in the flour, then egg, then panko crumbs and shallow fry in oil until golden brown. Once done, drain pork on paper towel.
  5. To finish, pour Chang’s Hoisin sauce over pork, before sprinkling with a few sesame seeds.
  6. Serve pork with the potato salad or very finely shredded cabbage and a lemon wedge.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Homemade Chicken Nuggets
author: Jackie
metadescription: Hungry kids? These homemade chicken nuggets are a real pleaser.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 500 grams boneless, skinless chicken breast fillets
  • 1 cup plain flour
  • 1 tsp. pouring salt
  • 1 large egg, beaten with a teaspoon of cold water
  • 2 cups panko crumbs
  • 2 cups neutral oil for frying
  • 200ml Chang’s Hoisin sauce
  • 1 large carrot, cut into matchsticks
  • 1 tsp. sugar
method:
Method:

  1. Place the chopped carrot in a small bowl and cover with cold water. Sprinkle over sugar then stir and allow to sit for 20 minutes.
  2. Cut the chicken into 3-4 cm pieces.
  3. Place the flour and salt in one bowl, the beaten egg in a bowl beside it and the breadcrumbs in a third bowl. Sprinkle a tablespoon or so of crumbs on a plate beside the breadcrumbs.
  4. Place a piece of chicken in the flour and coat completely then dust off excess before dipping into the egg and coating completely. Place egg coated chicken into crumbs and coating thoroughly, pressing the chicken into the crumbs to get as much as possible to adhere.
  5. Place the crumbed chicken on the plate then repeat the process to use up all the chicken.
  6. Heat the oil in a frying pan to medium heat (so the chicken will cook through without burning the crumbs) and cook the chicken until golden brown, turning once.
  7. Drain on paper towel.
  8. Drain the carrot and arrange on four small plates with the chicken nuggets piled on top and the Chang’s Hoisin sauce squirted over.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Hainan Chicken and Rice
author: Jackie
metadescription: Hainan Chicken and Rice
ingredients:
Ingredients:

Chicken and stock:

  • 1 whole small organic or free range chicken, trimmed of excess fat
  • 1 tbsp.{include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tablespoon Shaosing rice wine
  • ½ kg chicken carcasses
  • 1 large thumb ginger, sliced
  • 1 clove garlic, bruised
  • 2 red shallots, roughly chopped

Rice:

  • 1 tbsp.neutral oil
  • 3 cloves garlic, grated
  • 1 thumb ginger grated
  • 1 ½ cups long grain rice
  • 1 tsp. salt
  • 2 ½ cups stock from cooking chicken

Ginger sauce:

  • 2 spring onions, white and pale green parts only, very finely sliced
  • 1 thumb ginger, grated
  • 1/3 cup neutral oil
  • 2 tablespoons {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • Diced or sliced cucumber to serve
method:
Method:

  1. Pat the chicken dry with paper towel and rub the Shaosing and soy sauce into the skin.
  2. Place in a large pot with chicken carcases, ginger, garlic and shallots and approx. 4 litres cold water.
  3. Bring water to the boil, skimming any foam that forms on top. As soon as water has boiled, place a lid on the pot and turn off heat.
  4. After 30 mins turn heat back on and again bring to the point of boiling, again skimming off any impurities. Turn off the heat again and again place the lid on the pot and leave to stand for 30 minutes.Remove chicken from stock and place in a bowl, covered with foil to keep warm.
  5. Boil the stock for another 20 minutes, then strain through a sieve, then through muslin.
  6. Wash the rice in a rice cooker bowl and drain well.
  7. Heat the oil in a small pan and add the garlic and ginger, cooking until fragrant, not browned and add to the rice, stirring to incorporate then add the salt and stock. Cook the rice as normal until all the stock is absorbed.
  8. While the rice is cooking, place the spring onions and ginger for the sauce in a small serving bowl and heat the oil in a small saucepan until smoking. Pour the hot oil over the ginger and spring onions then stir through the oyster sauce.
  9. Chop the chicken with a cleaver and serve with the rice, cucumber and bowl of ginger sauce.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Five Spice Lamb Fillet and Eggplant Tortillas
author: Jackie
metadescription: Five Spice Lamb Fillet and Eggplant Tortillas
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 8 lamb fillets
  • 8 small wooden skewers, soaked in cold water for at least an hour
  • 1 tsp. heaped Chinese five spice powder
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed or finely grated
  • 1 tbsp. vegetable or neutral oil
  • 1 tbsp.Shaoxing rice wine
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • I large eggplant, cut into a 1 cm dice
  • 1 tbsp.salt
  • ½ cup vegetable or other neutral oil
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed or chopped
  • ½ medium chilli, chopped or ½ teaspoon dried chilli flakes
  • 2 tbsp.{include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • ¼ cup finely chopped coriander
  • 1 tbsp.rice vinegar
  • 1 medium cucumber, cut in 8-12 pieces, lengthways
  • 8 medium tortillas, wraps or Chinese pancakes
method:
Method:

  1. Marinate the lamb in the five spice powder, garlic, vegetable oil, Shaoxing and light soy sauce for 1-4 hours, covered in the refrigerator. Thread the lamb fillets onto the wooden skewers so they won’t curl up when cooking.
  2. Place the eggplant in a colander and sprinkle with the salt. Allow moisture to drain out for 20 minutes then rinse under cold water and dry well with paper towel. Heat the oil in a wok and fry the eggplant until lightly golden, adding a little more oil if necessary.
  3. Brush a BBQ plate or char grill plate with a little oil and cook the lamb to your liking over high heat. Sprinkle with a little sea salt and serve with the tortillas, eggplant and cucumber for guests to assemble.
  4. For gluten free alternative, use corn tortillas or lettuce leaves.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Variant – Gluten Free

title: Pork Hoisin Meatballs
author: Joy
metadescription: Asian-inspired hoisin pork meatballs served with Chang’s Super Lo-Cal Noodles.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 750g pork mince
  • 2 egg yolks
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 cup panko dried breadcrumbs (Japanese bread crumbs)
  • Finely grated zest of half a lemon
  • 1 tsp. five spice powder
  • ½ tsp. freshly ground black pepper
  • ½ cup neutral oil
  • 2 packs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Wide-Noodles'}
method:
Method:

  1. Combine pork, egg yolks, breadcrumbs, Chang’s Hoisin sauce, light soy, lemon zest, five spice powder and pepper in a bowl and mix very thoroughly with your hands.
  2. Either roll into walnut size meatballs or shape into rissoles if you prefer.
  3. Cover the flat grill on your BBQ with the oil and cook the meatballs over medium heat, not high-so they cook through without burning.
  4. Rinse the noodles thoroughly under cold water then place in a bowl of very hot water for a minute to warm. Place the noodles in a shallow bowl and arrange the meatballs on top.
  5. Serve with a little more Chang’s Hoisin sauce to dip the meatballs in or drizzle some over the top of them.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Easy Sukiyaki with Lo-Cal Noodles
author: Jackie
metadescription: The perfect beef hot pot to warm you up this
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 4 cups good quality light beef stock
  • 4 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 4 packets {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Wide-Noodles'}
  • 4 button mushrooms, thinly sliced
  • 2 cups Chinese cabbage, cut into strips
  • 100g very thinly sliced beef fillet or sirloin, trimmed of all fat
  • 200g firm tofu or grilled tofu (yaki tofu is available from Asian supermarkets)
  • 2 spring onions, finely sliced
method:
Method:

  1. Heat the stock, Chang’s Light Soy sauce, sugar and Chang’s Super Lo-Cal Noodles in a pot and heat, stirring occasionally until the sugar melts. Bring to the boil then reduce to a simmer for 5 minutes.
  2. Divide all other ingredients between four bowls and pour over simmering stock. Allow to stand for a couple of minutes so soften the vegetables and cook the meat before eating.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Dree

title: Dry Beef Rice Noodles
author: Jackie
metadescription: Flavoursome Dry Beef Rice Noodles
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 200g beef fillet, rump, sirloin or topside, cut in thin slices across the grain
  • 3 tbsp. neutral oil
  • 250g {include file='cms_template:Changs-Thai-Style-Rice-Noodles'}
  • 2 small choy sum, cut into individual leaves, tops trimmed
  • 1 cup bean sprouts
  • 2 cloves garlic, finely chopped
  • 2 spring onions, cut into 2 cm lengths
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Soy-Sauce-Dark'}
  • ½ tsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • 1 tsp. sesame seeds, toasted if preferred

Marinade:

  • ½ tsp. salt
  • ½ tsp. sugar
  • 1 tsp. grated ginger
  • 1 tsp. cornflour
  • 1 tsp. light soy sauce
method:
Method:

  1. Combine marinade ingredients and mix well with the meat. Marinate covered in the fridge for 2-12 hours. When ready, remove marinated beef from fridge.
  2. Put the noodles into a bowl and pour boiling water on top, allowingthem to soak for ten minutes. Drain just before using.
  3. Heat the oil to very hot in a large wok and cook the meat until it is golden brown. Remove to a bowl, leaving oil to quickly stir fry the garlic, spring onions and choy sum, until the choy sum start to soften. Add the noodles and bean sprouts and heat through.Return the beef to the wok and stir through the two soy sauces until they are absorbed. Transfer to a serving plate and sprinkle over sesames seeds.

Notes:

  1. Instead of swimming in sauce, this dish comes out of the wok virtually ‘dry’ or sauce free with no added cornflour so the noodles soak up all the delicious flavours.
allergen: Gluten Free, Dairy Free, Nut Free

title: Chinese Chicken Skewers
author: Jackie
metadescription: Marinated chicken skewers, best enjoyed straight from the BBQ
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 6 boneless chicken thighs, trimmed of excess fat and cut into 2-3cm pieces
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed
  • 1 thumb ginger, peeled and grated
  • 3 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. tomato ketchup
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 red capsicum, cut into 2cm pieces
  • 1 green capsicum, cut into 2cm pieces
method:
Method:

  1. Combine all ingredients except spring onions in a bowl and marinate covered in the fridge for 2-4 hours.
  2. Thread the chicken and capsicum onto pre-soaked marinated skewers and cook over low/medium heat on the char grill until just cooked through.
  3. Serve with steamed rice and Chinese greens.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Dree

title: Chicken Yakitori Skewers
author: Jackie
metadescription: Delicious Yakitori Chicken Skewers, perfect for the BBQ
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 400g boneless, skinless chicken thighs, trimmed of excess fat
  • 4-6 spring onions, white and pale green parts only, cut into 2-3 cm lengths
  • 8-12 bamboo skewers, soaked in water for at least an hour
  • 200ml Chang’s Yakitori Sauce
  • 1 tbsp. neutral oil
method:
Method:

  1. Cut chicken into 3cm pieces and place in a bowl with half the Chang’s Yakitori Sauce covered in the fridge for 2-4 hours.
  2. Thread the meat onto skewers, alternating with the spring onion pieces and cook over coal (ideally) or on BBQ grill plate, brushed with the neutral oil, or under the grill of your oven until just cooked through, brushing with the other half of the sauce.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Dree

title: Chicken and Celery Spring Rolls
author: Jackie
metadescription: These spring rolls make the perfect entrée or start to your own Asian banquet
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 packet frozen spring roll wrappers, thawed - or you could use rice paper
  • 1 dessertspoon neutral oil
  • 250g chicken mince
  • 1 tbl {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 3 tbsp. chopped bamboo shoots
  • 1 stalk celery, finely sliced/chopped
method:
Method:

  1. Heat a wok or frying pan and add the oil, then the chicken mince, working to break up any clumps of meat that form. Add the sauces when nearly cooked and remove from the heat before stirring through the celery and bamboo shoots (so they stay crunchy).
  2. Place a spoonful of the mixture in the middle of a sheet of spring roll wrapper and roll tightly, sealing the end with water from the end of your finger.
  3. Fry over medium heat only in neutral oil. Drain well and allow to cool just a little before serving.
  4. Serve with Chang’s Dark Soy Sauce, Chang's Gluten Free Tamari Light Soy Sauce with sliced fresh chilli or Chang’s Hoisin Sauce.
  5. Makes about twenty rolls.
  6. For a gluten free alternative, use rice paper instead of spring roll paper.
allergen: Nut Free, Diary Free, Variation - Gluten Free

title: Chicken and Cashew Nut
author: Jackie
metadescription: Chicken Cashew Nut with Oyster sauce, a dish the whole family will enjoy.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 tbsp. vegetable oil
  • 100g cashew nuts
  • 1 onion, chopped
  • 2 spring onions, sliced diagonally
  • 2 cloves garlic, finely chopped
  • 1 cup oyster mushrooms
  • 400g chicken thighs, diced
  • 4 tbl Chang’s Oyster Sauce
  • ¼ cup water
  • 1 packet (100 grams) Chang’s Original Fried Noodles
method:
Method:

  1. Heat a wok over a high heat then add the oil.Add the nuts and cook until lightly golden.Remove from the wok, and drain on a paper towel.
  2. Return the wok to the heat, adding oil if necessary, then add the onion, garlic, spring onions and chicken. Stir fry the dish for three minutes, or until chicken is golden brown.
  3. Add Oyster sauce, and cook until the vegetables are cooked, and the sauce begins to thicken.
  4. Serve with the Chang’s Original Fried Noodles. For a gluten-free alternative, replace the noodles with rice.
allergen: Dairy Free, Gluten Free - variation

title: Black Pepper Lamb and Green Beans
author: Jackie
metadescription: Simple Black Pepper Lamb and Green Beans.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 400g lamb fillet, sliced thinly
  • ½ bottle Chang’s Chinese Black Pepper Sauce
  • 1 handful of green beans, washed, ends removed and chopped in half
  • 2 tbsp. vegetable oil
  • 1 tsp. minced garlic
method:
Method:

  1. Heat oil in a wok over high heat, and stir fry half of the lamb until lightly brown and set aside.
  2. Cook the remaining lamb in the wok, and set aside.
  3. Put the green beans into the wok, and stir fry until colour begins changes to adeep green.
  4. Add the lamb back into the wok, then pour half bottle of Chang’s Black Pepper Sauce.
  5. Stir dish until beans and lamb are tender.
  6. Serve immediately, with rice.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: BBQ Pork Ribs with Hoisin
author: Jackie
metadescription: Melting BBQ Pork Ribs marinated with Hoisin
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1kg meaty pork spare ribs
  • 2 cups water
  • Few slices ginger
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Soy-Sauce-Dark'}
  • 3 cloves garlic, finely chopped
  • 2 tbsp. ginger, finely chopped or grated
  • 2 tbsp. brown sugar
  • ½ cup water for baking
  • 3 shallots diagonally shredded
method:
Method:

  1. Cut ribs into 6 cm pieces and place in a large saucepan, cover with water and sliced ginger. Bring to simmering point and simmer for 15 minutes.
  2. Drain and rinse under cold water. Put ribs in a large bowl.
  3. Combine the Chang's sauces with garlic, ginger and sugar. Pour mixture over ribs. You can cook the ribs immediately, or they can marinate in the hoisin mixture, covered, in the fridge overnight.
  4. Place the ribs in a baking dish, pour ½ cup of water in the bottom of the dish and bake in a preheated moderate oven (180C) for 40 minutes.
  5. Serve hot sprinkled with the shredded shallots and with some steamed rice.

Tips:

  1. Simmering them beforehand reduces the fat and keeps them tender.
  2. Tamari is soy sauce made without using wheat, so it is not only gluten free but also wheat free.
  3. The entire spring onion can be used for cooking. Use the white round part to add flavour. Finely slice the dark green parts and add to stir-fries or use as garnish.
allergen: Gluten Free, Dairy Free, Nut Free,

title: Balinese Pork
author: Jackie
metadescription: Balinese Pork, the perfect winter dish
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 2 tbsp. neutral oil
  • 600g lean pork belly, cut 3cm x 1 cm
  • 2 cloves garlic, sliced
  • 1 large thumb ginger, peeled and julienned (very thin matchsticks)
  • 6 shallots, peeled and sliced
  • Up to a dozen whole bird’s eye chillies, whole, for the brave, or one medium red chilli,
  • sliced, for the sane
  • ½ tsp. freshly ground black pepper
  • 4 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Kecap-Manis-Sweet-Soy-Sauce'}
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 cup chicken stock
method:
Method:

  1. Place the oil in a wok and when hot add the pork stirring occasionally for a few minutes while browning.
  2. Add the garlic, ginger, shallots, chillies and black pepper and stir for another minute to soften then add both soy sauces, stirring and allowing them to coat the pork.
  3. Add the stock and reduce to a simmer for about thirty minutes until pork is tender and nearly all liquid is absorbed. You may need to add more stock or water if the liquid reduces too quickly.
  4. Serve with plenty of steamed rice and green vegetables.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Asian Marinated Pork Chops
author: pwg
metadescription: Asian inspired marinated pork chops
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 4 x 250g meaty pork chops, ideally with some marbling through the meat
  • 4 tbsp. neutral oil
  • 350g fresh Asian stir fry vegetable mix
  • White and pale green part of 2 spring onions, very finely chopped

Marinade:

  • 2 tablespoons {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 teaspoon {include file='cms_template:Changs-Pure-Sesame-Oil'}
  • ½ cup mirin
  • ½ cup {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsp. brown sugar
  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed or grated
  • 1 thumb ginger, grated
method:
Method:

  1. Combine all marinade ingredients in a bowl and add the pork chops, mixing well to coat completely then marinate, covered in the fridge for 4-12 hours.
  2. Bring the pork back to room temperature. Brush a medium-hot flat grill with half the oil and cook until just cooked through. Rest for a couple of minutes loosely covered with foil.
  3. While the pork is resting, place the remaining oil in a hot wok and stir fry the vegetables until cooked – just a couple of minutes.
  4. Transfer to plates the vegetables to plates and top with Chang’s Oyster sauce drizzled over and the pork beside the vegetables
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: BBQ East West Lamb
author: pwg
metadescription: A delicious lamb and bokchoy combination.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 butterflied 2 – 2.5 kilo (before bone removed)leg of lamb, trimmed of excess fat
  • 1 thumb of ginger, thinly sliced
  • 6 cloves garlic
  • Zest and juice of 1 lemon
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsp. brown sugar
  • 2 tbsp. Worcestershire sauce
  • 1 tbsp. dry mustard
  • 1 tbsp. sea salt
  • 2tbsp. Dijon mustard
  • 4 tbsp. neutral oil
  • 3 bunches of bokchoy or your favourite Chinese green vegetables
method:
Method:

  1. Combine all ingredients except the lamb in a food processor and blend to a thin paste. Be sure to use a gluten-free mustard if necessary.
  2. Cover the lamb with the mixture and marinate, covered in the fridge for 4-12 hours.
  3. Bring back to room temperature and cook on the flat grill over medium/high heat for 10-15 minutes either side, basting with any remaining marinade until the last five minutes of cooking.
  4. Allow to rest for ten minutes loosely covered with foil while steaming your bokchoy or favourite Chinese green vegetables. Slice lamb and serve combined.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Chang’s Chopped Salad
author: pwg
metadescription: A fresh, crunchy salad for all occasions
ingredients:
You can choose from a wide range of ingredients for this dish. Just consider  creating a range of different colours and textures

Ingredients:

  • Iceberg
  • Avocado
  • Carrot
  • Celery
  • Corn
  • Cherry tomatoes
  • Cucumber
  • Green capsicum
  • Red capsicum
  • Yellow capsicum
  • Radishes
  • Hard boiled egg/quails egg
  • Green beans
  • Button mushrooms

Dressing:

  • {include file='cms_template:Changs-Crunchy-Fried-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Fried-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Gluten-Free-Fried-Noodles'} for a gluten-free option.
  • {include file='cms_template:Crispy-Noodle-Salad-Dressing'}
method:
Method

  1. Simply cut (chop) each ingredient into approximately the same size then place in a salad bowl and gently toss with Chang’s Asian Dressing and Chang’s Original Crunchy Noodles.
  2. For a gluten free alternative, simply remove the Chang’s Original Crunchy Noodles.

title: Black Pepper Crab
author: Pete
metadescription: Perfectly cooked Black Pepper Crab
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 1 large mud crab 1-1.5 kilos or 3 large blue swimmer crabs, cleaned and cut in 4 pieces each with shells cracked with the back of a cleaver
  • 4 tbsp. neutral oil
  • 50g butter
  • 4 cloves garlic, crushed or very finely chopped
  • 1 thumb ginger, grated
  • 1 medium green chilli, very finely sliced or diced-more to taste
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Soy-Sauce-Dark'}
  • 1 tsp. sugar
  • 1 heaped tbsp. freshly ground black pepper
  • 1 cup coriander leaves
  • I packet of {include file='cms_template:Changs-Long-Life-Noodles'}
method:
Method:

  1. Heat a wok that has a lid and add the oil, and then the crab. You might have to cook the crab in a couple of batches.
  2. Stir fry until the shells turn orange and don’t worry that the meat inside isn’t cooked.
  3. Remove the crab to a bowl and drain away all but one tablespoon of oil.
  4. Add the butter as when melted add the garlic, ginger and chilli. Cook until it becomes aromatic then add the oyster and soy sauces, and the sugar.
  5. Return the crab to the wok and stir to coat with the sauce then sprinkle over the pepper, stirring again to coat. Bring to the boil then reduce to a gentle simmer and place the lid on the wok, simmering for 5-10 minutes depending on the size of the crabs, until the meat is opaque.
  6. Cook the noodles as per the instructions on pack and drain then transfer to a platter, ladling the crab and all the sauce on top. For a gluten free alternative, service with rice or Chang’s Vermicelli Noodles.
  7. Scatter the coriander leaves on top of the crab and serve immediately with finger bowls for the mess.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free – variation

title: Pork and Asparagus Stir-Fry
author: Pete
metadescription: Quick and easy pork and asparagus stir fry with our Super Lo-Cal Noodles.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 2 tablespoons neutral oil
  • 300 grams pork fillet, thinly sliced
  • 2 spring onions, white and pale green parts only, sliced
  • 2 cloves garlic, very finely chopped or grated
  • ½ thumb ginger, cut into very fine matchsticks (julienne)
  • 1 bunch asparagus, woody ends discarded, cut into 2 cm lengths
  • 2 tablespoons {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tablespoon {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 packs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Wide-Noodles'}
method:
Method:

  1. Place drain noodles in a bowl and pour over boiling water to heat them through.
  2. While noodles are warming, place the oil in a hot wok and add the pork, garlic and ginger, stirring occasionally but trying to brown the pork against the sides of the wok.
  3. When pork is nearly cooked, add the spring onions and asparagus and stir fry for 30 seconds.
  4. Add the Chang’s Hoisin Sauce and Chang’s Light Soy Sauce and cook for one minute.
  5. Drain the noodles and place on a dish then spoon the pork and asparagus on top.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Hot and Sour Seafood with Super Lo-Cal Noodles
author: Pete
metadescription: A healthy, quick and easy warm noodle salad with prawns, squid and mussels
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 12 medium green king prawns, peeled and butterflied
  • 1 small squid tube, thinly sliced
  • 1 kg small fresh mussels
  • 2 spring onions, white and pale green parts only, sliced
  • 1 cup very finely shredded Chinese cabbage
  • 1 cup coriander leaves
  • 1 cup mint leaves
  • 2 medium red chillies, sliced more or less to your taste
  • 1 pack of Chang's Traditional Super Lo-Cal Noodles rinsed under cold water and drained

Dressing:

  • 2 tbsp. Chang's Fish Sauce
  • 2 tbsp. fresh lime juice
  • 1 tbsp. sugar
  • 1 tbsp. reserved mussel water
method:
Method:

  1. Using a large saucepan, heat salted water. Once boiling, turn heat down to a simmer.
  2. Add the squid and prawns into salted water, monitoring until just cooked. Remove seafood and reserve, leaving two tablespoons of the cooking water in the saucepan.
  3. Heat the water in the saucepan and when hot, add the mussels adding the mussels. After one minute, give the pan a shake and check when mussels begin to open.
  4. Drain mussels over a bowl and reserve the mussel cooking liquid. Then drain the reserved liquid through a fine sieve, discarding debris.
  5. Remove the meat from half the mussels and discard the shells, leaving the others intact.
  6. Mix all the dressing ingredients in a bowl or jar and stir until the sugar dissolves.
  7. Combine spring onions, Chinese cabbage, chillies and Super Lo Cal Noodles in a bowl, and stir through the dressing - allow to sit for five minutes.
  8. Then gently stir through the reserved seafood, along with the coriander and the mint, and serve immediately.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Hokkien Noodles with Pork and Snake Beans
author: Pete
metadescription: Simple pork and bean stir-fry with Hokkien noodles.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 2 tbsp. neutral oil
  • 200g pork mince
  • 2 cloves garlic, finely chopped or grated
  • 3 spring onions, white and pale green parts only, sliced
  • 1 bunch Snake beans, cut into 3-4 cm lengths or 12 – 15 green beans, halved
  • 400g {include file='cms_template:Changs-Shelf-Fresh-Noodles-Hokkien-Style'}
  • 2 tablespoons {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tablespoon {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
method:
Method:

  1. Heat the oil in a wok and add the garlic, stirring until fragrant.Add the spring onions and pork mince, ensuring to stir thoroughly to break up the mince and brown it.
  2. Finally, add the snake beans and cook for one minute, adding the sauces and the noodles and stirring to heat the noodles and coat all the ingredients.
  3. Transfer to a serving plate and serve immediately.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Kids Christmas Rocky Road
author: Pete
metadescription: Fun Rocky Road with a Chang’s crunch!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 500g milk chocolate
  • 1 cup marshmallows, cut into pieces
  • 2/3 cup leftover firm red jelly or raspberry jelly lollies, cut into pieces
  • 1 packet Chang’s Original Fried Noodles
method:
Method: 

  1. Begin by breaking the chocolate into pieces, placing into two rectangular plastic takeaway containers.
  2. Place the takeaway containers into the microwave, heating the chocolate for two minutes or until just melted.
  3. Stir through the marshmallows, jelly or lollies and Chang’s Fried Noodles and place in the fridge until the chocolate hardens.
  4. Once set, cut the Rocky Road into pieces about 2cm square.
allergen: Nut Free

title: Japanese Chicken and Mushroom Soup
author: Pete
metadescription: Super quick and easy to make Japanese chicken and mushroom soup. Perfect for any day!
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 4 cups chicken stock
  • 200g skinless chicken breast meat, shredded
  • 6 fresh shiitake mushrooms, sliced, or 4 dried shiitake mushrooms
  • 2 baby bok choy, leaves removed and reserved
  • 2 packs {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Wide-Noodles'}
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tbsp.mirin
  • 4 sprigs coriander leaves
method:
Method:

  1. Bring the chicken stock to the boil in a large saucepan.
  2. Add the Chang’s Light Soy Sauce, mirin, mushrooms and bok choy, reducing heat to a simmer and cooking for one minute.
  3. Rinse the Super Lo Cal Noodles in cold water, and distribute amongst soup bowls along with the shredded chicken breast.
  4. Ladle the soup into the bowls, ensuring vegetables are evenly distributed.
  5. Top soup with the coriander sprigs and serve immediately.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free, Gluten Free

title: Meatball and Super Lo-Cal Noodle Soup
author: Pete
metadescription: A warming beef and noodle soup.
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 400g lean beef or pork mince (finely ground)
  • 1 clove garlic, grated or very finely chopped
  • ½ thumb (approx. 1 heaped tsp.) grated ginger
  • 2 spring onions, white and pale green parts only, very finely sliced
  • 1 egg, lightly beaten
  • 1 cup Panko crumbs (Japanese breadcrumbs)
  • 1 tbsp. cornflour
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Hoisin-Sauce-280ml'}
  • ½ cup neutral oil

Soup:

  • 4 cups beef or chicken stock
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 tsp. sugar
  • ½ tsp. salt
  • 1 cup shredded Chinese cabbage
  • 1 bunch enoki mushrooms, bottom of stems removed
  • 6-8 oyster mushrooms, halved or left whole if small
  • 2 packets {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Noodles'} or {include file='cms_template:Changs-Traditional-Super-Lo-Cal-Wide-Noodles'}, rinsed in cold water
method:
Method:

  1. Combine all the meatball ingredients, except the oil, in either a food processor or by hand in a bowl.
  2. Allow meat to rest in the fridge for ten minutes. Remove from the fridge, and roll meat into walnut-sized balls with your hands.
  3. Heat the oil in a wok or frying pan and brown the meatballs in batches, turning occasionally until just cooked through. Once done, drain meatballs on a paper towel. At this point, meatballs can be refrigerated and used later.
  4. Heat the stock and add the Chang’s Light Soy Sauce, stock and sugar and bring to the boil. Add the Chinese cabbage, Chang’s Super Lo-Cal Noodles and reserved meatballs and simmer very gently for five minutes.
  5. Finally, add the mushrooms and simmer for just another minute.
  6. Ladle soup into individual bowls or serve from a soup tureen, sprinkling over a handful of sliced dark green spring onion tops.
allergen: Nut Free, Dairy Free

title: Marinated chicken thighs with a salsa salad
author: Pete
metadescription: Marinated chicken thighs served with a fresh salsa salad
ingredients:
Ingredients:

  • 8 evenly sized boned chicken thighs, trimmed off bone fragments and excess fat

Salsa salad:

  • 2 cloves garlic, crushed or grated
  • 1 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 2 tbsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Original-Oyster-Sauce-280ml'}
  • 1 medium red chilli, seeds discarded, very finely chopped
  • ¼ cup lemon juice
  • ½ tsp. freshly ground black pepper
  • 1 tsp. honey

Marinade:

  • 1 cup flat coriander leaves, very roughly chopped
  • 2 medium ripe tomatoes, seeds discarded, cut into a 1cm dice
  • 1 medium red capsicum, cut into a 1 cm diced
  • 2 spring onions, sliced
  • Kernels from 1 ear of corn that has been boiled and cooled
  • 1 Lebanese cucumber, seeds discarded and diced
  • ½ - 1 medium red chilli, seeds discarded, very finely diced
  • 2 tbsp. red wine vinegar
  • 1 tbsp. extra virgin olive oil
  • 1 tsp. {include file='cms_template:Changs-Tamari-Light-Soy-Sauce-280ml'}
method:
Method:

  1. Mix the marinade ingredients in a non-metallic bowl and add chicken, folding through the mixture to coat thoroughly. Cover and refrigerate for 2-4 hours.
  2. Bring back to room temperature before cooking on your char grill over medium-high heat, until the outside is scorched and the juices run clear not rosy.
  3. Mix the oil, vinegar and Chang’s Soy Sauce for the salsa salad and season with sea salt and black pepper.
  4. Fold through all the other ingredients and serve a big spoonful alongside a couple of chicken thighs that you have sprinkled with sea salt.
allergen: Gluten Free, Nut Free, Dairy Free
